nl: A smartphone allows users to make calls , send messages , and access the internet
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (smartphone(x) AND use(y) IMPLIES (allow_call(y, x) AND allow_send_message(y, x) AND allow_access_internet(y, x))))

nl: A flashlight emits light for illuminating dark areas , while a whistle produces sound for attracting attention
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (flashlight(x) AND whistle(y) IMPLIES (emit_light(x) AND produce_sound(y) AND attract_attention(y))))

nl: A solar panel absorbs sunlight , converts it into electricity through the photovoltaic effect , and stores the generated power in batteries for later use
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (panel_sun(x) AND sunlight(y) IMPLIES (absorb_sun(x, y) AND convert_electricity(x) AND store_power_battery(x))))

nl: An umbrella provides shelter from rain , while sunglasses protect the eyes from sunlight
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (umbrella(x) AND sunglasses(y) IMPLIES (shelter_rain(x) AND protect_eye(y) AND block_sunlight(y))))

nl: A bird species that migrates long distances often has a streamlined body shape and strong wing muscles to sustain flight
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (bird_species(x) AND migrate_long_distance(x) IMPLIES (streamline_body_shape(y) AND strong_wing_muscle(y) AND sustain_fly(x, y))))

nl: Mountains are taller than hills , but both can be climbed by hikers
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (mountain(x) AND hill(y) IMPLIES (tall_more(x, y) AND climb_hike(x) AND climb_hike(y))))

nl: The process of metamorphosis in animals involves a series of physical changes that lead to the transformation from one developmental stage to another , such as from a larva to an adult
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (metamorphosis(x) AND animal(y) IMPLIES (series_physical(x, y) AND transform(x) AND larva_adult(x, y))))

nl: A company that supports employee training and development and offers flexible work arrangements experiences increased productivity and job satisfaction among its workforce
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (company(x) AND support_employ_train(x) AND offer_flexible_work(x) IMPLIES (increase_productive(y) AND job_satisfy(y))))

nl: Mountains have peaks and valleys , while rivers flow through valleys
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (mountain(x) AND river(y) IMPLIES (have_peak(x) AND have_valley(x) AND flow_valley(y))))

nl: A river that flows through a city provides a source of water for its residents and contributes to the city 's economic activities
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((river(x) AND city(y) AND flow(x, y)) IMPLIES (provide_water_source(y, x) AND contribute_economy_activity(y, x))))

nl: Ice cream melts when exposed to heat , while water freezes when exposed to cold temperatures
fol: (FORALL x (ice_cream(x) AND expose_heat(x) IMPLIES melt(x)) AND FORALL y (water(y) AND expose_cold_temperature(y) IMPLIES freeze(y)))

nl: An athlete excels in a sport when they consistently win competitions and break records
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((athlete(x) AND sport(y) AND excel_in(x, y)) IFF (consistent_win(x, y) AND break_record(x, y))))

nl: A computer virus can spread through email attachments or unsecured websites , but not through properly encrypted messages
fol: (FORALL x (computer_virus(x) IMPLIES ((spread_email_attachment(x) OR spread_unsecure_website(x)) AND NOT spread_encrypt_message(x))))

nl: A job that is n't full - time might be part - time or freelance
fol: (FORALL x (job(x) AND NOT full_time(x) IMPLIES (part_time(x) OR freelance(x))))

nl: A bird can fly or walk , but not swim
fol: (FORALL x (bird(x) IMPLIES ((fly(x) OR walk(x)) AND NOT swim(x))))

nl: A vehicle needs fuel to function , and an electric vehicle needs electricity
fol: (FORALL x ((vehicle(x) AND NOT electric_vehicle(x)) IMPLIES need_fuel(x)) AND (electric_vehicle(x) IMPLIES need_electricity(x)))

nl: A musical instrument that has strings , is played with a bow , and can be held between the chin and shoulder is a violin if it has four strings
fol: (FORALL x (music_instrument(x) AND have_string(x) AND play_bow(x) AND hold_between_chin_shoulder(x) AND have_string(x) IMPLIES violin(x)))

nl: A weather event can be considered severe if it involves heavy rain , strong winds , or hail
fol: (FORALL x (weather_event(x) AND (heavy_rain(x) OR strong_winds(x) OR hail(x)) IMPLIES severe(x)))

nl: A book becomes a bestseller by having a compelling story , receiving positive reviews , or generating strong word of mouth
fol: (FORALL x (book(x) AND (compel_story(x) OR positive_review(x) OR strong_word_of_mouth(x)) IMPLIES sell(x)))

nl: A restaurant offers outdoor seating if it has a patio or a terrace that can accommodate customers
fol: (FORALL x (restaurant(x) AND (have_patio(x) OR have_terrace(x)) AND accommodate_customer(x) IMPLIES offer_outdoor_seat(x)))

nl: A person is considered an artist if they create visual , auditory , or performance works
fol: (FORALL x (person(x) AND (create_visual_work(x) OR create_auditory_work(x) OR create_performance_work(x)) IMPLIES artist(x)))

nl: Pets , such as cats and dogs , require regular care and attention
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((pet(x) AND (cat(y) OR dog(y))) IMPLIES require_regular_care(x, y)))

nl: An item is fragile if it is made of glass or porcelain and can be easily broken
fol: (FORALL x (item(x) AND (make_glass(x) OR make_porcelain(x)) AND easy_break(x) IMPLIES fragile(x)))

nl: A person who writes books , stories , or articles is an author
fol: (FORALL x ((person(x) AND ((write_book(x) OR write_story(x)) OR write_article(x))) IMPLIES author(x)))

nl: A fruit is considered ripe if it is soft , sweet , or easily separated from the tree
fol: (FORALL x (fruit(x) AND (soft(x) OR sweet(x) OR easy_separate_tree(x)) IMPLIES ripe(x)))

nl: A national park protects ecosystems and wildlife , and offers recreational activities for visitors
fol: (FORALL x (nation_park(x) IMPLIES (protect(x) AND (ecosystem(x) OR wildlife(x)) AND offer_recreation(x))))

nl: An animal thrives in its habitat when it has access to ample food sources , faces minimal predation , or finds suitable shelter
fol: (FORALL x (animal(x) AND (ample_food_source(x) OR minimal_predate(x) OR suitable_shelter(x)) IMPLIES thrive_habitat(x)))

nl: A beverage is refreshing when it 's cold , not overly sweet , and quenches thirst
fol: (FORALL x ((beverage(x) AND cold(x) AND NOT over_sweet(x) AND quench_thirst(x)) IMPLIES refresh(x)))

nl: A dish is a dessert if it is sweet , served after a meal , and not part of the main course
fol: (FORALL x (dish(x) AND is_sweet(x) AND serve_after_meal(x) AND NOT part_of_main_course(x) IMPLIES dessert(x)))

nl: A laptop is a portable computer , while a desktop is not portable
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (laptop(x) AND desktop(y) IMPLIES (portable(x) AND NOT portable(y))))

nl: A flower blooms when it receives sufficient sunlight and adequate water but not in extremely cold temperatures
fol: (FORALL x (flower(x) AND receive_sunlight(x) AND adequate_water(x) AND NOT extreme_cold(x) IMPLIES bloom(x)))

nl: A book can be borrowed from the library if it is not reserved , damaged , or already checked out
fol: (FORALL x (book(x) AND borrow_library(x) IMPLIES (not reserve(x) AND NOT damage(x) AND NOT check_borrow(x))))

nl: A polygon with six sides is called a hexagon , while a polygon with eight sides is called an octagon
fol: (FORALL x ((polygon(x) AND have_side(x) AND call(x, side, 6)) IMPLIES hexagon(x)) AND ((polygon(x) AND have_side(x) AND call(x, side, 8)) IMPLIES octagon(x)))

nl: An item is considered fragile if it is made of glass , ceramic , or thin plastic
fol: (FORALL x (item(x) AND (made_glass(x) XOR made_ceramic(x) XOR made_thin_plastic(x)) IMPLIES fragile(x)))

nl: A refrigerator preserves food by maintaining low temperatures and has shelves , but it is not a freezer
fol: (FORALL x (refrigerator(x) IFF (preserve_food(x) AND maintain_low_temperature(x) AND have_shelf(x) AND NOT freezer(x))))

nl: For a device to be considered portable , it should have a compact size , be lightweight , and have a battery or alternative power source
fol: (FORALL x (device(x) AND portable(x) IFF (compact_size(x) AND lightweight(x) AND (battery_power(x) OR alternative_power(x)))))

nl: A city x implements a new public transportation system y when the population density surpasses a predetermined threshold and sufficient funding is available
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (city(x) AND public_transport_system(y) AND population_density_surpass_threshold(x) AND suffice_fund(x, y) IMPLIES implement(x, y)))

nl: A volcano is a geological formation that releases molten rock , ash , and gases from Earth 's interior
fol: (FORALL x (volcano(x) IMPLIES (geology_formation(x) AND release_melt_rock(x) AND release_ash(x) AND release_gas(x) AND from_earths_interior(x))))

nl: A solar panel system installed on a rooftop with optimal sun exposure generates more electricity than a system installed in a shaded area
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (solar_panel_system(x) AND install_rooftop_sun_optimal(x) AND solar_panel_system(y) AND install_area_shade(y) IMPLIES generate_more_electricity(x, y)))

nl: Farmers grow crops on fields
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (person(x) AND crop(y) AND field(z) IMPLIES grow_on(x, y, z)))

nl: A river flows from its source to its mouth , often into a larger body of water
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((river(x) AND source(x) AND mouth(x) AND flow(x, y)) IMPLIES often_water(y)))

nl: When a teacher effectively communicates with students and gives constructive feedback , it can lead to better learning outcomes
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((teach(x) AND study(y) AND effective_communicate(x, y) AND constructive_feedback(x, y)) IMPLIES better_learn_outcome(y)))

nl: A pilot flies an airplane to a destination
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((pilot(x) AND airplane(y) AND destination(z)) IMPLIES fly(x, y, z)))

nl: A bakery sells fresh bread and pastries by baking them daily and using high - quality ingredients
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (bakery(x) AND bread_pastry(y) AND bake_daily(x) AND use_high_quality_ingredient(x, y) IMPLIES sell_fresh(x, y)))

nl: Astronauts explore space in spacecraft
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (astronaut(x) AND space(y) AND spacecraft(z) IMPLIES explore_space(x, y, z)))

nl: A scientist performs experiments to test hypotheses
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (scientist(x) AND experiment(y) AND hypothesize(z) IMPLIES perform_test(x, y, z)))

nl: A truck driver transports goods between locations using specific routes and trucks
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (truck_drive(x) AND good(y) AND route(z) IMPLIES transport(x, y, z)))

nl: An infectious agent that can only reproduce within a host cell , is composed of genetic material surrounded by a protein coat , and can cause a wide range of diseases is a virus
fol: (FORALL x (infect_agent(x) AND require_host_cell(x) AND compose_gene_material(x) AND surround_protein_coat(x) AND cause_disease(x) IMPLIES virus(x)))

nl: Builders construct buildings using tools
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (build_person(x) AND building(y) AND tool(z) IMPLIES construct_use(x, y, z)))

nl: A subscription service grants access to premium content if the user is active and has paid the monthly fee
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (subscribe_service(x) AND use(y) AND activity(y) AND pay_month_fee(y, x) IMPLIES access_premium_content(y, x)))

nl: A software x is compatible with an operating system y if x can function properly and without errors on y
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (software(x) AND system(y) AND function_proper(x, y) AND no_err(x, y) IMPLIES compatible(x, y)))

nl: A smartphone supports wireless charging if it is compatible with a wireless charging pad and the charging pad is powered on
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (smartphone(x) AND wireless_charging_pad(y) AND compatible(x, y) AND power_on(y) IMPLIES support_wireless_charge(x, y)))

nl: A substance that can conduct electricity , is malleable , and has a shiny appearance is a metal if it is solid at room temperature
fol: (FORALL x (substance(x) AND can_conduct_electricity(x) AND malleable(x) AND shiny_appear(x) AND solid_room_temperature(x) IMPLIES metal(x)))

nl: A room with more windows has better natural light than one with fewer windows
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (room(x) AND room(y) AND more_window(x) AND few_window(y) IMPLIES good_natural_light(x, y)))

nl: Carnivorous plants obtain nutrients by trapping and digesting insects , which helps them thrive in nutrient - poor environments
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((carnivorous_plant(x) AND insect(y) AND nutrient_poor_environment(z)) IMPLIES trap_and_digest(x, y, z)))

nl: A deciduous forest is characterized by trees that shed their leaves seasonally , typically having a temperate climate , and hosting a diverse range of plant and animal species
fol: (FORALL x (deciduous_forest(x) IMPLIES (characterize_tree(x) AND shed_leaf_season(x) AND temperate_climate(x) AND host_diverse_plant_species(x) AND host_diverse_animal_species(x))))

nl: A celestial body is a comet if it is composed of ice , dust , and rock and follows an elliptical orbit around a star
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (body(x) AND compose_ice_dust_rock(x) AND follow_elliptical_orbit(x, y) AND star(y) IMPLIES comet(x)))

nl: A computer is a laptop if it is portable , has an integrated keyboard and display , and runs on battery power
fol: (FORALL x (computer(x) AND portable(x) AND integrate_keyboard(x) AND integrate_display(x) AND run_battery(x) IMPLIES laptop(x)))

nl: A person who uses mathematical and scientific methods to study and analyze the properties and behavior of matter is a physicist
fol: (FORALL x (person(x) AND use_mathematics(x) AND use_science(x) AND study_property_matter(x) AND study_behave_matter(x) IMPLIES physicist(x)))

nl: A person is considered a psychologist if they study human behavior and mental processes to help individuals understand and improve their mental health
fol: (FORALL x (person(x) AND study_human_behave(x) AND study_mind_process(x) AND help_individual_understand_health(x) AND help_individual_improve_health(x) IMPLIES psychologist(x)))

nl: A radio receives signals and converts them into sound
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (radio(x) AND signal(y) AND sound(z) IMPLIES receive_and_convert(x, y, z)))

nl: An employee receives a bonus if they have high performance and their department has a budget surplus
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (employ(x) AND department(y) AND high_perform(x) AND budget_surplus(y) IMPLIES receive_bonus(x)))

nl: A dancer performs movements to express emotions and ideas
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((person_dance(x) AND move(y) AND emotion_idea(z)) IMPLIES perform_express(x, y, z)))

nl: A teacher imparts knowledge and skills to students in a classroom setting
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (teach(x) AND study(y) AND classroom(z) IMPLIES impart_knowledge_skill(x, y, z)))

nl: A plant x is native to a region y if it originated and evolved naturally in the area that constitutes y
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (plant(x) AND region(y) AND originate_naturally(x, y) AND evolve_area(x, y) IMPLIES native_to(x, y)))

nl: In a forest ecosystem , a plant that absorbs sunlight , converts it into energy through photosynthesis , and provides a habitat for various species is called a primary producer
fol: (FORALL x (forest_ecosystem(w) AND plant(x) AND absorb_sunlight(x) AND photosynthesis(x) AND provide_habitat(x) IMPLIES primary_produce(x)))

nl: A soccer ball is round and used for playing soccer , while a basketball is round and used for playing basketball
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((soccer_ball(x) AND basketball(y)) IMPLIES ((round(x) AND use_play_soccer(x)) AND (round(y) AND use_play_basketball(y)))))

nl: A chef prepares and cooks meals , while a server takes orders and delivers food to customers
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((chef(x) AND server(y)) IMPLIES ((prepare_meal(x) AND cook_meal(x)) AND (take_order(y) AND deliver_food(y)))))

nl: A doctor treats patients , while a researcher studies diseases
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((doctor(x) AND patient(y)) IMPLIES treat(x, y)) AND ((researcher(x) AND disease(y)) IMPLIES study(x, y)))

nl: Hamsters are nocturnal , butterflies are diurnal , and fireflies are crepuscular
fol: (FORALL x (hamster(x) IMPLIES nocturnal(x)) AND FORALL y (butterfly(y) IMPLIES diurnal(y)) AND FORALL z (firefly(z) IMPLIES crepuscular(z)))

nl: A triangle with angles a , b , and c is right - angled if exactly one of the angles is 90 degrees
fol: (FORALL a FORALL b FORALL c (triangle_angle(a, b, c) IMPLIES ((angle_right(a) XOR angle_right(b)) XOR angle_right(c))))

nl: A predator successfully hunts its prey if it can detect the prey , approach it stealthily , and capture it quickly
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (predator(x) AND prey(y) AND succeed_hunt(x, y) IFF (detect(x, y) AND approach_stealth(x, y) AND capture_quick(x, y))))

nl: Not all plants produce flowers , but those that do are called flowering plants
fol: (EXISTS x (plant(x) AND NOT produce_flower(x)) AND FORALL y (plant(y) AND produce_flower(y) IMPLIES flower_plant(y)))

nl: An activity that is performed outdoors and not a sport is either hiking or birdwatching
fol: (FORALL x (activity(x) AND outdoors(x) AND NOT sport(x) IMPLIES hike(x) XOR bird_watch(x)))

nl: An investment is profitable if it has a high return on investment or low risk , but not if it has hidden fees
fol: (FORALL x (invest(x) AND profitable(x) IFF ((high_risk(x) OR low_risk(x)) AND NOT hide_fee(x))))

nl: Mars has a thin atmosphere and lacks liquid water , while Earth has a dense atmosphere and abundant liquid water
fol: (FORALL x ((planet_mars(x) IMPLIES (thin_atmosphere(x) AND NOT liquid_water(x))) AND (planet_earth(x) IMPLIES (dense_atmosphere(x) AND abundant_liquid_water(x)))))

nl: A movie can be enjoyed by both children and adults if it contains no explicit content and has a family - friendly theme
fol: (FORALL x (movie(x) AND NOT contain_explicit_content(x) AND family_friendly_theme(x) IMPLIES enjoy(x) AND adult(x) AND child(x)))

nl: Lakes and rivers contain freshwater , while oceans and seas contain saltwater
fol: (FORALL x ((lake(x) OR river(x)) IMPLIES freshwater(x)) AND FORALL y ((ocean(y) OR sea(y)) IMPLIES saltwater(y)))

nl: A beverage is a soft drink if it is non - alcoholic , carbonated , and flavored with sweeteners or fruit juices
fol: (FORALL x (soft_drink(x) IFF (beverage(x) AND non_alcoholic(x) AND carbonate(x) AND (sweetener_flavor(x) OR fruit_juice_flavor(x)))))

nl: A square has four equal sides , while a rectangle has four sides with opposite sides equal
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (square(x) AND rectangle(y) IMPLIES (have_equal_side(x, 4) XOR (have_side(y, 4) AND opposite_side_equal(y)))))

nl: A product is successful in the market when it has a competitive price , high quality , and a strong brand image , but not if it lacks essential features
fol: (FORALL x (product(x) AND market_succeed(x) IFF (compete_price(x) AND high_quality(x) AND strong_brand_image(x) AND NOT lack_essential_feature(x))))

nl: A laptop is suitable for gaming if it has a powerful processor , an advanced graphics card , and sufficient RAM , but not if it overheats quickly
fol: (FORALL x (laptop(x) AND suitable_game(x) IFF (powerful_processor(x) AND advanced_graphics_card(x) AND suffice(x) AND NOT overheat_quick(x))))

nl: A scientist conducts experiments using laboratory equipment and collects data for analysis
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (scientist(x) AND experiment(y) AND laboratory_equipment(z) IMPLIES (conduct_use(x, y, z) AND collect_data(x, y))))

nl: In an orchestra , musicians play various instruments , such as violins , cellos , and flutes , harmoniously combining their sounds to produce a rich and captivating musical performance
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (orchestra(x) AND instrument(y) IMPLIES (musician_play(x, y) AND violin_cello_flute(y) AND harmonious_combine_sound(x) AND rich_captivate_performance(x))))

nl: Washing machines clean clothes by agitating them in water and detergent
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((wash_machine(x) AND clothes(y) AND (water(z) AND detergent(z))) IMPLIES clean_by_agitate_in(x, y, z)))

nl: A wind turbine generates electricity by capturing the kinetic energy of the wind , which spins the rotor blades and turns a generator , converting the mechanical energy into electrical power
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((turbine_wind(x) AND wind(y)) IMPLIES (generate_electricity(x) AND capture_kinetic_energy(x, y) AND spin_rotor(x) AND convert_energy(x))))

nl: In a democracy , citizens can vote to elect leaders and influence policies
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (democracy(x) AND citizen(y, x) AND lead(z) IMPLIES (can_vote_elect(y, z) AND can_influence_policy(y))))

nl: A biome is a large ecosystem characterized by specific types of vegetation , animal life , and climate conditions , such as tundra , desert , or rainforest
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((biome(x) AND ecosystem(y)) IMPLIES (characterize_specific_type(x, y) AND vegetation(x) AND animal_life(x) AND climate_condition(x))))

nl: Coral reefs , formed by the calcium carbonate skeletons of tiny marine organisms called corals , provide a habitat for numerous species of fish and invertebrates , creating a diverse and complex ecosystem
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (coral_reef(x) AND marine_organism(y) IMPLIES (form_by_calcium_carbonate_skeleton(x, y) AND coral(y) AND habitat_for_fish_invertebrate(x) AND diverse_complex_ecosystem(x))))

nl: A library contains books and journals
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((library(x) AND book(y) AND journal(z)) IMPLIES (contain(x, y) AND contain(x, z))))

nl: A scientist researches hypotheses and experiments to make discoveries
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (scientist(x) AND hypothesize(y) AND experiment(z) IMPLIES research(x, y, z) AND discover(x)))

nl: A book has pages and contains text , while a painting has a canvas and contains visual elements
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (book(x) AND painting(y) IMPLIES (have_page(x) AND contain_text(x) AND have_canvas(y) AND contain_visual_element(y))))

nl: A pencil writes on paper and an eraser removes marks
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (pencil(x) AND paper(y) AND eraser(z) IMPLIES (write_on(x, y) AND remove_mark(z))))

nl: In a restaurant , a chef prepares meals for customers , who can choose between appetizers , main courses , and desserts
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (restaurant(x) AND chef(y) AND customer(z) IMPLIES (prepare_meal(y, z) AND choose_between(z, appetizer, main_course, dessert))))

nl: Microbes break down organic matter. Fungi also decompose organic material. This decomposition by fungi plays a vital role in nutrient cycling. In fact , all fungi decompose organic matter , recycling nutrients back into the ecosystem
fol: (FORALL x (microbe(x) IMPLIES break_down_organic_matter(x))) AND ( FORALL x (fungus(x) IMPLIES decompose_organic_material(x))) AND ( FORALL x (fungus(x) IMPLIES play_vital_role_in_nutrient_cycle(x))) AND ( FORALL x (fungus(x) IMPLIES decompose_organic_matter(x))) AND ( FORALL x (fungus(x) IMPLIES decompose_organic_matter(x)))

nl: Mammals are warm - blooded. Reptiles are cold - blooded. If an animal is cold - blooded , its body temperature changes with its environment. Unlike mammals , warm - blooded animals maintain a constant body temperature. Therefore , since reptiles are cold - blooded , their body temperature changes with the temperature of their environment
fol: (FORALL x (mammal(x) IMPLIES warm_blood(x))) AND ( FORALL x (reptile(x) IMPLIES cold_blood(x))) AND ( FORALL x (cold_blood(x) IMPLIES body_temperature_changes_with_environment(x))) AND ( FORALL x (warm_blood(x) IMPLIES constant_body_temperature(x))) AND ( FORALL x (reptile(x) IMPLIES body_temperature_changes_with_environment(x)))

nl: A train ticket is either one - way or round - trip , but not both at the same time
fol: (FORALL x (train_ticket(x) IMPLIES (one_way(x) XOR round_trip(x)) AND NOT (one_way(x) AND round_trip(x))))

nl: Reptiles are defined by several key characteristics. They possess scaly skin. They are also cold - blooded. Furthermore , reptiles typically reproduce by laying eggs. Therefore , an animal is classified as a reptile if it has scaly skin , is cold - blooded , and lays eggs. Breathing with lungs and laying amniotic eggs further identifies an animal as a reptile
fol: (FORALL x (animal(x) AND have_scaly_skin(x) AND cold_blood(x) AND lay_egg(x) IMPLIES reptile(x))) AND ( FORALL x (animal(x) AND breathe_lung(x) AND lay_amniotic_egg(x) IMPLIES reptile(x)))

nl: A student passes an exam if they study diligently , understand the material , and complete the exam , unless they are caught cheating
fol: (FORALL x (person(x) AND ((study_diligent(x) AND understand_material(x) AND complete_exam(x)) AND NOT catch_cheat(x) IMPLIES pass_exam(x))))

nl: A government grant is awarded to a research project if it has scientific merit , adequate funding , and a well - structured proposal , but not if it involves unethical practices
fol: (FORALL x (research_project(x) AND ((scientific_merit(x) AND adequate_fund(x) AND well_structure(x)) AND NOT ethics_practice(x)) IMPLIES award_government_grant(x)))

nl: A restaurant serves different types of cuisine , like Italian , Chinese , or Indian , and can offer dine - in , takeout , or delivery options to customers
fol: (FORALL x (restaurant(x) IMPLIES ((cuisine_italian(x) OR cuisine_chinese(x) OR cuisine_indian(x)) AND (dine_in(x) OR takeout(x) OR deliver(x)))))

nl: A mobile application is popular if it is downloaded by a large number of users and receives positive reviews
fol: (FORALL x (mobile_application(x) AND large_number_download(x) AND EXISTS y (user(y) AND download(x, y)) AND positive_review(x) IMPLIES popular(x)))

nl: Lions and tigers are carnivores , whereas elephants and giraffes are herbivores
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((lion(x) OR tiger(x)) IMPLIES carnivore(x)) AND ((elephant(y) OR giraffe(y)) IMPLIES herbivore(y)))

nl: A musical instrument that produces sound by vibrating strings and is played by plucking , strumming , or striking the strings is a string instrument
fol: (FORALL x ((music_instrument(x) AND produce_sound(x) AND string_vibrate(x) AND (play_pluck(x) OR play_strum(x) OR play_strike(x))) IMPLIES string_instrument(x)))

nl: A museum exhibits various artifacts , artwork , or historical items , and it may host educational programs , guided tours , or special events
fol: (FORALL x (museum(x) IMPLIES ((exhibit_artifact(x) OR exhibit_artwork(x) OR exhibit_history_item(x)) AND (host_educate_program(x) OR host_guide_tour(x) OR host_special_event(x)))))

nl: A garden has flowers and plants that require sunlight and water to grow
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (garden(x) AND (flower(y) OR plant(y)) IMPLIES (have(x, y) AND require(y, sunlight) AND require(y, water))))

nl: Mathematicians solve problems and create theories , while poets compose verses and express emotions
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((mathematics(x) AND poet(y)) IMPLIES ((solve_problem(x) AND create_theory(x)) XOR (compose_verse(y) AND express_emotion(y)))))

nl: A smartphone can have a large or small screen , run on Android or iOS , and be equipped with a high - resolution or standard - resolution camera
fol: (FORALL x (smartphone(x) IMPLIES ((large_screen(x) XOR small_screen(x)) AND (run_android(x) XOR run_ios(x)) AND (high_resolution_camera(x) XOR standard_resolution_camera(x)))))

nl: A chemical reaction is endothermic if it absorbs heat , while it is exothermic if it releases heat
fol: (FORALL x (chemical_react(x) AND ((absorb_heat(x) AND NOT release_heat(x)) IMPLIES endothermic(x)) AND ((release_heat(x) AND NOT absorb_heat(x)) IMPLIES exothermic(x))))

nl: An apple is green if it is unripe , but it turns red when it is ripe , and some apples remain yellow when ripe
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((apple(x) AND ripe(y) IMPLIES green(x)) AND (apple(x) AND ripe(y) IMPLIES (red(x) XOR yellow(x)))))

nl: A vehicle can be considered a hybrid. This is if it utilizes both gasoline and electricity as power sources. A car , too , can be considered a hybrid. This is under the same conditions as any other vehicle. Therefore , a car using both gasoline and electricity is a hybrid
fol: (FORALL x (vehicle(x) AND uses_gasoline(x) AND uses_electricity(x) IMPLIES hybrid(x))) AND ( FORALL x (car(x) AND uses_gasoline(x) AND uses_electricity(x) IMPLIES hybrid(x))) AND ( FORALL x (car(x) AND uses_gasoline(x) AND uses_electricity(x) IMPLIES hybrid(x)))

nl: A productive team collaborates effectively , distributes work evenly among members , and establishes clear goals and expectations
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((team(x) AND team_member(y, x) AND collaborate_effective(x) AND distribute_work_even(x, y) AND establish_clear_goal(x)) IMPLIES productive(x)))

nl: A piece of clothing is considered a dress if it is a one - piece garment for women or girls , typically with a skirt and covering the upper body
fol: (FORALL x (clothe(x) AND piece(x) AND garment(x) AND woman_or_girl(x) AND skirt(x) AND cover_upper_body(x) IMPLIES dress(x)))

nl: A student excels in an academic subject when they possess a strong understanding of the material , demonstrate effective study habits , and consistently perform well on exams
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (person_study(x) AND subject(y) AND strong_understand(x, y) AND effective_study_habit(x, y) AND consistent_perform_exam(x, y) IMPLIES excel(x, y)))

nl: Mountains with snow on their peaks can influence the local climate and create different weather patterns
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (mountain(x) AND snow_on_peak(x) AND local_climate(y) AND weather_pattern(z) IMPLIES influence(x, y, z)))

nl: A computer x is vulnerable to a cyber attack y if it lacks security measures z that protect against the attack
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (computer(x) AND cyber_attack(y) AND security_measure(z) AND lack_protect(x, z, y) IMPLIES vulnerable_attack(x, y)))

nl: A student x will receive a scholarship from university y if they have an excellent academic record , participate in extracurricular activities , and submit a compelling application
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (person_study_university(x) AND university(y) AND excellent_academic_record(x) AND extracurricular_activity(x) AND compel_application(x) IMPLIES receive_scholarship(x, y)))

nl: In a computer network , servers store and manage data , while routers facilitate the transmission of data between devices
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (computer_network(x) AND server(y) AND router(z) AND store_manage_data(y, x) IMPLIES facilitate_transmit(z, x)))

nl: A bakery sells products like bread , pastries , and cakes , which are made from ingredients such as flour , sugar , and butter
fol: (FORALL x (bakery(x) IMPLIES (sell_bread(x) AND sell_pastry(x) AND sell_cake(x) AND make_flour(x) AND make_sugar(x) AND make_butter(x))))

nl: An ecosystem supports a species if it provides the necessary resources , habitat , and conditions for the species to thrive
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (ecosystem(x) AND species(y) AND resource_habitat_condition(z) AND provide_resource_habitat_condition(x, y, z) IMPLIES support_species(x, y, z)))

nl: A substance x reacts with substance y to produce compound z if x and y chemically combine to form z
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (substance(x) AND substance(y) AND compound(z) AND chemically_combines(x, y, z) IMPLIES react_to_produce(x, y, z)))

nl: A game is won by a team if they score more points than the opposing team
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (game(x) AND team(y) AND team(z) AND score_more(y, z, x) IMPLIES win_game(y, x)))

nl: Airplanes require a runway for takeoff and landing
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (airplane(x) IMPLIES (runway(y) AND take(x, y) AND runway(z) AND land(x, z))))

nl: A building that contains living spaces for multiple families , shares common areas , and is owned by a single entity is a residential apartment building
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (building(x) AND contain_live_space_multiple_family(x) AND share_area(x) AND single_entity(y) AND own_by(x, y) IMPLIES residence_apartment_building(x)))

nl: In an orchestra , musicians playing various instruments collaborate to create harmonious music
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (orchestra(x) AND musician(y) AND instrument(z) AND play(y, z) IMPLIES collaborate_create_harmonious_music(x, y, z)))

nl: A key opens a lock if it matches the lock 's pattern and is inserted and turned correctly
fol: (FORALL k FORALL l (key(k) AND lock(l) AND match_pattern(k, l) AND insert(k, l) AND turn_correct(k, l) IMPLIES open(k, l)))

nl: A lighthouse uses light to guide ships , a foghorn uses sound to guide ships , and a buoy uses markers to guide ships
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((lighthouse(x) AND foghorn(y) AND buoy(z)) IMPLIES (use_light(x) AND use_sound(y) AND use_marker(z))))

nl: Mercury is closer to the sun than Earth , Venus has a thick atmosphere , and Mars has the tallest volcano in the solar system
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((planet_name_mercury(x) AND planet_name_venus(y) AND planet_name_mars(z)) IMPLIES (close_to_sun(x) AND thick_atmosphere(y) AND tall_volcano(z))))

nl: Frogs are amphibians , eagles are birds , and dolphins are mammals
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (frog(x) IMPLIES amphibian(x)) AND (eagle(y) IMPLIES bird(y)) AND (dolphin(z) IMPLIES mammal(z)))

nl: A hammer is used for pounding , a screwdriver is used for turning screws , and a wrench is used for tightening nuts and bolts
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((hammer(x) AND screwdriver(y) AND wrench(z)) IMPLIES (use_hammer_pound(x) AND use_screwdriver_screw_turn(y) AND use_wrench_tighten_nut(z))))

nl: A biologist studies living organisms , a chemist studies chemicals , and a physicist studies the properties of matter and energy
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (biologist(x) AND chemist(y) AND physicist(z) IMPLIES (study_live_organism(x) AND study_chemical(y) AND study_property_matter_energy(z))))

nl: Journalists investigate and report news stories to inform the public about current events
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (journalist(x) AND news_story(y) AND current_event(z) IMPLIES (investigate(x, y) AND report(x, y) AND inform_public(x, z))))

nl: An island is surrounded by water , a mountain is taller than its surroundings , and a desert has little precipitation
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (island(x) AND mountain(y) AND desert(z) IMPLIES (surround_water(x) AND tall(y) AND little_precipitation(z))))

nl: Trees absorb carbon dioxide and release oxygen through photosynthesis
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (tree(x) AND oxygen(y) AND oxygen(z) IMPLIES (absorb(x, y) AND release(x, z) AND photosynthesis(x))))

nl: Comets are composed of ice , asteroids are composed of rock , and planets are composed of various elements
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (comet(x) IMPLIES compose_ice(x)) AND (asteroid(y) IMPLIES compose_rock(y)) AND (planet(z) IMPLIES compose_various_element(z)))

nl: A farmer grows crops , a rancher raises livestock , and a fisherman catches fish
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (farm_person(x) AND rancher(y) AND fish_person(z) IMPLIES (grow_crop(x) AND raise_livestock(y) AND catch_fish(z))))

nl: A butterfly has wings , and a bee has wings , but a worm does not have wings
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((butterfly(x) AND have_wing(x)) AND (bee(y) AND have_wing(y)) AND (worm(z) AND NOT have_wing(z))))

nl: A person is a dentist if they are trained and licensed to diagnose and treat oral diseases and maintain dental health
fol: (FORALL x (dentist(x) IFF (person(x) AND train(x) AND license(x) AND diagnose_disease(x) AND treat_disease(x) AND maintain_healthy(x))))

nl: A vehicle 's fuel efficiency can be measured in either miles per gallon or kilometers per liter. A car is considered fuel - efficient when its fuel consumption is low. Specifically , a car is fuel - efficient if it consumes less than a specified number of liters of fuel per 100 kilometers
fol: (FORALL x (vehicle(x) IMPLIES (fuel_efficiency_measure_mile(x) OR fuel_efficiency_measure_kilometer(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (car(x) IMPLIES (fuel_consume_low(x) IMPLIES fuel_efficient(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (car(x) IMPLIES (fuel_consume_less_specify(x) IMPLIES fuel_efficient(x))))

nl: Cats are recognized as carnivorous. They are also classified as mammals. This carnivorous nature means they primarily hunt and consume small prey. Such prey commonly includes birds and rodents
fol: (FORALL x (cat(x) IMPLIES carnivorous(x))) AND ( FORALL x (cat(x) IMPLIES mammal(x))) AND ( FORALL x (carnivorous(x) IMPLIES hunt_and_consume_small_prey(x))) AND ( FORALL x (small_prey(x) IMPLIES (bird(x) OR rodent(x))))

nl: An object is a sculpture if it is a three - dimensional work of art , is made from materials such as stone or metal , and is created by carving , modeling , or assembling
fol: (FORALL x (object(x) AND three_dimension_work_art(x) AND (make_stone(x) OR make_metal(x)) AND (create_carve(x) OR create_model(x) OR create_assemble(x)) IMPLIES sculpture(x)))

nl: A library stores books , periodicals , and other media for public use , and may offer additional services such as computer access , workshops , or study spaces
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (library(x) AND media(y) IMPLIES store_use_public(x, y) AND (offer_computer_access(x) OR offer_workshop(x) OR offer_study_space(x))))

nl: Countries with a high gross domestic product ( GDP ) typically invest more in education , healthcare , and infrastructure compared to countries with lower GDP
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (country(x) AND high_gdp(x) AND (educate(y) OR healthcare(y) OR infrastructure(y)) IMPLIES invest_more(x, y)))

nl: A successful entrepreneur has founded at least one profitable company and has a strong network of business contacts
fol: (FORALL x (entrepreneur(x) AND EXISTS y (company(y) AND found(x, y) AND profitable(y)) AND strong_network_of_contact(x) IMPLIES succeed(x)))

nl: An artist creates sculptures or paintings
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((artist(x) AND (sculpture(y) OR paint(z))) IMPLIES create(x, y) OR create(x, z)))

nl: A beverage can be hot or cold , but it is never both hot and cold simultaneously
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (beverage(x) AND hot(y) AND cold(z) IMPLIES NOT (hastemperature(x, y) AND hastemperature(x, z))))

nl: A square has four equal sides and four right angles , while a rectangle has four right angles but not all equal sides
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((square(x) AND rectangle(y)) IMPLIES ((four_equal_side(x) AND four_right_angle(x) AND four_right_angle(y)) AND NOT four_equal_side(y))))

nl: A person is considered a vegetarian if they do n't consume meat , poultry , or fish , but they do consume plant - based foods
fol: (FORALL x (person(x) AND NOT consume_meat(x) AND NOT consume_poultry(x) AND NOT consume_fish(x) AND consume_plant_food(x) IMPLIES vegetarian(x)))

nl: A secure password must contain uppercase letters , lowercase letters , and digits , but not include easily guessable patterns or common words
fol: (FORALL x (password(x) AND secure(x) IMPLIES (uppercase_letter(x) AND low_letter(x) AND digit(x) AND NOT guess_pattern(x) AND NOT common_word(x))))

nl: A celestial body is considered a star if it emits light due to nuclear fusion reactions in its core. This establishes a foundational definition for stellar objects. Now , consider a large celestial body that generates energy through nuclear fusion within its core. This process naturally results in the emission of both light and heat. Therefore , if a large celestial body generates energy through nuclear fusion in its core and emits light and heat , it is indeed a star. This comprehensive characteristic set implies the core conditions , allowing for this conclusion through Hypothetical Syllogism
fol: (FORALL x (celestial_body(x) AND fusion_in_core(x) AND emit_light(x) IMPLIES star(x))) AND ( FORALL x (large_celestial_body(x) AND generate_energy_through_fusion(x) IMPLIES (emit_light(x) AND emit_heat(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (large_celestial_body(x) AND generate_energy_through_fusion(x) AND emit_light(x) AND emit_heat(x) IMPLIES star(x)))

nl: A microphone amplifies sound and converts it to an electrical signal. Let 's define a microphone as an object that converts sound into electrical signals for amplification or recording. Therefore , if a device converts sound waves into electrical signals , it is a microphone. By the definition law , we can replace the initial definition of a microphone. Thus , a device that converts sound waves into electrical signals is , by definition , a microphone. This means such a device both amplifies sound and converts it to an electrical signal
fol: (FORALL x (microphone(x) IMPLIES (amplifies_sound(x) AND converts_to_electricity_signal(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((converts_sound(x) AND produces_electricity_signal(x)) IMPLIES microphone(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((device(x) AND converts(x, sound_wave, electricity_signal)) IMPLIES microphone(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((device(x) AND converts(x, sound_wave, electricity_signal)) IMPLIES (amplifies_sound(x) AND converts_to_electricity_signal(x))))

nl: A conductor facilitates the transmission of electricity. This is because a conductor is defined as a material that allows the easy flow of electric charge. Furthermore , a material is a conductor if it allows the flow of electric current with low resistance. Thus , a substance that allows electric charge to flow and has low resistance is , by definition , a conductor
fol: (FORALL x (conductor(x) IMPLIES (facilitate_transmit_electricity(x) AND allow_easy_flow_electric_charge(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (material(x) AND allow_electric_current_flow(x) AND low_resist(x) IMPLIES conductor(x))) AND ( FORALL x (substance(x) AND allow_flow_electric_charge(x) AND low_resist(x) IMPLIES conductor(x)))

nl: A cyclist needs a bicycle to participate in a race. Cyclists also use bicycles for transportation. If someone travels to work or school on a bicycle , they are considered a cyclist. Therefore , using a bicycle is central to both competitive racing and everyday commuting for cyclists
fol: (FORALL x (person_use_bicycle(x) IMPLIES need_bicycle_race(x))) AND ( FORALL x (person_use_bicycle(x) IMPLIES use_bicycle_transport(x))) AND ( FORALL x (person(x) AND travel_work_or_school_on_bicycle(x) IMPLIES person_use_bicycle(x))) AND ( FORALL x (person_use_bicycle(x) IMPLIES use_bicycle_compete(x) OR use_bicycle_commute(x)))

nl: A software company that develops accessible technology and adheres to ethical practices benefits the disabled community and gains a positive reputation
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((software_company(x) AND accessible_technology(y) AND develop(x, y) AND adhere_ethics(x)) IMPLIES (benefit_disable_community(z) AND gain_positive_reputation(x))))

nl: A carnivore eats meat , a herbivore eats plants , and an omnivore eats both meat and plants
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((carnivore(x) AND herbivore(y) AND omnivore(z)) IMPLIES ((eat_meat(x) AND eat_plant(y)) AND (eat_meat(z) AND eat_plant(z)))))

nl: If a vehicle is a car , it has four wheels and is powered by an internal combustion engine or an electric motor
fol: (FORALL x (car(x) IMPLIES (four_wheel(x) AND ((internal_combust_engine(x) AND NOT electric_motor(x)) OR (not internal_combust_engine(x) AND electric_motor(x))))))

nl: A table is a piece of furniture with a flat surface typically supported by legs. A table supports objects when it is stable. We know that a table is a table , meaning it exists. Therefore , a table supports objects
fol: (FORALL x (table(x) IMPLIES (furniture(x) AND flat_surface(x) AND support_leg(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (table(x) AND stable(x) IMPLIES support_object(x))) AND ( FORALL x (table(x) IMPLIES exist(x))) AND ( FORALL x (table(x) IMPLIES support_object(x)))

nl: A vegetarian is defined as someone who abstains from consuming meat , fish , or poultry. This definition can be simplified. An individual is still considered a vegetarian if they avoid meat and fish. The exclusion of poultry remains consistent across both definitions. Therefore , avoiding meat , fish , and poultry is the core requirement for vegetarianism
fol: (FORALL x (someone_thing(x) AND (NOT consume_meat(x) AND NOT consume_fish(x) AND NOT consume_poultry(x)) IMPLIES vegetarian(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((NOT consume_meat(x) AND NOT consume_fish(x)) IMPLIES vegetarian(x)))

nl: An apple is a fruit and a carrot is a vegetable , but neither is a mineral
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (apple(x) IMPLIES fruit(x)) AND (carrot(y) IMPLIES vegetable(y)) AND NOT (mineral(x) OR mineral(y)))

nl: A beverage is considered warm if its temperature exceeds room temperature. A beverage is either hot or cold. Lukewarm is not a possibility. Therefore , a beverage 's temperature must be definitively hot or cold
fol: (FORALL x (beverage(x) AND exceed_room_temperature(x) IMPLIES warm(x))) AND ( FORALL x (beverage(x) IMPLIES (hot(x) OR cold(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (beverage(x) IMPLIES NOT warm(x)))

nl: A painting 's value increases if it 's rare. A painting is also valuable if created by a famous artist. Furthermore , good condition enhances a painting 's value , especially if created by a renowned artist. Therefore , rarity and the artist 's fame significantly impact a painting 's worth. Excellent condition further increases the value of a painting by a renowned artist
fol: (FORALL x (paint(x) AND rare(x) IMPLIES value_increase(x))) AND ( FORALL x (paint(x) AND create_artist(x) IMPLIES value(x))) AND ( FORALL x (paint(x) AND good_condition(x) AND create_renowned_artist(x) IMPLIES value_increase(x))) AND ( FORALL x (paint(x) AND excellent_condition(x) AND create_renowned_artist(x) IMPLIES value_increase(x)))

nl: An architect designs buildings , bridges , or other structures and takes into account aesthetics , function , and safety
fol: (FORALL x (architect(x) IMPLIES ((design_building(x) OR design_bridge(x) OR design_other_structure(x)) AND consider_aesthetics(x) AND consider_function(x) AND consider_safe(x))))

nl: An animal is considered a mammal if it has hair or fur , gives birth to live young , and produces milk
fol: (FORALL x (animal(x) AND (hair_or_fur(x) OR fur(x) OR hair(x)) AND gives_birth_to_live_young(x) AND produce_milk(x) IMPLIES mammal(x)))

nl: An athlete is eligible for a competition if they meet the age requirement , pass a drug test , and are not disqualified for any other reason
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((athlete(x) AND compete(y) AND age_require(x, y) AND drug_test(x, y) AND NOT disqualify(x, y)) IMPLIES qualify(x, y)))

nl: A mail carrier delivers packages to recipients , except on Sundays
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((mail_carrier(x) AND package(y) AND person(z) AND NOT sunday(x)) IMPLIES deliver(x, y, z)))

nl: Mathematicians solve problems in fields such as algebra , calculus , and geometry
fol: (FORALL x (mathematician(x) AND solve_problem(x) AND FORALL y (problem(y) AND solve(y, x) IMPLIES (algebra(y) OR calculus(y) OR geometry(y)))))

nl: A game that is played on a board and involves moving pieces according to a set of rules is a board game
fol: (FORALL x (game(x) AND play_board(x) AND EXISTS y EXISTS z (piece(y) AND rule(z) AND involve_move_piece(x, y, z)) IMPLIES board_game(x)))

nl: An umbrella protects from rain , but it does n't protect from snow or strong wind
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((umbrella(x) AND rain(y)) IMPLIES protect(x, y)) AND (not ((umbrella(x) AND (snow(y) OR strong_wind(y))) IMPLIES protect(x, y))))

nl: Doctors diagnose illnesses. They also treat illnesses. Therefore , doctors diagnose and treat illnesses. Pharmacists dispense medication. Thus , pharmacists dispense medications. If doctors diagnose and treat illnesses , and pharmacists dispense medications , then the medical field functions
fol: (FORALL x (doctor(x) IMPLIES (diagnose_ill(x) AND treat_ill(x)))) AND ( FORALL y (chemist(y) IMPLIES dispense_medication(y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y ((doctor(x) AND (diagnose_ill(x) AND treat_ill(x))) AND (chemist(y) AND dispense_medication(y)) IMPLIES field_function))

nl: A well - designed product must be either user - friendly or durable , but not both , and it should be either eco - friendly or have a low manufacturing cost
fol: (FORALL x (well_design_product(x) IFF (((user_friendly(x) OR durable(x)) AND NOT (user_friendly(x) AND durable(x))) AND (eco_friendly(x) OR low_manufacture_cost(x)))))

nl: Museums preserve history and culture by displaying artifacts , artworks , and documents from various time periods and regions. Museums exhibit artifacts from different countries and historical periods. Each artifact displayed in a museum comes from a specific time period. Therefore , museums play a crucial role in preserving and showcasing the diverse history and cultural heritage of different places and eras
fol: (FORALL x (museum(x) IMPLIES preserve_history_culture(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (museum(x) AND artifact(y) IMPLIES exhibit(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (artifact(y) AND inmuseum(x, y) IMPLIES come_from_time_period(y))) AND ( FORALL x (museum(x) IMPLIES showcase_diverse_history_culture_heritage(x)))

nl: Birds can fly , but fish swim
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((bird(x) AND fish(y)) IMPLIES ((fly(x) AND NOT swim(y)) OR (not fly(x) AND swim(y)))))

nl: During an election , citizens vote for their preferred candidates , who compete for various political offices
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (elect(x) AND citizen(y) AND candidate(z) AND vote_for(y, z) AND compete(z) IMPLIES various(x, y, z)))

nl: A predator hunts and consumes prey for sustenance , while the prey population affects the abundance of predators in an ecosystem
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (predator(x) AND hunt_and_consume(x, y) AND prey(y) AND affect_abundance(y, z) AND ecosystem(z) IMPLIES sustenance_population(x, z)))

nl: A volcano erupts when molten rock , called magma , rises to the surface and is expelled as lava and ash
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((volcano(x) AND rock(y) AND surface(z) AND magma(y) AND rise(x,z)) IMPLIES erupt_lava_ash(x)))

nl: A computer network connects multiple devices if it enables data transmission and communication between the devices
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((computer_network(x) AND device(y) AND device(z) AND enable_data_transmit(x, y, z) AND communicate(x, y, z)) IMPLIES connect(x, y, z)))

nl: A painting is valuable when it is created by a renowned artist and is well preserved
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (paint(x) AND artist(y) AND preserve(z) AND renowned(y) AND well_preserve(x, z) IMPLIES value(x)))

nl: A computer boots up when the power button is pressed , the BIOS initializes , and the operating system loads
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (computer(x) AND power_button(y) AND press(y) AND initialize(z) AND load(x) IMPLIES boots(x)))

nl: A builder constructs houses using bricks and mortar
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z FORALL w (build(x) AND house(y) AND brick(z) AND mortar(w) IMPLIES construct_use(x, y, z, w)))

nl: A musical composition that combines catchy melodies , engaging rhythms , and evocative lyrics often becomes popular
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (music_thing(x) AND catchy_melody(y) AND engage_rhythm(z) AND evoke_lyric(x) AND combine_thing(x, y, z) IMPLIES often_popular(x)))

nl: A computer network allows data exchange between interconnected devices by facilitating communication through a set of protocols
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z FORALL w (computer_network(x) AND data_exchange(y) AND interconnect_device(z) AND communication_protocol(w) IMPLIES allow_data_exchange(x, y, z, w)))

nl: A mountain x has an elevation of y meters and is part of mountain range z if x 's peak is y meters above sea level and x is located within z
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((mountain(x) AND elevation(y) AND mountain_range(z) AND peak_at_elevation(x, y) AND located_within(x, z)) IMPLIES have_elevation_and_mountain_range(x, y, z)))

nl: When light passes through a prism , it is refracted and separates into its component colors
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (light(x) AND prism(y) AND pass(x, y) AND refract(x, z) AND component(z) IMPLIES separate(x, y, z)))

nl: A type of computer software that allows users to create , edit , and manage digital documents , such as text , images , and multimedia , is a document editor
fol: (FORALL x (computer_software(x) AND allow_document_create(x) AND allow_document_edit(x) AND manage_digital_document(x) AND manage_text(x) AND manage_image(x) AND manage_multimedia(x) IMPLIES document_edit(x)))

nl: A photographer takes photos , edits them , and uploads the images to a website for sharing
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((photograph(x) AND photo(y) AND website(z) AND take(x, y) AND edit(x, y)) IMPLIES upload(x, y, z)))

nl: A chemical reaction takes place between two elements if they combine and form a new compound
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (chemical_react(x) AND element(y) AND element(z) AND combine(y, z) AND form_compound(x) IMPLIES react(x, y, z)))

nl: A painter x uses color y for painting z if x applies y to the canvas and the artwork z features the color y
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((person_paint(x) AND color(y) AND artwork(z) AND apply_color(x, y) AND feature_color(z, y)) IMPLIES use_color_artwork(x, y, z)))

nl: A farmer cultivates crops on fertile land , providing food for the local community
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((farm_person(x) AND crop(c) AND fertile_land(y) AND food(f) AND local_community(z)) IMPLIES cultivate_provide_food(x, c, y, f, z)))

nl: Air conditioning systems cool and dehumidify indoor spaces , providing comfort during hot and humid conditions
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((air_system(x) AND cool(c) AND dehumidify(d) AND indoor_space(y) AND hot_humid_condition(z)) IMPLIES cool_dehumidify_comfort(x, c, d, y, z)))

nl: This particular game is multiplayer. It also operates as an online game. Video games generally offer various modes of play. These options can include a single - player mode , a multiplayer mode , or even a combination of both. Consequently , this specific game exemplifies the multiplayer mode available within the broader category of video games
fol: (EXISTS x (game(x) AND multiplayer(x) AND online(x))) AND ( FORALL x (video_game(x) IMPLIES (single_player_mode(x) OR multiplayer_mode(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (game(x) AND multiplayer(x) IMPLIES video_game(x)))

nl: A person is officially considered a senior citizen upon reaching a specific age. Eligibility for a senior discount , however , is determined by two distinct criteria. Firstly , an individual qualifies if they have attained this specific senior age. Alternatively , a person may also be eligible if they are retired , regardless of their age. Consequently , any individual who meets the age requirement for senior citizen status will automatically be eligible for the senior discount
fol: (FORALL x (person(x) AND senior_citizen(x) IMPLIES eligible_for_senior_discount(x))) AND ( FORALL x (person(x) AND reach_specific_age(x) IMPLIES senior_citizen(x))) AND ( FORALL x (eligible_for_senior_discount(x) IFF (reach_specific_age(x) OR retire(x))))

nl: A car has four wheels. Furthermore , all cars possess both four wheels and an engine. If a vehicle has four wheels and is designed for passenger transportation , then it is classified as a car. Therefore , a car can be defined by its four wheels and purpose of passenger transportation. The engine provides the power needed for movement
fol: (FORALL x (car(x) IMPLIES have_wheel(x))) AND ( FORALL x (car(x) IMPLIES (have_wheel(x) AND have_engine(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((vehicle(x) AND wheel(x) AND passenger_transport(x)) IMPLIES car(x))) AND ( FORALL x (car(x) IMPLIES (wheel(x) AND passenger_transport(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (have_engine(x) IMPLIES provide_power(x)))

nl: A camera captures images. Additionally , a camera can record videos. Since it captures both still and moving images , a camera must have a lens. A video camera also captures moving images. Therefore , both cameras share a common function , capturing visual data
fol: (FORALL x (camera(x) IMPLIES capture_image(x))) AND ( FORALL x (camera(x) IMPLIES record_video(x))) AND ( FORALL x (camera(x) IMPLIES have_lens(x))) AND ( FORALL x (video_camera(x) IMPLIES capture_move_image(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((camera(x) OR video_camera(x)) IMPLIES capture_visual_data(x)))

nl: A volcano can be active , dormant , or extinct , while a mountain is not volcanic
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (volcano(x) AND mountain(y) IMPLIES ((activity(x) OR dormant(x) OR extinct(x)) AND NOT volcano(y))))

nl: Not every music genre is fast - paced ; some are slow , and others have a moderate tempo
fol: (NOT ( FORALL x (music_genre(x) IMPLIES fast_pace(x))) AND EXISTS y EXISTS z (music_genre(y) AND slow(y) AND music_genre(z) AND moderate_tempo(z)))

nl: An appliance can be small or large , designed for the kitchen or laundry room , and powered by electricity , gas , or batteries
fol: (FORALL x (appliance(x) IMPLIES ((small(x) XOR large(x)) AND ((kitchen_appliance(x)) XOR (laundry_appliance(x))) AND ((electricity(x) OR gas(x) OR battery_power(x))))))

nl: A job can be classified as either full - time or part - time , but not both. If a job 's work hours are less than full - time hours , then it is a part - time job. Therefore , if a job is not full - time , it must be part - time
fol: (FORALL x (job(x) IMPLIES (full_time(x) OR part_time(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (job(x) AND work_hour_less(x) IMPLIES part_time(x))) AND ( FORALL x (job(x) AND NOT full_time(x) IMPLIES part_time(x)))

nl: Fruits have seeds , but vegetables do not typically. This implies that if something is a fruit , it will have seeds. Furthermore , if something does not have seeds , it is likely not a fruit. Since vegetables generally lack seeds , they are typically not classified as fruits
fol: (FORALL x (fruit(x) IMPLIES seed(x))) AND ( FORALL y (vegetable(y) IMPLIES NOT seed(y))) AND ( FORALL x (NOT seed(x) IMPLIES NOT fruit(x))) AND ( FORALL z (vegetable(z) IMPLIES NOT fruit(z)))

nl: Cats are domesticated animals. They sleep for many hours each day. If cats are more active at night , then they are nocturnal. Some cats prefer to sleep during the day and be active at night , making them nocturnal. Therefore , cats are nocturnal animals
fol: (FORALL c (cat(c) IMPLIES domesticate_animal(c))) AND ( FORALL c (cat(c) IMPLIES sleep_many_hour(c))) AND ( FORALL c (cat(c) AND more_active_at_night(c) IMPLIES nocturnal(c))) AND ( EXISTS c (cat(c) AND sleep_during_day(c) AND active_at_night(c))) AND ( FORALL c (cat(c) IMPLIES nocturnal(c)))

nl: Insects are defined by having six legs. This means they possess three pairs of legs. Additionally , an insect 's body is segmented. Specifically , an insect 's body has three main parts : the head , thorax , and abdomen. Therefore , insects have six legs and a segmented body comprised of a head , thorax , and abdomen
fol: (FORALL x (insect(x) IMPLIES have_leg(x))) AND ( FORALL x (insect(x) IMPLIES have_pair_leg(x))) AND ( FORALL x (insect(x) IMPLIES have_segment(x))) AND ( FORALL x (insect(x) IMPLIES (have_body_part(x, head) AND have_body_part(x, thorax) AND have_body_part(x, abdomen)))) AND ( FORALL x (insect(x) IMPLIES (have_leg(x) AND have_segment(x) AND have_body_part(x, head) AND have_body_part(x, thorax) AND have_body_part(x, abdomen))))

nl: A desert is a place that receives very little rainfall. Extreme temperature variations are also characteristic of deserts. Therefore , a region with low precipitation and extreme temperature fluctuations is a desert. This definition encompasses both hot and cold deserts , as both share these defining features
fol: (FORALL x (place(x) AND receive_very_little_rain(x) IMPLIES desert(x))) AND ( FORALL x (desert(x) IMPLIES have_extreme_temperature_vary(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((low_precipitation(x) AND extreme_temperature_vary(x)) IMPLIES desert(x))) AND ( FORALL x (desert(x) IMPLIES (hot(x) OR cold(x))))

nl: A mountain range is a series of mountains that are geographically related and share common geological origins , such as tectonic activity or volcanic processes
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (mountain_range(x) IMPLIES (series_mountain(x, y) AND geographic_relate(y) AND share_originate(y, z) AND (tectonic_activity(z) OR volcano_process(z)))))

nl: Engineers apply scientific principles to design , build , and maintain structures , machines , or systems
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (engineer(x) AND (structure(y) OR machine(y) OR system(y)) IMPLIES (apply_science_principle(x) AND designs(x, y) AND builds(x, y) AND maintains(x, y))))

nl: A chair is for sitting , a table is for placing items , and a bed is for sleeping or resting
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((chair(x) AND table(y) AND bed(z)) IMPLIES ((sit(x) AND place_item(y)) AND (sleep(z) OR rest(z)))))

nl: An event can be indoors , outdoors , or virtual , but not all three options
fol: (EXISTS x (event(x) AND ((indoor(x) AND outdoors(x) AND NOT virtual(x)) OR (indoor(x) AND NOT outdoors(x) AND virtual(x)) OR (not indoor(x) AND outdoors(x) AND virtual(x)))))

nl: A concerto is a musical composition. It is written for a solo instrument and accompanied by an orchestra. A symphony is also a musical composition. However , a symphony is written for an entire orchestra. Symphonies have multiple movements , combining various instruments. These instruments create harmony together
fol: (FORALL x (concerto(x) IMPLIES (music(x) AND write_solo_instrument(x) AND accompany_orchestra(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (symphony(x) IMPLIES (music(x) AND write_orchestra(x) AND have_multiple_move(x) AND combine_various_instrument(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (have_multiple_move(x) AND combine_various_instrument(x) IMPLIES create_harmony(x)))

nl: A material is an insulator if it resists the flow of electric current. Insulators also have low electrical conductivity. Thus , a material 's resistance to electric current flow implies it is an insulator. Furthermore , insulators resist the flow of electric charge. This resistance prevents the transfer of electricity. Consequently , insulators provide protection against electric shock
fol: (FORALL x (material(x) AND resist_flow_electric_current(x) IMPLIES insulator(x))) AND ( FORALL x (insulator(x) IMPLIES low_electric_conduct(x))) AND ( FORALL x (resist_flow_electric_current(x) IMPLIES insulator(x))) AND ( FORALL x (insulator(x) IMPLIES resist_flow_electric_charge(x))) AND ( FORALL x (resist_flow_electric_charge(x) IMPLIES prevent_transfer_electricity(x))) AND ( FORALL x (insulator(x) IMPLIES protect_against_electric_shock(x)))

nl: An athlete can participate in individual sports , team sports , or both , but cannot participate in neither
fol: (FORALL x (athlete(x) IMPLIES ((individual_sport(x) AND NOT team_sport(x)) OR (not individual_sport(x) AND team_sport(x)) OR (individual_sport(x) AND team_sport(x)))))

nl: A good insulator resists the flow of heat , electricity , or sound. Material X is a good thermal insulator. Therefore , Material X has low thermal conductivity and prevents heat transfer. Insulators , in general , resist the flow of heat , electricity , * and * sound. These properties collectively define a material 's insulating capability
fol: (FORALL x (good_insulator(x) IMPLIES (resist_heat_flow(x) AND resist_electricity_flow(x) AND resist_sound_flow(x)))) AND ( material(variablex) AND good_thermal_insulator(variablex)) AND ( good_thermal_insulator(variablex) IMPLIES (low_thermal_conduct(variablex) AND prevent_heat_transfer(variablex))) AND ( FORALL x (insulator(x) IMPLIES (resist_heat_flow(x) AND resist_electricity_flow(x) AND resist_sound_flow(x))))

nl: Astronauts explore space. They also conduct experiments. When astronauts explore space , they wear spacesuits. Geologists , on the other hand , study Earth 's structure. They also investigate Earth 's processes. So , astronauts focus on space , while geologists focus on Earth
fol: (FORALL x (astronaut(x) IMPLIES (explore_space(x) AND conduct_experiment(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (geologist(x) IMPLIES (study_structure(x) AND study_process(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (astronaut(x) AND explore_space(x) IMPLIES wear_spacesuit(x))) AND ( FORALL x (astronaut(x) IMPLIES focus_space(x))) AND ( FORALL x (geologist(x) IMPLIES focus_earth(x)))

nl: In a theater , actors perform on stage , and audiences watch the performance from seats
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((theater(x) AND act(y) AND audience(z) AND in_theater(y, x) AND in_theater(z, x)) IMPLIES (perform_stage(y) AND watch(z))))

nl: A sustainable water management plan that prioritizes water conservation , efficient distribution , and pollution control can safeguard water resources and ensure long - term availability
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (sustainable_water_manage_plan(x) AND prioritize_water_conserve(y) AND efficient_distribute(z) AND pollute_control(x) AND prioritize_elements(x, y, z) IMPLIES safeguard_water_resource(x) AND ensure_long_term_available(x)))

nl: In a restaurant , chefs cook food , and waiters serve food to customers
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (restaurant(x) AND chef(y) AND waiter(z) AND worksat(y, x) AND worksat(z, x) IMPLIES (cook_food(y) AND serve_food_customer(z))))

nl: Fish swim. This is a known fact. No fish can live on land without water. Therefore , a fish 's survival depends on aquatic environments. Their ability to swim is essential to their existence in water. Consequently , a fish removed from water will die
fol: (FORALL x (fish(x) IMPLIES swim(x))) AND ( FORALL x (fish(x) IMPLIES NOT live_land_without_water(x))) AND ( FORALL x (fish(x) IMPLIES depend_on(x, environment_water))) AND ( FORALL x (fish(x) IMPLIES survive_have(x, swim))) AND ( FORALL x (fish(x) AND NOT water(x) IMPLIES die(x)))

nl: Libraries provide access to various resources. These include books , magazines , and digital resources. They also often offer study areas. Furthermore , libraries may host events and workshops. Thus , libraries serve multiple functions beyond simply lending materials. They support learning and community engagement
fol: (FORALL x (library(x) IMPLIES (offer_access_book(x) AND offer_access_magazine(x) AND offer_access_digital_resource(x) AND provide_study_area(x) AND host_event(x) AND host_workshop(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (library(x) IMPLIES (support_learn(x) AND support_community_engage(x))))

nl: A well - maintained bicycle with properly inflated tires ensures a smoother ride. This also reduces the risk of accidents. If a bicycle has a flat tire , it is difficult to ride. Consequently , a bicycle with flat tires cannot be ridden efficiently until the tires are inflated
fol: (FORALL x (well_maintain_bicycle(x) AND proper_inflate_tire(x) IMPLIES (ensure_smooth_ride(x) AND reduce_risk_accident(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (bicycle(x) AND flat_tire(x) IMPLIES difficult_ride(x))) AND ( FORALL x (bicycle(x) AND flat_tire(x) IMPLIES NOT can_be_ridden_until_inflate(x))) AND ( FORALL x (bicycle(x) AND flat_tire(x) IMPLIES NOT ensure_smooth_ride(x)))

nl: Museums often display artifacts , works of art , or specimens related to history , science , or culture , but not all exhibits are open to the public
fol: (FORALL x (museum(x) IMPLIES ((display_artifact(x) OR display_work_art(x) OR display_specimen(x)) AND (relate_history(x) OR relate_science(x) OR relate_culture(x)) AND NOT open_public(x))))

nl: An engineer designs machines or structures , but not both , and does n't work on electronic devices or software
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (engineer(x) AND (machine(y) XOR structure(y)) AND NOT electronic_device(y) AND NOT software(y) IMPLIES design(x, y)))

nl: A digital camera is an electronic device. It captures and records visual images. This device also processes these images digitally. Digital cameras store the captured images. Some digital cameras also record videos. Therefore , a digital camera is an electronic device that captures images , records videos , and stores multimedia files
fol: (FORALL x (digital_camera(x) IMPLIES electronic_device(x))) AND ( FORALL x (digital_camera(x) IMPLIES captures_visual_image(x))) AND ( FORALL x (digital_camera(x) IMPLIES record_visual_image(x))) AND ( FORALL x (digital_camera(x) IMPLIES process_image_digitally(x))) AND ( FORALL x (digital_camera(x) IMPLIES store_image_digitally(x))) AND ( FORALL x (digital_camera(x) IMPLIES record_video(x))) AND ( FORALL x (digital_camera(x) IMPLIES store_multimedia_file(x)))

nl: An object qualifies as a toy if it 's designed for play. Furthermore , a toy is typically made of either wood or plastic. However , a toy is n't simultaneously made of both wood and plastic. Toys can also incorporate metal in their construction. Thus , a toy can be made of wood , plastic , or metal
fol: (FORALL x ((object(x) AND meant_for_play(x)) IMPLIES toy(x))) AND ( FORALL x (toy(x) IMPLIES (made_of_wood(x) OR made_of_plastic(x) OR made_of_metal(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (toy(x) IMPLIES NOT (made_of_wood(x) AND made_of_plastic(x))))

nl: A photosynthetic organism , such as a plant or algae , uses sunlight , water , and carbon dioxide to produce glucose and oxygen
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((photosynthetic_organism(x) AND (plant(x) OR algae(x))) AND sunlight(y) AND water(z) IMPLIES produce_glucose_oxygen(x, y, z)))

nl: An organism that can synthesize its own food using light , water , and carbon dioxide , while producing oxygen as a byproduct , is specifically termed a photoautotroph. This complex process of food creation is known as photosynthesis. Therefore , any organism exhibiting these traits is also correctly identified as a photosynthetic organism. Furthermore , such an organism commonly possesses the capacities for movement and reproduction
fol: (FORALL x (organism(x) AND synthesize_food(x) AND use_light_water(x) AND produce_oxygen(x) IMPLIES photoautotroph(x))) AND ( FORALL x (photoautotroph(x) IMPLIES produce_food_photosynthesis(x))) AND ( FORALL x (photoautotroph(x) IMPLIES photosynthetic_organism(x))) AND ( FORALL x (photosynthetic_organism(x) IMPLIES (move(x) AND reproduce(x))))

nl: A building that contains a collection of books , manuscripts , or other materials for reading and research is a library or an archive
fol: (FORALL x (building(x) AND contain_collect(x) AND (contain_book(x) OR contain_manuscript(x) OR contain_other_material(x)) AND read(x) AND research(x) IMPLIES (library(x) OR archive(x))))

nl: A national park consists of protected natural areas , where visitors can engage in activities such as hiking , camping , and wildlife observation
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((nation_park(x) AND protect_natural_area(x) AND visit(y) AND (hike(z) OR camp(z) OR wildlife_observe(z))) IMPLIES engage_activity(y, z, x)))

nl: A planet orbits a star and is not a satellite of another planet , while a moon is a natural satellite orbiting a planet
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((planet(x) AND star(y) AND moon(z)) IMPLIES ((orbit(x, y) AND NOT satellite_planet(x)) AND (natural_satellite(z) AND orbit(z, x)))))

nl: Athletes qualify for a team if they exhibit skill , discipline , and teamwork. A key aspect of athletic success is the ability to excel in various sports , including both individual and team sports. Therefore , demonstrating the required skills is essential for team membership
fol: (FORALL x (athlete(x) AND demonstrate_skill(x) AND demonstrate_discipline(x) AND demonstrate_teamwork(x) IMPLIES qualify_team(x))) AND ( FORALL x (athlete(x) IMPLIES (excel_individual_sport(x) OR excel_team_sport(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (athlete(x) AND demonstrate_skill(x) IMPLIES essential_team_member(x)))

nl: A watch 's primary function is to display time. This display can be realized through either a digital or an analog mechanism. Some watches incorporate a combination of both digital and analog displays. Furthermore , watches often include additional features. These may involve date displays or alarm functions. Thus , watches are versatile timekeeping devices
fol: (FORALL x (watch(x) IMPLIES display_time(x))) AND ( FORALL x (display_time(x) IMPLIES (digital(x) OR analog(x) OR combine_both(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (watch(x) IMPLIES (include_date(x) OR include_alarm(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (watch(x) IMPLIES versatile_time(x)))

nl: In any food chain or food web , primary producers are fundamental. They convert sunlight into usable energy. This process occurs through photosynthesis. Primary consumers then acquire this energy by feeding on the primary producers. Following this , secondary consumers obtain energy by consuming the primary consumers. This sequential transfer of energy ensures a continuous flow throughout the ecosystem
fol: (FORALL x (food_chain(x) IMPLIES primary_producer(x))) AND ( FORALL x (primary_producer(x) IMPLIES convert_sunlight_energy(x))) AND ( FORALL x (convert_sunlight_energy(x) IMPLIES photosynthesis(x))) AND ( FORALL x (secondary_consumer(x) IMPLIES feed_on(x, primary_producer))) AND ( FORALL x (secondary_consumer(x) IMPLIES feed_on(x, primary_consumer))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (transfer_energy(x, y) IMPLIES continue_flow_ecosystem(x, y)))

nl: Some buildings have both historic significance and welcome visitors. Buildings with modern designs can be either offices or museums. Paris is the capital of France. It is famous for landmarks like the Eiffel Tower. Paris also houses the Louvre Museum
fol: (EXISTS x (building(x) AND history_significant(x) AND welcome_visit(x))) AND ( FORALL x (building(x) AND modern_design(x) IMPLIES (office(x) OR museum(x)))) AND ( capital(paris, france) AND fame(paris, eiffeltower) AND house(paris, louvremuseum))

nl: A person who practices medicine and treats patients is known as a doctor. It follows that a doctor will either practice general medicine or perform surgery. Therefore , an individual who practices medicine and treats patients will necessarily engage in either medical or surgical practice. Furthermore , a doctor who specializes specifically in the heart is recognized as a cardiologist
fol: (FORALL x ((practice_medicine(x) AND treat_patient(x)) IMPLIES doctor(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((doctor(x) AND specialize_in_heart(x)) IMPLIES cardiologist(x))) AND ( FORALL x (doctor(x) IMPLIES (practice_general_medicine(x) OR perform_surgery(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((practice_medicine(x) AND treat_patient(x)) IMPLIES (medical_practice(x) OR surgery_practice(x))))

nl: Rivers transport sediment and nutrients from their source to the ocean , shaping coastlines and supporting marine ecosystems
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (river(x) AND sediment(x) AND nutrient(x) AND ocean(x) AND coastline(y) AND marine_ecosystem(z) IMPLIES transport_shape_support(x, x, x, x, y, z)))

nl: An iceberg melts when the surrounding water temperature rises above freezing , the iceberg is exposed to sunlight , and the air temperature is warm enough
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((iceberg(x) AND water_temperature(y) AND air_temperature(z) AND above_freeze(y) AND expose_sunlight(x) AND warm_enough(z)) IMPLIES melt(x)))

nl: In a game of chess , a player can capture the opponent 's piece only if their piece occupies the same square
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (chess_game(x) AND play(y) AND oppose(z) AND piece(y) AND piece(z) AND occupy_same_square(y, z) IMPLIES capture(y, z)))

nl: A plant that uses the process of photosynthesis to convert sunlight , carbon dioxide , and nutrients into chemical energy for growth is a photoautotroph
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z FORALL a (plant(x) AND sunlight(y) AND chemical(z) AND nutrients(a) AND photosynthesis(x, y, z, a) IMPLIES photoautotroph(x)))

nl: A company can launch a product in a market if the product complies with market regulations , the company has a distribution network in the market , and the company has completed the required marketing activities
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (company(x) AND product(y) AND market(z) AND comply_regulate(y, z) AND distribute_network(x, z) AND complete_market(x, y) IMPLIES can_launch(x, y, z)))

nl: A machine that is used to convert energy from one form to another , consists of moving mechanical parts , and requires regular maintenance to function properly is an energy conversion machine
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (machine(x) AND energy_form(y) AND mechanics_part(z) AND convert_energy(x, y) AND consist_of(x, z) AND require_regular_maintain(x) IMPLIES energy_convert_machine(x)))

nl: An airport can handle international flights if it has customs facilities , immigration services , and adequate infrastructure
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((airport(x) AND customs_facility(y) AND immigrate_service(z) AND adequate_infrastructure(x) AND have(x, y) AND have(x, z)) IMPLIES handle_international(x)))

nl: A fire requires fuel , heat , and oxygen to burn
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z FORALL w (fire(x) AND fuel(y) AND heat(z) AND oxygen(w) IMPLIES (require(x, y) AND require(x, z) AND require(x, w))))

nl: A bicycle has two wheels and is powered by pedaling , while a motorcycle has two wheels and an engine , and a car has four wheels and an engine
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((bicycle(x) AND motorcycle(y) AND car(z)) IMPLIES ((have_wheel(x) AND power_pedal(x)) AND (have_wheel(y) AND have_engine(y)) AND (have_wheel(z) AND have_engine(z)))))

nl: A successful space mission is characterized by multiple factors. First , it must reach its destination. Second , the mission needs to fulfill its objectives , including scientific aims. Furthermore , the return of valuable data is crucial for judging success. A well - trained crew is essential for mission success. Advanced technology also significantly contributes to a successful mission. Adequate funding ensures the mission can be properly executed. If a space mission achieves its scientific objectives and returns valuable data , it 's considered a success. Completing the planned duration further reinforces its successful designation. Therefore , success in space missions involves a combination of planning , resources , and execution
fol: (FORALL x (space_mission(x) IMPLIES (reach_destination(x) AND fulfill_objective(x) AND return_value_data(x) IMPLIES succeed(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (space_mission(x) IMPLIES (well_train_crew(x) AND advanced_technology(x) AND adequate_fund(x) IMPLIES succeed(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (space_mission(x) IMPLIES (achieve_science_objective(x) AND return_value_data(x) AND complete_plan_duration(x) IMPLIES succeed(x))))

nl: Flowers pollinated by insects usually exhibit bright colors and strong fragrances. The presence of bright colors and a sweet fragrance implies that a flower is pollinated by insects. Therefore , if a flower has bright colors and a strong fragrance , it is likely pollinated by insects. This suggests an evolutionary advantage : attracting insects leads to successful pollination. Consequently , these traits are commonly observed in insect - pollinated flowers
fol: (FORALL x (flower(x) AND pollinate_by_insect(x) IMPLIES (have_bright_color(x) AND have_strong_fragrance(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (flower(x) AND (have_bright_color(x) AND have_sweet_fragrance(x)) IMPLIES pollinate_by_insect(x))) AND ( FORALL x (flower(x) AND (have_bright_color(x) AND have_strong_fragrance(x)) IMPLIES pollinate_by_insect(x))) AND ( FORALL x (attract_insect(x) IMPLIES succeed_pollination(x))) AND ( FORALL x (flower(x) AND pollinate_by_insect(x) IMPLIES (have_bright_color(x) AND have_strong_fragrance(x))))

nl: An airplane is a vehicle designed for air travel. It must have wings to be considered an airplane. Furthermore , an airplane can fly and is powered by engines. Transportation airplanes also include a fuselage and are typically powered by jet engines. Therefore , a vehicle traveling through the air with wings is indeed an airplane
