nl: A gemstone is either precious or semiprecious. It cannot be both. If a gemstone is rare , durable , and exhibits exceptional beauty , then it is classified as precious. Jewelers craft jewelry
fol: (FORALL x (gemstone(x) IMPLIES (precious(x) OR semiprecious(x)))) AND ( FORALL x NOT (precious(x) AND semiprecious(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((gemstone(x) AND rare(x) AND durable(x) AND exceptional_beauty(x)) IMPLIES precious(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y ((person(x) AND jewelry(y)) IMPLIES craft(x, y)))

nl: A palindrome is defined as a word , phrase , or sequence that reads identically forward and backward. This characteristic applies even when spaces , punctuation , and capitalization are disregarded. Consequently , for any text to be classified as a palindrome , it must inherently maintain this symmetrical readability. Interestingly , languages themselves exhibit diverse reading orientations. Some are written from left to right , others from right to left , and a few can even accommodate both directions. Yet , the core property of a palindrome remains consistent , independent of a language 's specific directional flow
fol: (FORALL x (palindrome(x) IFF read_same_forward_backward(x))) AND ( FORALL x (text(x) AND read_same_forward_backward_space_punctuate_capitalize(x) IMPLIES palindrome(x))) AND ( EXISTS x (language(x) AND left_to_right(x))) AND ( EXISTS x (language(x) AND right_to_left(x))) AND ( EXISTS x (language(x) AND both_direction(x))) AND ( FORALL x (palindrome(x) IMPLIES consistent(x)))

nl: A light bulb emits light when turned on. A lamp also emits light when turned on. Thus , both a light bulb and a lamp emit light when turned on ( Composition ). A light bulb is either lit or unlit. Therefore , if a light bulb is not lit , then it is unlit ( Material Implication )
fol: (FORALL x (light_bulb(x) AND turn_on(x) IMPLIES emit_light(x))) AND ( FORALL x (lamp(x) AND turn_on(x) IMPLIES emit_light(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((light_bulb(x) OR lamp(x)) AND turn_on(x) IMPLIES emit_light(x))) AND ( FORALL x (light_bulb(x) IMPLIES (light(x) OR unlit(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (light_bulb(x) AND NOT light(x) IMPLIES unlit(x)))

nl: A fitness instructor teaches and guides people in exercise. A personal trainer provides individualized fitness instruction and training. Therefore , a personal trainer is a type of fitness instructor. If a person is a fitness trainer , they either teach yoga or weightlifting. If someone instructs in yoga , they are a yoga teacher. Thus , personal trainers may also be yoga teachers
fol: (FORALL x (personal_trainer(x) IMPLIES provide_individualize_fitness_train(x))) AND ( FORALL x (fitness_instructor(x) IMPLIES (person(x) AND teach_exercise(x) AND guide_exercise(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (personal_trainer(x) IMPLIES (teach_yoga(x) OR teach_weightlift(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (instruct_yoga(x) IMPLIES yoga_teacher(x))) AND ( FORALL x (personal_trainer(x) IMPLIES fitness_instructor(x))) AND ( FORALL x (personal_trainer(x) IMPLIES yoga_teacher(x)))

nl: Reptiles are animals with scales , cold blood , and a tendency to lay eggs. An organism is classified as a reptile if it possesses these three characteristics. A reptile 's scaly skin and cold - blooded nature are defining features. Reptiles also typically lay eggs , often on land. Therefore , an animal meeting these criteria is considered a reptile
fol: (FORALL x (animal(x) AND have_scale(x) AND cold_blood(x) AND lay_egg(x) IMPLIES reptile(x))) AND ( FORALL x (reptile(x) IMPLIES (animal(x) AND have_scale(x) AND lay_egg(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (organism(x) AND cold_blood(x) AND have_scale(x) AND lay_egg(x) IMPLIES reptile(x))) AND ( FORALL x (reptile(x) IMPLIES (have_scale(x) AND cold_blood(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (reptile(x) IMPLIES lay_egg(x))) AND ( FORALL x (animal(x) AND have_scale(x) AND cold_blood(x) AND lay_egg(x) IMPLIES reptile(x)))

nl: An athlete excels in a sport through consistent good performance and high skill. Exceptional skills are also crucial for athletic excellence. Discipline is also essential. Furthermore , an athlete needs either natural talent or strong determination to truly excel. Therefore , excellence in sports requires a combination of skill , discipline , consistent performance , and innate talent or unwavering effort
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((athlete(x) AND sport(y) AND consistent_perform(x, y) AND high_skill(x, y)) IMPLIES excel(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (athlete(x) AND excel(x, y) IMPLIES (exceptional_skill(x) AND discipline(x) AND (natural_talent(x) OR strong_determine(x))))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (excel(x, y) IMPLIES (high_skill(x, y) AND discipline(x) AND consistent_perform(x, y) AND (natural_talent(x) OR strong_determine(x)))))

nl: A substance is classified as a metal if it 's a solid at room temperature , conducts electricity , and possesses a metallic luster. This shiny appearance is often described as a metallic sheen. Furthermore , metals are malleable and ductile , meaning they can be hammered into sheets or drawn into wires. Efficient conduction of both heat and electricity is another key characteristic. Therefore , an element that exhibits good conductivity , a shiny polish , and malleability is a metal. In short , a material 's hardness , shininess , and electrical conductivity are strong indicators of its metallic nature
fol: (FORALL x (substance(x) AND solid_temperature(x) AND conduct_electricity(x) AND metal_luster(x) IMPLIES metal(x))) AND ( FORALL x (material(x) AND conduct_electricity(x) AND shiny_appearance(x) AND malleable(x) IMPLIES metal(x))) AND ( FORALL x (element(x) AND good_conduct_electricity(x) AND shiny_polish(x) AND malleable(x) IMPLIES metal(x))) AND ( FORALL x (material(x) AND hard(x) AND shiny(x) AND conduct_electricity(x) IMPLIES metal(x)))

nl: Countries with a high GDP and a stable political environment often have a high quality of life. Stable governments , strong economies , and efficient infrastructure also contribute to a higher standard of living. High GDP per capita , a high human development index , and a high standard of living define developed countries. Therefore , countries with a democratic government and a high human development index are categorized as developed nations. A high quality of life is a likely outcome in developed nations
fol: (FORALL x (country(x) AND high_gross_product(x) AND stable_politics(x) IMPLIES high_quality_life(x))) AND ( FORALL x (country(x) AND democracy_government(x) AND high_human_index(x) IMPLIES develop_nation(x))) AND ( FORALL x (country(x) AND stable_government(x) AND strong_economy(x) AND efficient_infrastructure(x) IMPLIES high_standard_live(x))) AND ( FORALL x (country(x) AND high_gross_product_capita(x) AND high_human_index(x) AND high_standard_live(x) IMPLIES develop_country(x))) AND ( FORALL x (develop_nation(x) IMPLIES high_quality_life(x)))

nl: Plants require sunlight , water , and carbon dioxide for photosynthesis. Photosynthesis is necessary for plants to produce energy. Therefore , a plant needs sunlight to produce energy. Vascular plants are classified by their ability to photosynthesize and their possession of vascular tissues and chlorophyll. Plants with green leaves perform photosynthesis during daylight hours
fol: (FORALL x (plant(x) IMPLIES (sunlight(x) AND water(x) AND carbondioxide(x) IMPLIES photosynthesis(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (plant(x) IMPLIES (photosynthesis(x) IMPLIES produce_energy(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (plant(x) IMPLIES (sunlight(x) IMPLIES produce_energy(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (vascular_plant(x) IMPLIES (plant(x) AND can_photosynthesize(x) AND have_vascular_tissue(x) AND have_chlorophyll(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (plant(x) IMPLIES (have_green_leaf(x) IMPLIES perform_photosynthesis_daylight(x))))

nl: Photosynthesis is the process green plants use to convert sunlight into chemical energy stored in glucose. Photosynthesis also involves converting carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen. Since photosynthesis is a process used by green plants , and photosynthesis requires chlorophyll , then plants need chlorophyll. However , no mammal can naturally produce chlorophyll. Therefore , no mammal can perform photosynthesis
fol: (FORALL x ((green_plant(x) AND photosynthesis(x) AND convert(x, sunlight, chemical_energy)) IMPLIES store_in_glucose(chemical_energy))) AND ( FORALL x (photosynthesis(x) IMPLIES convert(x, water, glucose, oxygen))) AND ( FORALL x ((green_plant(x) AND photosynthesis(x)) IMPLIES require_chlorophyll(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((plant(x) AND require_chlorophyll(x)) IMPLIES has_chlorophyll(x))) AND ( FORALL x (mammal(x) IMPLIES NOT produce_chlorophyll(x))) AND ( FORALL x (mammal(x) IMPLIES NOT photosynthesis(x)))

nl: A smartphone offers several key features. It provides communication capabilities. Crucially , it also allows internet access. Furthermore , a smartphone runs various applications. These apps support both communication and entertainment. Modern smartphones feature a touchscreen display. They also typically include a camera and GPS functionality. Thus , a smartphone is a versatile tool
fol: (FORALL x (smartphone(x) IMPLIES (communicate_capable(x) AND internet_access(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (smartphone(x) IMPLIES run_application(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y ((application(y) AND run_on(y, x)) IMPLIES (communicate_app(y) OR entertain_app(y)))) AND ( FORALL x (smartphone(x) IMPLIES (touchscreen_display(x) AND camera(x) AND gps_functionality(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (smartphone(x) IMPLIES versatile_tool(x)))

nl: A sculptor creates three - dimensional art. This is accomplished by carving or modeling materials. Therefore , if someone carves or models materials to create three - dimensional art , they are a sculptor. An artist specializing in sculpture primarily creates three - dimensional works. They often use materials like stone , metal , or wood. Hence , sculptors often utilize these materials in their work
fol: (FORALL x (sculpt_person(x) IMPLIES create_dimension_art(x))) AND ( FORALL x (create_dimension_art(x) IMPLIES (carve(x, material) OR model(x, material)))) AND ( FORALL x ((carve(x, material) OR model(x, material)) IMPLIES create_dimension_art(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((carve(x, material) OR model(x, material)) AND create_dimension_art(x) IMPLIES sculpt_person(x))) AND ( FORALL x (artist(x) AND specialize_sculpture(x) IMPLIES create_dimension_work(x))) AND ( FORALL x (create_dimension_work(x) IMPLIES often_use_material(x))) AND ( FORALL x (sculpt_person(x) IMPLIES often_use_material(x)))

nl: A person who performs in a theatrical production is defined as an actor. If a person is an actor , they are known to work in either theater or film. Consequently , by applying * * Hypothetical Syllogism * * , it follows that someone performing in a theatrical production works in theater or film. Additionally , an actor performs in plays , movies , or television shows. Similarly , through * * Hypothetical Syllogism * * , a person who performs in a theatrical production also performs in plays , movies , or television shows. Finally , utilizing the rule of * * Composition * * , it can be inferred that if a person is an actor , they concurrently work in theater or film and perform in plays , movies , or television shows
fol: (FORALL x (perform_in_theater_production(x) IMPLIES act(x))) AND ( FORALL x (act(x) IMPLIES (work_in_theater(x) OR work_in_film(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (act(x) IMPLIES (perform_in_play(x) OR perform_in_movie(x) OR perform_in_television_show(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (perform_in_theater_production(x) IMPLIES (work_in_theater(x) OR work_in_film(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (perform_in_theater_production(x) IMPLIES (perform_in_play(x) OR perform_in_movie(x) OR perform_in_television_show(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (act(x) IMPLIES ((work_in_theater(x) OR work_in_film(x)) AND (perform_in_play(x) OR perform_in_movie(x) OR perform_in_television_show(x)))))

nl: A location with high annual rainfall is classified as a wet climate. A tropical climate is characterized by high temperatures , heavy rainfall , and high humidity year - round. Therefore , a city experiencing high temperatures and significant rainfall throughout the year can be classified as having a tropical climate. Furthermore , tropical climates are hot and humid , contrasting with polar climates , which are cold and have low humidity
fol: (FORALL x (location(x) AND high_rain(x) IMPLIES wet_climate(x))) AND ( FORALL x (tropic_climate(x) IMPLIES (high_temperature(x) AND heavy_rain(x) AND high_humidity(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) AND high_temperature(x) AND significant_rain(x) IMPLIES tropic_climate(x))) AND ( FORALL x (tropic_climate(x) IMPLIES (hot_temperature(x) AND high_humidity(x)))) AND ( FORALL y (pole_climate(y) IMPLIES (cold_temperature(y) AND low_humidity(y))))

nl: A sport is classified as either indoor or outdoor. If a sport is played in a closed space , it 's considered an indoor sport. This closed environment is typically a gymnasium or arena. Conversely , if a sport is played in an open field , it 's an outdoor sport. Therefore , the location determines the sport 's classification
fol: (FORALL x (sport(x) IMPLIES (play_in_close_environment(x) OR play_in_open_field(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (sport(x) AND play_in_close_environment(x) IMPLIES play_indoor(x))) AND ( FORALL x (play_in_close_environment(x) IMPLIES (gymnasium(x) OR arena(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (sport(x) AND play_in_open_field(x) IMPLIES play_outdoor(x))) AND ( FORALL x (play_indoor(x) IMPLIES NOT play_outdoor(x))) AND ( FORALL x (play_outdoor(x) IMPLIES NOT play_indoor(x)))

nl: A person who visits at least three continents is considered a world traveler
fol: (FORALL x ((person(x) AND EXISTS y EXISTS z EXISTS w (continent(y) AND continent(z) AND continent(w) AND visit(x, y) AND visit(x, z) AND visit(x, w))) IMPLIES world_travel(x)))

nl: Not every smartphone with a good camera has both a long - lasting battery and a fast processor. A high - quality smartphone should offer long battery life. It should also offer a powerful camera and a responsive user interface. However , not all smartphones have both a high - quality camera and long battery life. This implies that some smartphones lack at least one of these features. Therefore , a smartphone having a good camera does n't guarantee long battery life and a fast processor simultaneously
fol: (EXISTS x (smartphone(x) AND good_camera(x) AND NOT (long_last_battery(x) AND fast_processor(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (smartphone(x) IMPLIES (long_battery_life(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (smartphone(x) IMPLIES (powerful_camera(x) AND respond_interface(x)))) AND ( EXISTS x (smartphone(x) AND NOT (high_quality_camera(x) AND long_battery_life(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (smartphone(x) AND good_camera(x) IMPLIES NOT (long_last_battery(x) AND fast_processor(x))))

nl: A reptile with a long body , lacking legs , and possessing a forked tongue is a snake. Reptiles that are venomous and have long tails can inhabit either deserts or rainforests. Reptiles possessing a long , slender body , lacking legs , and covered in scales are typically snakes. Snakes are a type of reptile
fol: (FORALL x (reptile(x) AND long_body(x) AND lack_leg(x) AND have_fork_tongue(x) IMPLIES snake(x))) AND ( FORALL x (reptile(x) AND venomous(x) AND long_tail(x) IMPLIES (found_in_desert(x) OR found_in_rainforest(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (reptile(x) AND long_slender_body(x) AND no_leg(x) AND cover_in_scale(x) IMPLIES snake(x))) AND ( FORALL x (snake(x) IMPLIES reptile(x)))

nl: A strong password includes uppercase and lowercase letters , numbers , and special characters. This combination makes it more resistant to hacking attempts. Therefore , a password containing this mix is considered strong. A password lacking this variety is considered weak. Using a strong password is crucial for online security
fol: (FORALL x (password(x) AND contain_uppercase(x) AND contain_low(x) AND contain_number(x) AND contain_special(x) IMPLIES strong(x))) AND ( FORALL x (password(x) AND contain_uppercase(x) AND contain_low(x) AND contain_number(x) AND contain_special(x) IMPLIES more_resist_hack(x))) AND ( FORALL x (strong(x) IMPLIES crucial_online_secure(x))) AND ( FORALL x (password(x) AND NOT (contain_uppercase(x) AND contain_low(x) AND contain_number(x) AND contain_special(x)) IMPLIES weak(x)))

nl: A doctor assesses a patient 's symptoms. Based on these symptoms , the doctor can prescribe medication. Medical doctors use prescriptions to treat illnesses. Symptom management is another reason for prescribing medication. Therefore , a doctor prescribes medication to address both the underlying illness and the patient 's specific symptoms
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (doctor(x) AND patient(y) AND have_symptom(y) AND medication(z) IMPLIES prescribe(x, y, z))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (doctor(x) AND patient(y) AND ill(z) IMPLIES prescribe_medication(x, y, z))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (doctor(x) AND patient(y) AND symptom(z) AND medication(z) IMPLIES prescribe(x, y, z))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (doctor(x) AND patient(y) AND ill(z) IMPLIES treat(x, y, z)))

nl: A building cannot simultaneously have both a rooftop garden and a basement. This is because the building has either a rooftop garden or a basement , but not both. It is possible , however , for the building to have a rooftop garden without an elevator. Therefore , the presence of a rooftop garden does not necessitate the presence of an elevator
fol: (FORALL x (building(x) IMPLIES ((have_roof_garden(x) OR have_basement(x)) AND NOT (have_roof_garden(x) AND have_basement(x))))) AND ( EXISTS x (building(x) AND have_roof_garden(x) AND NOT have_elevator(x))) AND ( EXISTS x (building(x) AND have_roof_garden(x) AND NOT have_elevator(x)))

nl: A musical instrument produces sound through vibration. This vibration can occur in strings , air columns , or membranes. Instruments vibrating membranes are called membranophones. A drum is a musical instrument that produces sound by vibrating a stretched membrane. Typically , drums are played by striking them with hands or drumsticks. Therefore , a drum is a membranophone
fol: (FORALL x (music_instrument(x) IMPLIES produce_sound_through_vibration(x))) AND ( FORALL x (produce_sound_through_vibration(x) IMPLIES (vibrate_string(x) OR vibrate_air_column(x) OR vibrate_membrane(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (instrument(x) AND vibrate_membrane(x) IMPLIES membranophone(x))) AND ( FORALL x (drum(x) IMPLIES (music_instrument(x) AND produce_sound_by_vibrating_stretch_membrane(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (drum(x) IMPLIES (typically_play_by_strike_hand(x) OR typically_play_by_strike_stick(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (drum(x) IMPLIES membranophone(x)))

nl: A chef who cooks food in a professional kitchen also possesses culinary training. Therefore , it is true that a chef cooks food. In a different professional domain , a driver transports people. Likewise , a teacher is dedicated to educating students. Furthermore , if a person is a waiter , they work either in a restaurant or a café
fol: (FORALL x (chef(x) IMPLIES (cook_food(x) AND professional_kitchen(x) IMPLIES culinary_train(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (chef(x) IMPLIES cook_food(x))) AND ( FORALL y (drive(y) IMPLIES transport_person(y))) AND ( FORALL z (teach(z) IMPLIES educate(z))) AND ( FORALL x (waiter(x) IMPLIES (work_restaurant(x) OR work_cafe(x))))

nl: A well - maintained building is characterized by cleanliness , functioning utilities , and structural soundness. An eco - friendly building uses solar panels and incorporates green spaces. However , excessive waste generation contradicts its eco - friendliness. Furthermore , a building is also eco - friendly if it utilizes solar panels , has good insulation , and recycles waste
fol: (FORALL x (building(x) IMPLIES (well_maintained(x) IFF (clean(x) AND function_utility(x) AND structure_soundness(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (building(x) IMPLIES (eco_friendly(x) IFF ((solar_panel(x) AND green_space(x)) AND NOT excessive_waste(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (building(x) IMPLIES (use_solar_panel(x) AND good_insulation(x) AND recycle_waste(x) IMPLIES eco_friendly(x))))

nl: Doctors have a crucial role in healthcare. They diagnose illnesses affecting patients. As part of their diagnostic process , doctors examine patients. Following a diagnosis , doctors prescribe treatments. These treatments often include medications. Therefore , doctors diagnose patients and prescribe medications to treat their illnesses
fol: (FORALL x (doctor(x) IMPLIES crucial_role_in_healthcare(x))) AND ( FORALL x (doctor(x) IMPLIES diagnose_ill(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (doctor(x) AND patient(y) IMPLIES examine(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (doctor(x) IMPLIES prescribe_treat(x))) AND ( FORALL x (treat(x) IMPLIES include_medication(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (doctor(x) AND patient(y) IMPLIES diagnose(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (doctor(x) AND patient(y) AND ill(z) IMPLIES prescribe_medication(x, y, z)))

nl: For a room to be classified as a bedroom , it must contain at least one bed. This essential feature also makes it likely a bedroom. A room is considered a bedroom if it is primarily used for sleeping , contains a bed , and is typically found in residential buildings. Alternatively , it qualifies as a bedroom if it contains a bed and has a window
fol: (FORALL x (room(x) IMPLIES (have_bed(x) IMPLIES likely_bedroom(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (room(x) IMPLIES (bedroom(x) IMPLIES contain_bed(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (room(x) IMPLIES (use_sleep(x) IMPLIES bedroom(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (room(x) IMPLIES (primary_use_sleep(x) AND contain_bed(x) AND typical_find_building(x, residence) IMPLIES bedroom(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (room(x) IMPLIES ((EXISTS y (bed(y) AND contain(x, y))) AND have_window(x) IMPLIES bedroom(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (room(x) IMPLIES (have_bed(x) IMPLIES bedroom(x))))

nl: Mammals are known for giving birth to live young. Reptiles , on the other hand , typically lay eggs. This distinction is fundamental in the animal kingdom. However , some reptiles , like certain snakes , are exceptions. While most snakes lay eggs , some give birth to live young. Therefore , the general rule about reptiles laying eggs has notable exceptions within the species
fol: (FORALL x (mammal(x) IMPLIES give_birth_live_young(x))) AND ( FORALL y (reptile(y) IMPLIES lay_egg(y))) AND ( FORALL x (reptile(x) IMPLIES lay_egg(x))) AND ( EXISTS z (reptile(z) AND snake(z) AND give_birth_live_young(z))) AND ( FORALL s (snake(s) IMPLIES (lay_egg(s) OR give_birth_live_young(s))))

nl: An organism is either unicellular or multicellular. Crucially , it is not possible for an organism to be both unicellular and multicellular simultaneously. This means that if an organism is not unicellular , it logically follows that it is multicellular. Conversely , should an organism not be multicellular , then it must be unicellular
fol: (FORALL x (organism(x) IMPLIES (unicellular(x) OR multicellular(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (organism(x) IMPLIES NOT (unicellular(x) AND multicellular(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (organism(x) AND NOT unicellular(x) IMPLIES multicellular(x))) AND ( FORALL x (organism(x) AND NOT multicellular(x) IMPLIES unicellular(x)))

nl: A refrigerator cools food. This specific action is crucial for food preservation. In contrast , a microwave primarily heats food , and it does so quickly. It performs no cooling function ; consequently , it offers no food preservation. An oven , however , serves different culinary functions. It is designed to bake or roast food. Additionally , an oven also provides a general heating capability
fol: (FORALL x (refrigerator(x) IMPLIES cool_food(x))) AND ( FORALL x (cool_food(x) IMPLIES preserve_food(x))) AND ( FORALL y (microwave(y) IMPLIES heat_food_quick(y))) AND ( FORALL y (microwave(y) IMPLIES NOT preserve_food(y))) AND ( FORALL z (oven(z) IMPLIES (bake_food(z) OR roast_food(z)))) AND ( FORALL z (oven(z) IMPLIES heat_food(z)))

nl: An exothermic chemical reaction releases heat. This release of heat often manifests as energy in forms such as light or sound. Exothermic reactions are also characterized by a negative change in enthalpy. This means the products have lower energy than the reactants. Furthermore , a reaction must be either exothermic or endothermic to occur , provided reactants are present
fol: (FORALL x (chemical_reaction(x) AND exothermic(x) IMPLIES release_heat(x))) AND ( FORALL x (release_heat(x) IMPLIES release_energy_heat(x))) AND ( FORALL x (exothermic(x) IMPLIES negative_enthalpy_change(x))) AND ( FORALL x (negative_enthalpy_change(x) IMPLIES low_energy_product(x))) AND ( FORALL x (chemical_reaction(x) AND react_present(x) IMPLIES (exothermic(x) OR endothermic(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (release_energy_heat(x) IMPLIES (release_light(x) OR release_sound(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (chemical_reaction(x) AND release_heat(x) AND negative_enthalpy_change(x) IMPLIES exothermic(x)))

nl: A film belongs to the science fiction genre if it features imaginative content. This content often relates to concepts of space , time , or advanced technology. For instance , films featuring futuristic technology directly qualify for this genre. Similarly , those that explore extraterrestrial life are also classified as science fiction. Additionally , a movie can be considered science fiction if it takes place in an alternate reality. Finally , any film based on imaginative scientific concepts inherently fits within this genre
fol: (FORALL x (film(x) AND feature_imaginative_content(x) IMPLIES science_fiction_genre(x))) AND ( FORALL x (feature_imaginative_content(x) IMPLIES (relate_to_space(x) OR relate_to_time(x) OR relate_to_advanced_technology(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (film(x) AND feature_futuristic_technology(x) IMPLIES science_fiction_genre(x))) AND ( FORALL x (film(x) AND explore_extraterrestrial_life(x) IMPLIES science_fiction_genre(x))) AND ( FORALL x (movie(x) AND take_place_alternate_reality(x) IMPLIES science_fiction_genre(x))) AND ( FORALL x (film(x) AND base_on_imaginative_science_concept(x) IMPLIES science_fiction_genre(x)))

nl: A smartphone offers a satisfying user experience if it has a responsive touchscreen , long battery life , and fast processing. However , security is also paramount. A smartphone is not secure if it lacks encryption or has weak passwords. A fingerprint scanner alone does not guarantee security. Therefore , a truly satisfying smartphone experience requires both usability and robust security features
fol: (FORALL x (smartphone(x) AND respond_touchscreen(x) AND long_battery_life(x) AND fast_process(x) IMPLIES satisfy_experience(x))) AND ( FORALL x (smartphone(x) IMPLIES (NOT secure(x) IMPLIES (NOT encrypt(x) OR weak_password(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (finger_scan(x) IMPLIES NOT guarantee_security(x))) AND ( FORALL x (satisfy_experience(x) IMPLIES (usability(x) AND robust_security_feature(x))))

nl: A computer program crashes when it encounters an error it cannot handle. This means the program cannot continue to function. If a program produces an incorrect output or crashes unexpectedly , it has a bug. Therefore , if a computer program encounters an error , it likely has a bug. A software application can be classified as a video game if it involves user interaction , offers a virtual environment , and incorporates a scoring or progress system
fol: (FORALL x (computer_program(x) AND encounter_error(x) IMPLIES crash(x))) AND ( FORALL x (computer_program(x) AND crash(x) IMPLIES NOT continue_function(x))) AND ( FORALL x (computer_program(x) AND (incorrect_output(x) OR crash_surprise(x)) IMPLIES have_bug(x))) AND ( FORALL x (computer_program(x) AND encounter_error(x) IMPLIES have_bug(x))) AND ( FORALL x (software_application(x) AND use_interact(x) AND virtual_environment(x) AND score_progress_system(x) IMPLIES video_game(x)))

nl: Predators hunt to survive. If a predator needs food and a prey is within reach , then the predator hunts the prey. Therefore , a predator hunts its prey because it needs food. The prey , in turn , relies on escape or defense mechanisms. This constant interaction shapes the survival strategies of both predator and prey
fol: (FORALL x (predator(x) IMPLIES need_survive(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (predator(x) AND need_food(x) AND prey(y) AND in_reach(y, x) IMPLIES hunt(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (predator(x) AND hunt(x, y) AND need_food(x) IMPLIES hunt(x, y))) AND ( FORALL y (prey(y) IMPLIES (rely_escape(y) OR rely_defend_mechanism(y)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y ((predator(x) AND prey(y)) IMPLIES interact_shape_strategy(x, y)))

nl: A plant is classified as a succulent if it has thick , fleshy leaves or stems that store water. Succulents can survive in arid environments. Because they are succulents , they store water in their leaves , stems , or roots. Therefore , a plant can be characterized as a succulent when it stores water in its leaves or stems and thrives in arid environments. Furthermore , succulents exhibit a thick , fleshy appearance. Consequently , a plant is a succulent if it stores water in its leaves and can survive in arid environments
fol: (FORALL x (plant(x) AND (thick_leaf(x) OR thick_stem(x)) AND store_water(x) IMPLIES succulent(x))) AND ( FORALL x (succulent(x) IMPLIES survive_arid_environment(x))) AND ( FORALL x (succulent(x) IMPLIES store_water_leaf_stem_root(x))) AND ( FORALL x (plant(x) AND store_water_leaf_stem(x) AND survive_arid_environment(x) IMPLIES succulent(x))) AND ( FORALL x (succulent(x) IMPLIES thick_fleshy_appearance(x))) AND ( FORALL x (plant(x) AND store_water_leaf(x) AND survive_arid_environment(x) IMPLIES succulent(x)))

nl: If a person is a pilot , they fly airplanes. If a person is a pilot , they also navigate and operate aircraft. Every airplane has a pilot and a copilot. Hence , if an aircraft is an airplane , it has a pilot. A pilot can fly planes , and if a person is a pilot , then they can fly airplanes or helicopters. Therefore , pilots must fly and navigate aircraft
fol: (FORALL x (person(x) AND pilot(x) IMPLIES fly_plane(x))) AND ( FORALL x (person(x) AND pilot(x) IMPLIES (navigate_aircraft(x) AND operate_aircraft(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (airplane(x) IMPLIES (have_pilot(x) AND have_pilot(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (aircraft(x) AND airplane(x) IMPLIES have_pilot(x))) AND ( FORALL x (pilot(x) IMPLIES can_fly_plane(x))) AND ( FORALL x (pilot(x) IMPLIES (fly_plane(x) OR fly_helicopter(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (pilot(x) IMPLIES (fly_aircraft(x) AND navigate_aircraft(x))))

nl: Musical instruments create sound. This sound arises from vibration or resonance ( Material Implication ). More precisely , a musical instrument produces sound through the vibration of a resonating body. If an instrument generates vibrations that create a specific auditory sensation , it produces a specific sound ( Modus Ponens ). Therefore , when an instrument generates sound through vibration or resonance , it produces music
fol: (FORALL x (instrument(x) IMPLIES produce_sound(x))) AND ( FORALL x (produce_sound(x) IMPLIES (vibration(x) OR resonance(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (instrument(x) AND resonance_body(x) IMPLIES produce_sound_vibration(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (instrument(x) AND generate_vibration(x) AND create_auditory_sensation(x, y) IMPLIES specific_sound(y))) AND ( FORALL x ((generate_sound_vibration(x) OR generate_sound_resonance(x)) IMPLIES produce_music(x))) AND ( FORALL x (instrument(x) AND (vibration(x) OR resonance(x)) IMPLIES produce_music(x)))

nl: Artificial intelligence systems process large amounts of data. They learn patterns and make decisions based on this input. To perform these functions effectively , AI systems require robust data input processing capabilities and advanced learning algorithms. A computer algorithm that processes large datasets , adapts to new data , and makes accurate predictions is defined as a machine learning algorithm. Consequently , the learning components within AI systems are powered by machine learning algorithms. These systems continuously improve their performance , and this improvement occurs as they learn from incoming data
fol: (FORALL x (artificial_intelligence_system(x) IMPLIES (process_large_amounts_data(x) AND learn_patterns(x) AND make_decisions_base_input(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (artificial_intelligence_system(x) IMPLIES (require_robust_data_input_process(x) AND require_advanced_learn_algorithms(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (computer_algorithm(x) AND process_large_datasets(x) AND adapt_new_data(x) AND make_accurate_predictions(x) IMPLIES machine_learning_algorithm(x))) AND ( FORALL x (artificial_intelligence_system(x) IMPLIES power_machine_learning_algorithms(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (artificial_intelligence_system(x) AND incoming_data(y) AND learn(x, y) IMPLIES improve_perform(x)))

nl: A bicycle has two wheels. A car has four wheels. A skateboard has four wheels. A scooter has two or three wheels. Since a cyclist can either ride a bicycle or a unicycle , we use this to derive what happens next. If the cyclist rides a bicycle , they use two wheels. Otherwise , if they use a unicycle , the amount of wheels used is not two
fol: (FORALL x (bicycle(x) IMPLIES have_wheel(x, 2))) AND ( FORALL x (car(x) IMPLIES have_wheel(x, 4))) AND ( FORALL x (skateboard(x) IMPLIES have_wheel(x, 4))) AND ( FORALL x (scooter(x) IMPLIES (have_wheel(x, 2) OR have_wheel(x, 3)))) AND ( FORALL x (person(x) IMPLIES (ride(x, bicycle) OR ride(x, unicycle)))) AND ( FORALL x ((person(x) AND ride(x, bicycle)) IMPLIES use_wheel(x, 2))) AND ( FORALL x ((person(x) AND ride(x, unicycle)) IMPLIES NOT use_wheel(x, 2)))

nl: A computer 's performance hinges on several key factors. Sufficient memory is crucial for smooth operation. A fast processor ensures efficient execution of tasks. Furthermore , an optimized and up - to - date operating system contributes significantly to overall efficiency. When these components – memory , processor , and operating system – function correctly , the computer runs efficiently. Keeping the system free from malware and ensuring sufficient storage also contribute to optimal performance. Therefore , a combination of adequate resources and system maintenance results in a smoothly running and efficient computer
fol: (FORALL x (computer_system(x) IMPLIES (ample_memory(x) IMPLIES run_efficient(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (computer_system(x) IMPLIES (powerful_processor(x) IMPLIES run_efficient(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (computer_system(x) IMPLIES (optimize_system(x) IMPLIES run_efficient(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (computer_system(x) IMPLIES (adequate_resource(x) AND system_maintain(x) IMPLIES run_efficient(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (computer_system(x) IMPLIES (free_malware(x) IMPLIES optimal_perform(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (computer_system(x) IMPLIES (suffice_storage(x) IMPLIES optimal_perform(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (computer_system(x) IMPLIES (optimal_perform(x) IFF (run_smooth(x) AND run_efficient(x)))))

nl: A volcano is dormant if it has n't erupted in the past years. Let 's denote " a volcano is dormant " as D , and " a volcano has erupted in the past years " as E. We can represent this as ( ¬E → D ) via contraposition which is ( ¬D → E ). Volcanoes are active , dormant , or extinct , but not more than one at the same time. If a volcano is not dormant ( ¬D ) , then it must have erupted in the past years ( E )
fol: (FORALL x (volcano(x) AND NOT erupt_year(x) IMPLIES dormant(x))) AND ( FORALL x (volcano(x) IMPLIES ((activity(x) OR dormant(x) OR extinct(x)) AND NOT (activity(x) AND dormant(x)) AND NOT (activity(x) AND extinct(x)) AND NOT (dormant(x) AND extinct(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (volcano(x) AND NOT dormant(x) IMPLIES erupt_year(x)))

nl: Bicycles have two wheels. Unicycles , in contrast , have only one wheel and are therefore not bicycles. Tricycles have three wheels , and cars have four. These examples illustrate the varying numbers of wheels on different vehicles. A skateboard , unlike the others , also has four wheels
fol: (FORALL x (bicycle(x) IMPLIES have_wheel(x, 2))) AND ( FORALL y (unicycle(y) IMPLIES have_wheel(y, 1) AND NOT bicycle(y))) AND ( FORALL z (tricycle(z) IMPLIES have_wheel(z, 3))) AND ( FORALL w (car(w) IMPLIES have_wheel(w, 4))) AND ( FORALL s (skateboard(s) IMPLIES have_wheel(s, 4)))

nl: A planet orbits a star. A planet is a large , spherical celestial body. Planets do not produce their own light. Instead , they emit light due to reflected sunlight. Therefore , a planet is an object in space with a fixed orbit around a star that is spherical and reflects light
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (planet(x) AND star(y) IMPLIES orbit(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (planet(x) IMPLIES (large(x) AND sphere_body(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (planet(x) IMPLIES NOT produce_light(x))) AND ( FORALL x (planet(x) IMPLIES emit_light(x))) AND ( FORALL x (planet(x) IMPLIES reflect_sunlight(x))) AND ( FORALL x (planet(x) IMPLIES (object_space(x) AND fix_orbit_star(x) AND sphere(x) AND reflect_light(x))))

nl: A car 's operation involves fuel consumption and exhaust gas emissions. Fuel consumption depends on the car 's design and manufacturer recommendations. If a car 's engine is designed for fuel type Y and the manufacturer recommends Y , then the car consumes fuel Y. Furthermore , fuel consumption leads to emissions. If a car consumes fuel type Y , and the combustion of Y results in emission Z , then the car produces emission Z
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (car(x) IMPLIES (consume(x, y) IFF fuel(y)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (car(x) IMPLIES (emit(x, y) IFF exhaust_gas(y)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (car(x) AND fuel_type(y) AND engine_design(x, y) AND manufacture_recommend(x, y) IMPLIES consume(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (car(x) AND fuel_type(y) AND consume(x, y) AND combust_produce(y, z) IMPLIES emit(x, z))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (consume(x, y) IMPLIES emit(x, y)))

nl: A durable material resists wear and tear. Durability also implies withstanding physical stress and maintaining structural integrity. Strength in a material means resisting pressure and wear. Therefore , a durable material is also a strong material. Some materials possess both durability and lightweight properties. Consequently , only these lightweight and durable materials are truly suitable for outdoor applications
fol: (FORALL x (durable_material(x) IMPLIES resist_wear_tear(x))) AND ( FORALL x (material(x) AND (resist_pressure(x) AND resist_wear(x)) IMPLIES strong(x))) AND ( FORALL x (material(x) AND withstand_physical_stress(x) AND maintain_structure_integrity(x) IMPLIES durable_material(x))) AND ( EXISTS x (material(x) AND durable_material(x) AND lightweight(x))) AND ( FORALL x (durable_material(x) IMPLIES strong(x))) AND ( FORALL x (suitable_outdoor_use(x) IMPLIES (material(x) AND lightweight(x) AND durable_material(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((lightweight(x) AND durable_material(x)) IMPLIES suitable_outdoor_use(x)))

nl: Antibiotics are effective against bacteria. Antibiotics are ineffective against viruses. This means an antibiotic does not treat viral infections. A medication can treat bacterial infections or viral infections , but not both. An antibiotic treats bacterial infections , therefore , it does not treat viral infections. An antiviral drug treats viral infections. An antibiotic is not an antiviral drug , due to their opposite functions
fol: (FORALL x (antibiotic(x) IMPLIES effective_against_bacteria(x))) AND ( FORALL x (antibiotic(x) IMPLIES NOT effective_against_virus(x))) AND ( FORALL x (antibiotic(x) IMPLIES NOT treat_virus_infect(x))) AND ( FORALL x (medication(x) IMPLIES (treat_bacteria_infect(x) OR treat_virus_infect(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (treat_bacteria_infect(x) IMPLIES NOT treat_virus_infect(x))) AND ( FORALL x (antibiotic(x) IMPLIES treat_bacteria_infect(x))) AND ( FORALL x (drug_virus(x) IMPLIES treat_virus_infect(x))) AND ( FORALL x (antibiotic(x) IMPLIES NOT drug_virus(x)))

nl: A computer is either a desktop or a laptop. It cannot be both simultaneously. If it is a laptop , it will have either an Intel , AMD , or ARM processor. However , a laptop cannot contain multiple processor types
fol: (FORALL x (computer(x) IMPLIES (desktop(x) OR laptop(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (computer(x) IMPLIES NOT (desktop(x) AND laptop(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (laptop(x) IMPLIES (processor(x) OR processor(x) OR processor(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (laptop(x) IMPLIES NOT ((processor(x) AND processor(x)) OR (processor(x) AND processor(x)) OR (processor(x) AND processor(x)))))

nl: Evaporation transforms a liquid into a vapor. This occurs when molecules gain enough energy to overcome attractive forces. Therefore , a liquid changing to a vapor involves energy. The freezing point represents when a substance transitions from a liquid to a solid. Hence , the freezing point marks a distinct phase change
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y ((liquid(x) AND vapor(y) AND evaporate(x) AND molecule_gain_energy(x) AND overcome_force_attract(x)) IMPLIES transform_to_vapor(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y ((substance(x) AND freeze_point(y) AND liquid(x) AND solid(x)) IMPLIES change_at_temperature(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x ((liquid(x) AND transform_to_vapor(x)) IMPLIES involve_energy(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((freeze_point(x)) IMPLIES distinct_phase_change(x)))

nl: To qualify for a promotion , an employee needs either excellent performance and good communication skills , or completion of a management training course. Eligibility also depends on excellent performance coupled with a specific tenure. Promotion itself hinges on outstanding performance or extended service , lasting at least 5 years. Exceeding sales targets can lead to either a bonus or a promotion. A bonus is granted when performance targets are met or exceeded , combined with at least one year of employment. Thus , performance and experience are key factors in career advancement
fol: (FORALL x (qualify_promote(x) IFF ((excellent_perform(x) AND good_communicate_skill(x)) OR complete_manage_train(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (employ(x) AND excellent_perform(x) AND temporal_tenure(x) IMPLIES promote(x))) AND ( FORALL x (employ(x) AND (outstanding_perform(x) OR tenure(x)) IMPLIES promote(x))) AND ( FORALL x (employ(x) AND exceed_sell_target(x) IMPLIES (bonus(x) OR promote(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (employ(x) AND meet_perform_target(x) AND tenure(x) IMPLIES bonus(x)))

nl: People living in cities or the countryside either work or are retired. Employment and unemployment are mutually exclusive possibilities. Retirement is also a possibility alongside employment and unemployment. Therefore , if someone lives in a city or the countryside , they must be either employed , unemployed , or retired
fol: (FORALL x (person(x) AND (live_city(x) OR live_countryside(x)) IMPLIES (work(x) OR retire(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (person(x) IMPLIES (employ(x) OR NOT employ(x) OR retire(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (employ(x) IMPLIES NOT employ(x))) AND ( FORALL x (NOT employ(x) IMPLIES NOT employ(x))) AND ( FORALL x (person(x) AND (live_city(x) OR live_countryside(x)) IMPLIES (employ(x) OR NOT employ(x) OR retire(x))))

nl: Airports are essential for air travel. They facilitate the arrival , departure , and transfer of passengers. Airports provide runways for takeoff and landing. Furthermore , they offer terminals for passengers and infrastructure for cargo transportation. These terminals often include gates and amenities like shopping and dining. Therefore , airports comprehensively facilitate air travel
fol: (FORALL x (airport(x) IMPLIES (essential_for_air_travel(x) AND facilitate_arrive(x) AND facilitate_depart(x) AND facilitate_transfer(x)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (airport(x) AND runway(y) IMPLIES provide_runway_take_land(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (airport(x) AND terminal(y) IMPLIES provide_terminal_passenger(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (airport(x) IMPLIES provide_infrastructure_cargo_transport(x))) AND ( FORALL x (terminal(x) IMPLIES (include_gate(x) AND has_shop_dine_amenity(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (airport(x) IMPLIES comprehensive_facilitate_air_travel(x)))

nl: A material is biodegradable if it can be decomposed by living organisms , such as bacteria or fungi. This decomposition process breaks the material down into simpler substances. Biodegradable materials naturally return nutrients to the environment as they decompose. Consequently , biodegradable materials have a reduced impact on waste accumulation. Therefore , a biodegradable material is one that decomposes naturally and integrates back into the environment
fol: (FORALL x (material(x) AND decompose_by_organism(x) IMPLIES biodegradable(x))) AND ( FORALL x (material(x) AND break_down_by_organism(x) AND involve_bacteria(x) AND involve_fungus(x) IMPLIES decompose_by_organism(x))) AND ( FORALL x (biodegradable(x) IMPLIES (break_down_natural(x) AND return_nutrient(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (material(x) AND biodegradable(x) IMPLIES reduce_impact_waste(x))) AND ( FORALL x (material(x) AND break_down_natural(x) AND return_nutrient(x) IMPLIES integrate_environment(x))) AND ( FORALL x (material(x) AND can_decompose_by_organism(x) IMPLIES biodegradable(x))) AND ( FORALL x (substance(x) AND break_down_by_live_organism(x) IMPLIES biodegradable(x)))

nl: A city with a beach cannot have ski resorts or deserts. Cities with ski resorts also lack deserts. If a city has no mountains and no beach , it is either a desert or a forest city. A city must have a beach , mountains , or both. Therefore , a destination is a beach if it is neither a mountain nor a city , applying Modus Ponens and Disjunctive Syllogism
fol: (FORALL x (city(x) AND have_beach(x) IMPLIES (NOT have_ski_resort(x) AND NOT have_desert(x)))) AND ( FORALL y (city(y) AND have_ski_resort(y) IMPLIES NOT have_desert(y))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) AND NOT have_mountain(x) AND NOT have_beach(x) IMPLIES (desert_city(x) OR forest_city(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) IMPLIES (have_beach(x) OR have_mountain(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (destination(x) AND NOT have_mountain(x) AND NOT city(x) IMPLIES have_beach(x)))

nl: Deciduous trees lose their leaves annually. This shedding often occurs in autumn. If a tree sheds its leaves in autumn , it is deciduous. Conversely , evergreen trees retain their leaves year - round. Therefore , a plant is an evergreen if it retains its leaves throughout the year. Trees are either deciduous or evergreen , but not both. Trees with leaves that change color in autumn are typically deciduous
fol: (FORALL x (deciduous_tree(x) IMPLIES lose_leaf_year(x))) AND ( FORALL x (deciduous_tree(x) IMPLIES shed_leaf_autumn(x))) AND ( FORALL x (shed_leaf_autumn(x) IMPLIES deciduous_tree(x))) AND ( FORALL x (evergreen_tree(x) IMPLIES retain_leaf_year(x))) AND ( FORALL x (plant(x) AND retain_leaf_year(x) IMPLIES evergreen_tree(x))) AND ( FORALL x (tree(x) IMPLIES (deciduous_tree(x) OR evergreen_tree(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (tree(x) IMPLIES NOT (deciduous_tree(x) AND evergreen_tree(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (tree(x) AND have_leaf(x) AND change_color_autumn(x) IMPLIES deciduous_tree(x)))

nl: A secure password must meet several criteria. It needs at least one uppercase and one lowercase letter. Additionally , a secure password must include at least one number. Finally , a secure password must be at least eight characters long. A password lacking any of these features is not secure. For a password to be considered strong , it should also contain special characters. Therefore , combining uppercase and lowercase letters , numbers , and special characters creates a highly secure password
fol: (FORALL x (password(x) IMPLIES (contain_uppercase(x) AND contain_low(x) AND contain_number(x) AND long_at_least(x, 8) IMPLIES secure(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (password(x) AND NOT secure(x) IMPLIES (NOT contain_uppercase(x) OR NOT contain_low(x) OR NOT contain_number(x) OR NOT long_at_least(x, 8)))) AND ( FORALL x (password(x) AND contain_special(x) IMPLIES strong(x))) AND ( FORALL x (password(x) AND (contain_uppercase(x) AND contain_low(x) AND contain_number(x) AND contain_special(x)) IMPLIES high_secure(x)))

nl: A mountain 's defining characteristic is its height. Its elevation is above sea level. If a mountain 's elevation exceeds a certain threshold and it 's not a hill , then it 's considered tall. Specifically , a mountain is tall if its height is above a certain number of meters. A mountain is taller than a hill. Therefore , a mountain has a higher elevation than a hill. Mountains can also be compared by their elevation ; the one with the higher elevation is the taller mountain
fol: (FORALL x (mountain(x) IMPLIES high(x))) AND ( FORALL x (mountain(x) IMPLIES elevation_above_sea_level(x))) AND ( FORALL x (mountain(x) IMPLIES (exceed_threshold(x) IMPLIES tall(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (mountain(x) AND exceed_threshold(x) AND NOT hill(x) IMPLIES tall(x))) AND ( FORALL x (mountain(x) AND high_above_meter(x) IMPLIES tall(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (mountain(x) AND hill(y) IMPLIES tall_than(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (mountain(x) AND hill(y) IMPLIES higher_elevation(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (mountain(x) AND mountain(y) AND higher_elevation(x, y) IMPLIES tall_than(x, y)))

nl: A refrigerator maintains a cold temperature. This cold temperature slows down bacterial growth. Therefore , the refrigerator prevents the growth of bacteria. Refrigerators also delay food spoilage. Since food spoilage is caused by bacterial growth , preventing bacterial growth also prevents spoilage. Thus , a refrigerator preserves perishable food items. Fridges are used to store food at low temperatures. These low temperatures are maintained to extend the shelf life of the food. Hence , refrigerators are crucial for food preservation
fol: (FORALL x (refrigerator(x) IMPLIES maintain_cold_temperature(x))) AND ( FORALL x (cold_temperature(x) IMPLIES slow_grow_bacteria(x))) AND ( FORALL x (refrigerator(x) IMPLIES prevent_grow_bacteria(x))) AND ( FORALL x (refrigerator(x) IMPLIES delay_food_spoil(x))) AND ( FORALL x (food_spoil(x) IMPLIES cause_grow_bacteria(x))) AND ( FORALL x (prevent_grow_bacteria(x) IMPLIES prevent_food_spoil(x))) AND ( FORALL x (refrigerator(x) IMPLIES preserve_perishable_food_item(x))) AND ( FORALL x (refrigerator(x) IMPLIES store_food_low_temperature(x))) AND ( FORALL x (low_temperature(x) IMPLIES extend_shelf_life_food(x))) AND ( FORALL x (refrigerator(x) IMPLIES crucial_food_preserve(x)))

nl: Wind turbines capture wind energy. They convert this wind energy into electrical energy. Therefore , wind turbines generate electricity. This electricity is created by converting the kinetic energy of the wind. Because wind turbines convert wind energy into electricity , the resulting power is considered renewable energy. Unlike a wind turbine , a windmill converts wind energy into mechanical energy
fol: (FORALL x (turbine_wind(x) IMPLIES capture_wind_energy(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (turbine_wind(x) AND capture_wind_energy(x) IMPLIES convert_electricity_energy(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (turbine_wind(x) IMPLIES generate_electricity(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (electricity(x) AND kinetic_energy(y) AND wind(y) IMPLIES create_convert(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (turbine_wind(x) AND convert_wind_energy_electricity(x, y) IMPLIES renew_energy(y))) AND ( FORALL x (windmill(x) IMPLIES convert_wind_energy_mechanics(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (convert_wind_energy_mechanics(x) IMPLIES NOT turbine_wind(x)))

nl: A prism has two parallel and congruent bases connected by parallelogram faces. A pyramid has a polygonal base and triangular faces meeting at a single apex. A structure with a square base and four triangular faces is a pyramid. Therefore , if an object has a polygonal base and triangular faces that meet at a common vertex , it is a pyramid ( Universal Instantiation ). A basketball is round. A cube is square. A pyramid has triangular faces. Thus , a ball is round , and a pyramid has triangular faces ( Composition )
fol: (FORALL x (prism(x) IFF (two_parallel_congruent_base(x) AND parallelogram_face(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (pyramid(x) IFF (polygonal_base(x) AND triangular_face(x) AND meet_at_common_vertex(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((structure(x) AND square_base(x) AND four_triangular_face(x)) IMPLIES pyramid(x))) AND ( round(basketball)) AND ( square(cube)) AND ( triangular_face(pyramid)) AND ( round(ball) AND triangular_face(pyramid))

nl: A company with a global presence and innovative technologies is often a major player. This is especially true if they manufacture vehicles. Efficient supply chain management is crucial for success. It integrates technology , streamlined logistics , and strong supplier relationships. These elements optimize operations and minimize costs. Ultimately , this leads to improved customer satisfaction. Similarly , a company specializing in electronics with a global reach is likely a major electronics corporation
fol: (FORALL x (company(x) AND globe_present(x) AND innovate_technology(x) IMPLIES major_play(x))) AND ( FORALL x (company(x) AND globe_present(x) AND innovate_technology(x) AND manufacture_vehicle(x) IMPLIES major_vehicle(x))) AND ( FORALL x (efficient_supply_chain_manage(x) IMPLIES (integrate_technology(x) AND streamline_logistics(x) AND strong_supply_relation(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (efficient_supply_chain_manage(x) IMPLIES (optimize_operate(x) AND minimize_cost(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((optimize_operate(x) AND minimize_cost(x)) IMPLIES improve_customer_satisfy(x))) AND ( FORALL x (company(x) AND specialize_manufacture_electronics(x) AND globe_present(x) IMPLIES major_electronics_corporation(x)))

nl: A construction project requires planning , coordination , and resources. Materials and labor are examples of such resources. If a person is a builder , then they construct houses or commercial buildings. Therefore , a builder engages in construction. A person who assesses property value is called an appraiser. Appraisers often deal with the value of buildings
fol: (FORALL x (project_construct(x) IMPLIES (require_plan(x) AND require_coordinate(x) AND require_resource(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((material(x) OR labor(x)) IMPLIES resource(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((person(x) AND build(x)) IMPLIES (construct_house(x) OR construct_commerce(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((person(x) AND build(x)) IMPLIES engage_construct(x))) AND ( FORALL x (value_property(x) IMPLIES appraise(x))) AND ( FORALL x (appraise(x) IMPLIES deal_value_building(x)))

nl: A musical instrument can produce sound through strings , air , or percussion. A piano is a musical instrument. A piano creates sound by striking strings with hammers. Therefore , a piano produces sound through strings. Guitars are also musical instruments. Guitars are played by plucking or strumming strings. Instruments like guitars and pianos can be played solo or in an ensemble. Thus , these instruments are often used for entertainment , self - expression , or cultural activities
fol: (FORALL x (music_instrument(x) IMPLIES produce_sound(x))) AND ( FORALL x (piano(x) IMPLIES music_instrument(x))) AND ( FORALL x (piano(x) IMPLIES produce_sound_strike_string(x))) AND ( FORALL x (guitar(x) IMPLIES music_instrument(x))) AND ( FORALL x (guitar(x) IMPLIES play_pluck_strum(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((piano(x) OR guitar(x)) IMPLIES (play_solo(x) OR play_ensemble(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((piano(x) OR guitar(x)) IMPLIES (entertain_use(x) OR self_express_use(x) OR culture_use(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (music_instrument(x) IMPLIES (produce_sound_string(x) OR produce_sound_air(x) OR produce_sound_percussion(x))))

nl: Flowers attract pollinators using their colors and scents. This attraction helps the plant reproduce through pollen transfer. If a flower produces a scent * or * displays vibrant colors * and * the garden provides a suitable habitat , it attracts pollinators. A flower attracting pollinators through color and scent is called a pollinator - friendly flower. Therefore , a pollinator - friendly flower attracts pollinating insects , aiding in pollination
fol: (FORALL x (flower(x) IMPLIES (produce_smell(x) OR display_vibrant_color(x) IMPLIES attract_pollen(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (flower(x) AND attract_pollen_by_color(x) AND attract_pollen_by_smell(x) IMPLIES pollen_friendly(x))) AND ( FORALL x (pollen_friendly(x) IMPLIES (flower(x) AND attract_pollen_insect(x) AND help_reproduce(x)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (flower(x) AND pollen(y) IMPLIES attract(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (attract(x, y) IMPLIES help_reproduce(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (flower(x) AND (produce_smell(x) OR display_vibrant_color(x)) AND suitable_habitat(x) IMPLIES attract_pollen(x)))

nl: A beverage is refreshing if it is cold , non - alcoholic , and quenches thirst. Alternatively , a beverage is refreshing if it 's cold , not too sweet , non - alcoholic , and also quenches thirst. Quenching thirst requires the beverage to be cold and non - alcoholic. Therefore , coldness and the absence of alcohol are essential for both quenching thirst and contributing to a refreshing quality
fol: (FORALL x (beverage(x) IMPLIES (refresh(x) IFF (cold(x) AND NOT alcoholic(x) AND quench_thirst(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (beverage(x) IMPLIES ((cold(x) AND NOT too_sweet(x) AND quench_thirst(x) AND NOT alcoholic(x)) IMPLIES refresh(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (beverage(x) IMPLIES ((cold(x) AND NOT alcoholic(x)) IMPLIES quench_thirst(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (beverage(x) IMPLIES (quench_thirst(x) IMPLIES (cold(x) AND NOT alcoholic(x)))))

nl: A library offers various resources to its patrons. These resources include books , periodicals , and digital materials. Patrons can borrow these items or use them within the library. Libraries also provide access to digital resources. A student , for example , reads a book in the library
fol: (FORALL x (library(x) IMPLIES (provide_book(x) AND provide_periodical(x) AND provide_digital_material(x)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (patron(y) AND have_resource(x) IMPLIES (borrow(y,x) OR use_within_library(y,x)))) AND ( FORALL x (library(x) IMPLIES provide_access_to_digital_resource(x))) AND ( EXISTS x EXISTS y EXISTS z (patron(x) AND book(y) AND library(z) AND read(x, y, z)))

nl: An engine powers a vehicle if it converts energy into mechanical force that propels the vehicle. A car engine combusts fuel in the presence of oxygen , generating heat and mechanical energy. This mechanical energy propels the vehicle forward. Consequently , a car engine powers the car. Therefore , if an engine uses the force generated by the expansion of heated gas to propel a vehicle , it is classified as a rocket engine. Thus , a rocket engine is an engine
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (engine(x) AND vehicle(y) AND convert_energy(x) AND propel_vehicle(x, y) IMPLIES power(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (car_engine(x) IMPLIES (combust_fuel(x, y) AND fuel(y) AND present_oxygen(x) AND generate_heat(x) AND generate_mechanics(x) AND propel_forward(x,y)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (engine(x) AND vehicle(y) AND use_force_gas(x) AND propel_forward(x, y) IMPLIES rocket_engine(x))) AND ( FORALL x (rocket_engine(x) IMPLIES engine(x)))

nl: Detectives investigate crimes. Their investigations involve gathering evidence and interviewing witnesses at crime scenes. This process aims to solve the case. A person who investigates and solves crimes is called a detective. Judges , on the other hand , make legal decisions. Detectives find clues to solve mysteries. Therefore , detective work is crucial for uncovering the truth in criminal cases
fol: (FORALL x (detective(x) IMPLIES investigate_crime(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (detective(x) AND crime(y) IMPLIES investigate(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (investigate(x, y) IMPLIES gather_evidence(x) AND interview_witness(x))) AND ( FORALL x (investigate_and_solve_crime(x) IMPLIES detective(x))) AND ( FORALL x (detective(x) IMPLIES find_clue(x))) AND ( FORALL x (judge(x) IMPLIES make_legal_decision(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (detective(x) AND mystery(y) IMPLIES solve(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (detective_work(x) IMPLIES crucial_for_uncover_truth(x)))

nl: Objects with lower density than water float. If an object has a density greater than water , it sinks. Therefore , an object either floats or sinks , depending on its density relative to water. Restrained objects that are less dense than water may not immediately float , but generally will
fol: (FORALL x (object(x) AND EXISTS y (water(y) AND density_less(x, y)) IMPLIES float(x))) AND ( FORALL x (object(x) AND density_great(x, water) IMPLIES sink_water(x))) AND ( FORALL x (object(x) IMPLIES (density_less(x, water) OR density_great(x, water)))) AND ( FORALL x (object(x) AND restrain(x) AND density_less(x, water) IMPLIES NOT immediate_float(x))) AND ( FORALL x (object(x) AND density_less(x, water) IMPLIES general_float(x)))

nl: A device is considered wireless if it transmits information without physical wires or cables. Computer networks utilize both wired and wireless connections. Therefore , an electronic device can communicate with another device using either a wired or wireless connection. Wireless devices offer a convenient alternative to wired connections within a network. The choice between wired and wireless depends on factors such as distance , speed requirements , and security considerations
fol: (FORALL x (device(x) AND transmit_information(x) IMPLIES wireless(x))) AND ( FORALL x (computer_network(x) IMPLIES (exists_wire_connection(x) AND exists_wireless_connection(x)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (electronic_device(x) AND electronic_device(y) IMPLIES (exists_wire_connection(x, y) OR exists_wireless_connection(x, y) IMPLIES communicate(x, y)))) AND ( FORALL x (wireless(x) IMPLIES convenient_alternative(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (choice_between(x, y) IMPLIES (depend(x, distance) AND depend(x, speed_require) AND depend(x, security_consider))))

nl: If the weather is sunny and the location is by the beach , then a vacation is enjoyable. If the weather is sunny and the location is in the mountains , a vacation is also enjoyable. Therefore , if the weather is sunny and the location is either by the beach or in the mountains , a vacation is enjoyable. A vacation is enjoyable if the weather is sunny and the location has beautiful scenery or exciting activities. If the weather is not sunny or the accommodations are unsatisfactory , then a vacation is not enjoyable. If the weather is sunny , and the location is by the beach , then the trip is enjoyable. A trip is more enjoyable if it involves visiting new places or meeting interesting people
fol: (FORALL x ((sunny_weather(x) AND beach_location(x)) IMPLIES enjoy(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((sunny_weather(x) AND mountain_location(x)) IMPLIES enjoy(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((sunny_weather(x) AND (beach_location(x) OR mountain_location(x))) IMPLIES enjoy(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((sunny_weather(x) AND (beautiful_scenery(x) OR excite_activity(x))) IMPLIES enjoy(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((NOT sunny_weather(x) OR NOT satisfactory_accommodation(x)) IMPLIES NOT enjoy(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((sunny_weather(x) AND beach_location(x)) IMPLIES trip(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((visit_new_place(x) OR meet_interest_person(x)) IMPLIES more_enjoy(x)))

nl: A scientist studies a specific field and works at a research institute. To be considered a scientist , one must conduct research. The ultimate goal is to expand knowledge and understanding within their specific field of expertise. Therefore , a scientist conducts research and performs experiments to expand knowledge

nl: A diet leads to weight loss if it results in reduced calorie intake and regular exercise. An effective weight - loss diet contains low - calorie foods and promotes regular exercise. Therefore , reduced calorie intake and regular exercise are key to an effective diet. A balanced diet promotes overall health and wellbeing. Eating a balanced diet also contributes to maintaining a healthy body weight. Healthy food helps in maintaining a balanced diet. nl: Both rain and snow are recognized forms of precipitation. Rain is specifically defined as liquid precipitation. In contrast , snow is classified as solid precipitation. fol: (FORALL x (city(x) AND low_crime_rate(x) AND well_light_street(x) IMPLIES safe(x))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) AND low_crime_rate(x) AND clean_environment(x) AND access_quality_healthcare(x) IMPLIES (livable(x) AND safe(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) AND low_crime_rate(x) AND clean_environment(x) AND access_quality_healthcare(x) IMPLIES safe(x))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) AND safe(x) AND low_crime_rate(x) AND clean_environment(x) AND access_quality_healthcare(x) IMPLIES livable(x))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) AND afford_house(x) AND access_quality_educate(x) AND low_crime_rate(x) IMPLIES livable(x))) AND ( FORALL x (livable(x) IMPLIES (city(x) AND low_crime_rate(x) AND good_job_opportunity(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (NOT ideal_live(x) IMPLIES (city(x) AND high_crime_rate(x))))

nl: A book can be classified as either fiction or nonfiction. Nonfiction books are based on factual information and real events. Conversely , a book is considered fiction if it is * not * based on real events or facts. Thus , fiction relies on imagination and does not represent actual facts. Therefore , the basis of the story determines the genre
fol: (FORALL x (book(x) IMPLIES (fiction(x) OR non_fiction(x)))) AND ( FORALL y (non_fiction(y) IMPLIES (base_on_fact(y) AND real_event(y)))) AND ( FORALL z (fiction(z) IMPLIES (NOT real_event(z) AND NOT base_on_fact(z)))) AND ( FORALL w (fiction(w) IMPLIES (imagine_base(w) AND NOT represent_fact(w)))) AND ( FORALL u (book(u) IMPLIES (base_of_story(u) IMPLIES determine_genre(u))))

nl: Escarpments are natural landforms characterized by steep , rocky cliffs. These cliffs are formed by the erosion of softer rock layers beneath harder ones. A plateau is a landform with a flat or gently sloping surface elevated above the surrounding area. Plateaus are often found in arid regions. Mountains are landforms that rise significantly above their surroundings , featuring steep slopes and a relatively confined summit
fol: (FORALL x ((natural_landform(x) AND steep_rock_cliff(x)) IMPLIES escarp(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((escarp(x) AND erode_layer(x, y, z) AND soft_rock(y) AND hard_rock(z)) IMPLIES form_by_erode(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((landform(x) AND flat_or_gentle_surface(x) AND elevate_above_surround_area(x)) IMPLIES plateau(x))) AND ( FORALL x (plateau(x) IMPLIES find_in_arid_region(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((landform(x) AND rise_significant(x) AND have_steep_slope(x) AND confine_summit(x)) IMPLIES mountain(x)))

nl: Plants need sunlight , water , and nutrients to grow. These are essential resources for their survival. If a plant receives adequate sunlight , water , and nutrients , it grows well. Healthy growth leads to the production of flowers or fruits. Therefore , providing these resources ensures a plant 's health and vitality. Plants require nutrients to maintain their health based on specific biological needs

nl: A building is a museum if it houses and displays art , historical artifacts , or scientific objects for public viewing and education. A building is also a museum if it is dedicated to the collection , preservation , and display of objects of historical , artistic , or scientific interest. Another criterion is that a building is a museum if it houses and displays collections of artifacts , is open to the public , and serves educational or cultural purposes. A building is considered a museum if it houses and displays collections of objects of artistic , historical , or scientific interest. Therefore , if a building fulfills any of these conditions , then that building is a museum
fol: (FORALL x ((building(x) AND house_display(x, art, history, science) AND public_view(x)) IMPLIES museum(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((building(x) AND house_collect(x) AND open_public(x) AND serve_culture(x)) IMPLIES museum(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((building(x) AND dedicate_collect(x) AND dedicate_preserve(x) AND dedicate_display(x) AND (history_interest(x) OR art_interest(x) OR science_interest(x))) IMPLIES museum(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((building(x) AND house_collect(x) AND display_collect(x) AND art_interest(x)) IMPLIES museum(x))) AND ( FORALL x (((building(x) AND house_display(x, art, history, science) AND public_view(x)) OR (building(x) AND house_collect(x) AND open_public(x) AND serve_culture(x)) OR (building(x) AND dedicate_collect(x) AND dedicate_preserve(x) AND dedicate_display(x) AND (history_interest(x) OR art_interest(x) OR science_interest(x))) OR (building(x) AND house_collect(x) AND display_collect(x) AND art_interest(x))) IMPLIES museum(x)))

nl: Electric vehicles ( EVs ) use electricity for propulsion. They are recharged through an external power source. EVs emit no tailpipe emissions , reducing air pollution. Because they use only electricity , they produce no emissions. EVs contribute to reduced greenhouse gas emissions. Furthermore , an EV with autonomous driving , an electric powertrain , and efficient energy consumption promotes a cleaner , safer transportation system. EVs rely on battery storage for energy. An EV with a long range and fast charging appeals to consumers. Therefore , electric vehicles play a vital role in reducing carbon emissions and promoting sustainable transportation
fol: (FORALL x (electric_vehicle(x) IMPLIES (use_electricity_for_propel(x) AND recharge_external_power_source(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (electric_vehicle(x) IMPLIES no_tailpipe_emit(x))) AND ( FORALL x (electric_vehicle(x) IMPLIES reduce_air_pollute(x))) AND ( FORALL x (electric_vehicle(x) IMPLIES no_emit(x))) AND ( FORALL x (electric_vehicle(x) IMPLIES reduce_greenhouse_gas_emit(x))) AND ( FORALL x (electric_vehicle(x) AND autonomy_drive(x) AND electric_powertrain(x) AND efficient_energy_consume(x) IMPLIES contribute_clean_safe_transport(x))) AND ( FORALL x (electric_vehicle(x) IMPLIES rely_battery_store(x))) AND ( FORALL x (electric_vehicle(x) AND long_range(x) AND fast_charge(x) IMPLIES appeal_consume(x))) AND ( FORALL x (electric_vehicle(x) IMPLIES play_vital_role_reduce_carbon_emit(x))) AND ( FORALL x (electric_vehicle(x) IMPLIES promote_sustain_transport(x)))

nl: A lawyer is defined as someone who provides legal advice and represents clients in court. Therefore , representing clients and providing legal advice are essential characteristics of a lawyer. This representation includes handling legal matters such as negotiation , drafting documents , and litigation. An attorney is also a term used to describe someone who represents clients in legal disputes and offers advice. In essence , a lawyer practices law by representing clients and advising them on legal matters
fol: (FORALL x ((provide_legal_advise(x) AND represent_client_court(x)) IMPLIES lawyer(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((person(x) AND provide_legal_advise(x) AND represent_client_court(x)) IMPLIES lawyer(x))) AND ( FORALL x (lawyer(x) IMPLIES (represent_client(x) AND provide_legal_advise(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (lawyer(x) IMPLIES handle_legal_matter(x))) AND ( FORALL x (attorney(x) IMPLIES represent_client_legal_dispute_and_provide_legal_advise(x))) AND ( FORALL x (lawyer(x) IFF (represent_client(x) AND advise_client_legal_matter(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (practice_law(x) IMPLIES (represent_client(x) AND advise_client_legal_matter(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (handle_legal_matter(x) IMPLIES (negotiate(x) OR draft_document(x) OR sue(x))))

nl: A smartphone operates in distinct audio modes. These modes include silent , vibrate , and ring. The phone cannot be in multiple modes simultaneously. If the phone receives a call and is not in silent mode , it rings. Conversely , a phone is in silent mode if it produces no sound for incoming calls or messages. Therefore , the phone is either silent or has sound enabled. Thus , the phone can be either in silent mode or ringing
fol: (FORALL x (smartphone(x) IMPLIES ((silent(x) OR vibrate(x) OR ring(x)) AND NOT (silent(x) AND vibrate(x)) AND NOT (silent(x) AND ring(x)) AND NOT (vibrate(x) AND ring(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (smartphone(x) AND receive_call(x) AND NOT silent(x) IMPLIES ring(x))) AND ( FORALL x (smartphone(x) AND no_sound_incoming_calls(x) AND no_sound_incoming_messages(x) IMPLIES silent(x))) AND ( FORALL x (smartphone(x) IMPLIES (silent(x) OR sound_enable(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (smartphone(x) IMPLIES (silent(x) OR ring(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (smartphone(x) AND no_sound_produce(x) IMPLIES silent(x)))

nl: A movie cannot be considered a blockbuster if it has poor reviews. Therefore , it follows that any blockbuster must possess positive reviews. This positive review status is a necessary attribute for a blockbuster. Beyond this , specific criteria allow a movie to achieve blockbuster status. One such criterion is having a large budget and a famous director , alongside positive reviews. Another pathway involves a non - indie film that garners either large box office revenue or critical acclaim , also coupled with positive reviews. A third condition , remarkably simpler , is met if a movie achieves high box office revenue combined with positive reviews. In summary , a movie is a blockbuster if it consistently receives positive reviews and fulfills any of these specified conditions
fol: (FORALL x (movie(x) IMPLIES (blockbuster(x) IMPLIES NOT poor_review(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (blockbuster(x) IMPLIES positive_review(x))) AND ( FORALL x (movie(x) AND large_budget(x) AND famous_director(x) AND positive_review(x) IMPLIES blockbuster(x))) AND ( FORALL x (movie(x) AND NOT indie_film(x) AND (large_box_office_revenue(x) OR critical_acclaim(x)) AND positive_review(x) IMPLIES blockbuster(x))) AND ( FORALL x (movie(x) AND high_box_office_revenue(x) AND positive_review(x) IMPLIES blockbuster(x))) AND ( FORALL x (blockbuster(x) IFF (positive_review(x) AND ((large_budget(x) AND famous_director(x)) OR (NOT indie_film(x) AND (large_box_office_revenue(x) OR critical_acclaim(x))) OR high_box_office_revenue(x)))))

nl: A park is defined as a public outdoor area designed for recreation. It 's also a private outdoor area designated for recreation , leisure , or conservation. A park is not an industrial zone. Public parks typically feature greenery , play areas , or walking paths. Therefore , a place is considered a park if it is a public outdoor area for recreation and relaxation , enjoyment , or conservation
fol: (FORALL x ((park(x) IFF public_outdoor_area(x) AND designate_recreation(x)) OR (park(x) IFF private_outdoor_area(x) AND (designate_recreation(x) OR designate_leisure(x) OR designate_conserve(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (park(x) IMPLIES NOT industry_zone(x))) AND ( FORALL x (public_park(x) IMPLIES (vegetation(x) OR play_area(x) OR walk_path(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (place(x) AND public_outdoor_area(x) AND recreation(x) AND relax(x) AND enjoy(x) AND conserve(x) IMPLIES park(x)))

nl: A substance is categorized as an acid if it donates protons , maintains a pH level below seven , and reacts with bases to form salts. Conversely , a substance is a base when it reacts with an acid , producing salt and water. This means bases are also characterized by their ability to neutralize acids and form salts. In contrast to these reactive substances , an inert substance exhibits no such chemical activity. Specifically , an inert substance does not react with acids , nor does it react with bases
fol: (FORALL x (substance(x) AND (NOT react_with_acid(x) AND NOT react_with_base(x)) IMPLIES inert(x))) AND ( FORALL x (substance(x) AND (EXISTS y (acid(y) AND react(x, y, salt, water)) IMPLIES base(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (substance(x) AND (donate_proton(x) AND ph_level_below(x, 7) AND react_with_base_to_form_salt(x)) IMPLIES acid(x))) AND ( FORALL x (substance(x) AND react_with_acid_to_form_salt_and_water(x) IMPLIES base(x))) AND ( FORALL x (base(x) IMPLIES (neutralize_acid(x) AND form_salt(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (substance(x) AND acid(x) IFF (ph_level_below(x, 7) AND donate_proton(x))))

nl: A bird can either fly or swim , but not both. Some birds can swim and dive underwater. A bird that can swim has webbed feet and is adapted to living in aquatic environments. These swimming birds are , therefore , waterfowl. A bird that has waterproof feathers , webbed feet , and dives underwater to catch fish is a diving bird. The inability to both fly and swim simultaneously applies to these specific waterfowl and diving birds
fol: (FORALL x (bird(x) IMPLIES (fly(x) OR swim(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (bird(x) IMPLIES NOT (fly(x) AND swim(x)))) AND ( EXISTS x (bird(x) AND swim(x) AND dive_underwater(x))) AND ( FORALL x (bird(x) AND swim(x) IMPLIES (have_web_foot(x) AND adapt_water_environment(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (bird(x) AND swim(x) AND have_web_foot(x) AND adapt_water_environment(x) IMPLIES waterfowl(x))) AND ( FORALL x (bird(x) AND have_waterproof_feather(x) AND have_web_foot(x) AND dive_underwater_catch_fish(x) IMPLIES dive_bird(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((waterfowl(x) OR dive_bird(x)) IMPLIES NOT (fly(x) AND swim(x))))

nl: A fish swims in water. No fish can survive without water. Therefore , if a fish exists , it needs water to survive. The statement " a fish swims in water or is caught by a fisherman " implies that a fish 's survival depends on being in water. Since no fish can survive in boiling water , boiling water can not be a habitat for fish. Certain fish , like salmon and trout , swim upstream to spawn in freshwater habitats. Since the fish needs water , the fish can be in the sea or ocean. However , the fish can not live in both places at once
fol: (FORALL x (fish(x) IMPLIES swim_in_water(x))) AND ( FORALL x (fish(x) IMPLIES survive_without_water(x))) AND ( FORALL x (fish(x) IMPLIES need_water_to_survive(x))) AND ( FORALL x (fish(x) IMPLIES (swim_in_water(x) OR catch_by_person(x) IMPLIES survival_depend_on_water(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (fish(x) AND survive_boil_water(x) IMPLIES false)) AND ( FORALL x (boil_water(x) AND habitat_for_fish(x) IMPLIES false)) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y ((fish(x) AND (salmon(x) OR trout(x)) AND freshwater_habitat(y)) IMPLIES swim_upstream_to_spawn(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (fish(x) IMPLIES need_water_to_survive(x))) AND ( FORALL x (fish(x) IMPLIES (be_in_sea(x) OR be_in_ocean(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (fish(x) AND be_in_sea(x) AND be_in_ocean(x) IMPLIES false))

nl: Silk is smooth , wool is warm , and leather is durable. Clothing materials include cotton , silk , or synthetic fabrics. If a fabric is not synthetic , it is cotton , linen , or silk. A fabric is either natural or synthetic. If a fabric is synthetic , it is not made of cotton , silk , or wool. Natural fabrics can be made into clothing items. Therefore , since silk is a natural fabric , silk can be made into clothing items

nl: A tree provides shade. If a tree provides shade , it is tall and has many leaves. Shade is created when a tree has dense foliage and is exposed to sunlight. A large canopy and direct sunlight also enable a tree to provide shade. Furthermore , a tree with a large canopy , height , and abundant leaves provides shade. Therefore , trees with dense foliage and exposure to sunlight , as well as tall trees with large canopies and many leaves , are suitable for providing shade in a park
