nl: A food item is unhealthy if it is high in sugar or high in fat. If a food item contains high amounts of sugar or fat , it is considered unhealthy. Conversely , if a food item contains essential nutrients and has a low calorie count , it is deemed healthy. Therefore , a food item is healthy if it is not high in sugar or fat. This implies that food items that are not naturally high in nutrients can be deemed healthy. A food item is high in antioxidants if it contains compounds that neutralize free radicals. Additionally , a food item is suitable for a healthy diet if it 's low in calories , high in nutrients , and has a long shelf life. Therefore , a meal is considered healthy if it includes fruits , vegetables , and lean proteins
fol: (FORALL x (food_item(x) AND (high_sugar(x) OR high_fat(x)) IMPLIES healthy(x))) AND ( FORALL x (food_item(x) AND essential_nutrient(x) AND low_calorie_count(x) IMPLIES healthy(x))) AND ( FORALL x (food_item(x) AND contain_compound(x, y) AND neutralize_radical(y) IMPLIES high_antioxidant(x))) AND ( FORALL x (food_item(x) AND low_calorie(x) AND high_nutrient(x) AND long_shelf_life(x) IMPLIES suitable_healthy_diet(x))) AND ( FORALL x (healthy_meal(x) IMPLIES (contain_fruit(x) AND contain_vegetables(x) AND contain_lean_protein(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((contain_fruit(x) AND contain_vegetables(x) AND contain_lean_protein(x)) IMPLIES healthy_meal(x))) AND ( FORALL x (food_item(x) AND NOT (high_sugar(x) OR high_fat(x)) IMPLIES healthy(x)))

nl: A company issues stocks to raise capital. This capital is then used to finance operations or investments. When a company issues shares of stock for sale to the general public , it goes public. nl: Test results are either positive or negative , but not both , and similarly , confirmed or unconfirmed , but not both. Bank accounts can have positive , negative , or zero balances. A non - zero number is either positive or negative , but not both. Negation is a mathematical operation that reverses a number 's sign. A number can only be one of the following : positive , negative , or zero. Therefore , if a number is negative , it is not positive
fol: (FORALL x (test_result(x) IMPLIES ((positive(x) OR negative(x)) AND NOT (positive(x) AND negative(x)) AND (confirm(x) OR unconfirm(x)) AND NOT (confirm(x) AND unconfirm(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (bank_account(x) IMPLIES (positive_balance(x) OR negative_balance(x) OR zero_balance(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((number(x) AND NOT zero(x)) IMPLIES (positive(x) OR negative(x)) AND NOT (positive(x) AND negative(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((mathematics_operate(x) AND reverse_sign(x)) IMPLIES negation(x))) AND ( FORALL x (number(x) IMPLIES (positive(x) OR negative(x) OR zero(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((number(x) AND negative(x)) IMPLIES NOT positive(x)))

nl: Birds can fly. Fish can swim. These aerial and aquatic movements are linked to specific physical characteristics. Indeed , birds possess wings , which directly enable their flight. Similarly , fish possess fins , allowing them to swim effectively in water. Furthermore , mammals typically move by walking or running. Lions and horses , for example , demonstrate this ability by running. However , not all animal movement fits these primary categories. Spiders distinctly navigate by crawling. Conversely , snails are characterized by their very slow pace
fol: (FORALL x (bird(x) IMPLIES fly(x))) AND ( FORALL y (fish(y) IMPLIES swim(y))) AND ( FORALL x (bird(x) IMPLIES wing(x))) AND ( FORALL y (fish(y) IMPLIES fin(y))) AND ( FORALL z (mammal(z) IMPLIES (walk(z) OR run(z)))) AND ( FORALL z (lion(z) IMPLIES run(z))) AND ( FORALL w (horse(w) IMPLIES run(w))) AND ( FORALL s (spider(s) IMPLIES crawl(s))) AND ( FORALL n (snail(n) IMPLIES move_slow(n)))

nl: Scientists typically dedicate themselves to a single primary field of study. This implies that while a scientist may specialize in physics , chemistry , or biology , they cannot simultaneously specialize in any combination of two or all three of these disciplines. Accordingly , individuals recognized as chemists , physicists , or biologists are all classified under the broader term of scientists. Aligning with these focused areas of expertise , a specific research center concentrates on diverse scientific disciplines , notably biology , chemistry , and physics. This center further extends its reach through international collaborations , engaging with institutions from countries such as the United States , China , and Germany
fol: (FORALL x (scientist(x) IMPLIES (specialize_biology(x) OR specialize_chemistry(x) OR specialize_physics(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((chemist(x) OR physicist(x) OR biologist(x)) IMPLIES scientist(x))) AND ( FORALL x (scientist(x) IMPLIES NOT ((specialize_physics(x) AND specialize_chemistry(x)) OR (specialize_physics(x) AND specialize_biology(x)) OR (specialize_chemistry(x) AND specialize_biology(x))))) AND ( EXISTS x (research_center(x) AND (focus_on(x, biology_discipline) AND focus_on(x, chemistry_discipline) AND focus_on(x, physics_discipline)))) AND ( EXISTS x (research_center(x) AND EXISTS y (institution(y) AND (country(y, "unitedstates") OR country(y, "china") OR country(y, "germany")) AND collaborate(x, y))))

nl: A successful business maintains customer satisfaction. This success also generates profit. Furthermore , successful businesses innovate to stay competitive. A successful business strategy generates growth and increases market share. This strategy also improves customer satisfaction. The success of a business depends on customer satisfaction. It also relies on employee engagement and strong financial performance. A company goes bankrupt if it is unable to pay its debts. Bankruptcy also results from a negative net worth. Finally , failing to generate sufficient revenue leads to bankruptcy. Therefore , customer satisfaction and financial stability are crucial for business survival

nl: A vaccine is effective if it prevents illness or reduces the severity of symptoms. If a vaccine is effective , it prevents infection , has minimal side effects , and provides long - term protection. Furthermore , a vaccine is effective against a virus if it provides immunity and has minimal side effects. Because a vaccine provides immunity if it 's effective and administered according to guidelines , the vaccine must be administered correctly. A vaccine undergoes successful clinical trials , meets safety standards , and is deemed effective by health authorities for approval. Therefore , to be approved for use , a vaccine must also be effective against a virus if it has undergone successful clinical trials and is approved by health authorities
fol: (FORALL x (vaccine(x) AND (prevent_ill(x) OR reduce_symptom_severe(x)) IMPLIES effective(x))) AND ( FORALL x (vaccine(x) AND effective(x) IMPLIES (prevent_infect(x) AND minimal_side_effect(x) AND long_term_protect(x)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (vaccine(x) AND virus(y) AND provide_immunity(x, y) AND minimal_side_effect(x) IMPLIES effective_against(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (vaccine(x) AND effective(x) AND conform_guideline(x, y) IMPLIES provide_immunity(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (vaccine(x) AND effective(x) AND conform_guideline(x, y) IMPLIES correct_administer(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (vaccine(x) AND succeed_clinic_try(x) AND meet_safe_standard(x) AND deem_effective(x) IMPLIES approve_use(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (vaccine(x) AND succeed_clinic_try(x) AND approve_authority(y, x) IMPLIES effective_against_virus(x)))

nl: A laptop 's portability depends on several factors. First , a lightweight design is crucial. Second , a long battery life contributes to portability. Third , a compact design enhances its ease of carrying. However , if a laptop is too fragile or too large , it negates its portability , even with lightweight design and long battery life. Therefore , a truly portable laptop is lightweight , durable , compact , and possesses a long - lasting battery
fol: (FORALL x (laptop(x) IMPLIES (lightweight_design(x) IMPLIES portable(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (laptop(x) IMPLIES (long_battery_life(x) IMPLIES portable(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (laptop(x) IMPLIES (compact_design(x) IMPLIES portable(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (laptop(x) IMPLIES ((fragile(x) OR too_large(x)) IMPLIES NOT portable(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (laptop(x) IMPLIES (portable(x) IFF (lightweight(x) AND durable(x) AND compact(x) AND long_lasting_battery(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (laptop(x) IMPLIES (lightweight(x) IFF (light(x) AND easy_to_carry(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (laptop(x) IMPLIES (light(x) AND short_battery_life(x) IMPLIES portable(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (laptop(x) IMPLIES (portable(x) IMPLIES (lightweight(x) AND long_battery_life(x)))))

nl: Rivers that do n't flow into lakes , other rivers , or inland seas flow into the ocean. River * x * flows through countries * y * and * z * if it originates in * y * , passes through * z * , and both share its water. If a city has a river , it possesses bridges and riverbanks. Consider a specific river flowing into a specific lake. Therefore , that lake does not flow into that river
fol: (FORALL x (river(x) AND NOT flow_into_lake(x) AND NOT flow_into_river(x) AND NOT flow_into_inland_sea(x) IMPLIES flow_into_ocean(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (river(x) AND country(y) AND country(z) AND originate_in(x, y) AND pass_through(x, z) AND share_water(x, y, z) IMPLIES flow_through(x, y, z))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) AND have_river(x) IMPLIES (have_bridge(x) AND have_bank(x)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (river(x) AND lake(y) AND flow_into(x, y) IMPLIES NOT flow_into(y, x)))

nl: A film with engaging storytelling , strong acting , and appealing cinematography often garners critical acclaim. Critical acclaim also results from a compelling story , outstanding performances , and remarkable cinematography that avoids clichés. Furthermore , a thought - provoking storyline , impressive cinematography , and a strong cast can lead to critical acclaim. Therefore , multiple combinations of elements can contribute to a film 's success with critics. These elements often include compelling narratives and high - quality production values
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (film(x) AND engage_story(y) AND strong_perform(z) AND appeal_cinematography(x) AND element(x, y, z) IMPLIES often_critical_applaud(x))) AND ( FORALL x (film(x) AND compel_story(x) AND outstanding_perform(x) AND remarkable_cinematography(x) AND avoid_cliche(x) IMPLIES critical_applaud(x))) AND ( FORALL x (film(x) AND think_story(x) AND impress_cinematography(x) AND strong_cast(x) IMPLIES critical_applaud(x))) AND ( EXISTS x (multiple_combine_element(x) IMPLIES contribute_film_succeed_critic(x))) AND ( FORALL x (compel_narrative(x) IMPLIES high_quality_value(x)))

nl: Not every computer is a laptop , meaning some computers are not laptops. Some computers are either powerful or have large storage. Laptops are portable computers. Desktops are stationary computers. Both laptops and desktops are types of computers. However , portability differentiates them : laptops are portable , desktops are not. Therefore , computers exhibit variety in type , power , and storage capacity
fol: (EXISTS x (computer(x) AND NOT laptop(x))) AND ( EXISTS y (computer(y) AND (powerful(y) OR large_storage_capacity(y)))) AND ( FORALL x (laptop(x) IMPLIES portable_computer(x))) AND ( FORALL y (desktop(y) IMPLIES stationary_computer(y))) AND ( FORALL x ((laptop(x) OR desktop(x)) IMPLIES computer(x))) AND ( FORALL x (laptop(x) IMPLIES portable(x))) AND ( FORALL y (desktop(y) IMPLIES NOT portable(y))) AND ( EXISTS z (computer(z) AND (type_variety(z) OR power_variety(z) OR storage_variety(z))))

nl: A coat is warm if it is made of wool or has a thick lining. A coat keeps one warm. Using Modus Ponens , we can infer that either a coat is made of wool or has a thick lining. A type of clothing is a coat if it is an outer garment worn for warmth or protection against the weather and has sleeves. Therefore , a coat is an outer garment worn for warmth or protection against the weather and has sleeves. A jacket keeps a person warm if it is made of insulating material and fits well. A jacket can be waterproof and not insulated or it can be waterresistant and insulated. A coat provides insulation
fol: (FORALL x ((coat(x) AND keep_warm(x)) IMPLIES (make_wool(x) OR thick_lining(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (outer_garment(x) AND wear_warm(x) AND have_sleeve(x) IMPLIES coat(x))) AND ( FORALL x (coat(x) IMPLIES (outer_garment(x) AND wear_warm(x) AND have_sleeve(x)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y ((jacket(x) AND person(y) AND make_insulate(x) AND fit_well(x, y)) IMPLIES keep_warm(x, y))) AND ( EXISTS x (jacket(x) AND ((waterproof(x) AND NOT insulate(x)) OR (water_resist(x) AND insulate(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (coat(x) IMPLIES keep_warm(x))) AND ( FORALL x (coat(x) IMPLIES provide_insulate(x)))

nl: Apples and oranges are types of fruit. If a fruit is an apple or an orange , it is considered a popular fruit. If a fruit is an apple or an orange , it is also considered a common fruit. A fruit is either an apple or an orange unless it is a banana. Oranges are citrus fruits , apples are pomaceous fruits , and bananas are berries. A fruit that is not red but has seeds could be a pear , a green apple , or a yellow pepper. A fruit that is typically red , round , and has a core with seeds is an apple
fol: (FORALL x ((apple(x) OR orange(x)) IMPLIES fruit(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((fruit(x) AND (apple(x) OR orange(x))) IMPLIES popular_fruit(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((fruit(x) AND (apple(x) OR orange(x))) IMPLIES common_fruit(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((fruit(x) AND NOT banana(x)) IMPLIES (apple(x) OR orange(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (orange(x) IMPLIES citrus_fruit(x))) AND ( FORALL x (apple(x) IMPLIES pomaceous_fruit(x))) AND ( FORALL x (banana(x) IMPLIES berry(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((fruit(x) AND NOT red(x) AND seed(x)) IMPLIES (pear(x) OR green_apple(x) OR yellow_pepper(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((fruit(x) AND red(x) AND round(x) AND have_core_with_seed(x)) IMPLIES apple(x)))

nl: A therapist is someone who provides treatment to help individuals with mental , emotional , or physical issues. Physical therapists help patients recover from injuries and improve their physical abilities. Occupational therapists help clients develop , recover , or maintain daily living and work skills. Speech therapists provide therapy to individuals with speech and language disorders. Furthermore , therapists , in general , help individuals cope with and overcome personal challenges. A psychologist specializes in the diagnosis and treatment of mental health disorders , providing support and guidance. Therefore , all these professions fall under the broader category of therapists
fol: (FORALL x (person(x) AND provide_therapy(x) AND (help_mind_issue(x) OR help_emotion_issue(x) OR help_physical_issue(x)) IMPLIES therapist(x))) AND ( FORALL x (person(x) AND help_injure_recover(x) AND improve_physical_skill(x) IMPLIES physical_therapist(x))) AND ( FORALL x (person(x) AND help_client_develop_skill(x) AND help_client_recover_work_skill(x) IMPLIES occupation_therapist(x))) AND ( FORALL x (provide_therapy_speak_language_disorder(x) IMPLIES speak_therapist(x))) AND ( FORALL x (therapist(x) IMPLIES help_individual_cope_challenge(x))) AND ( FORALL x (person(x) AND specialize_diagnose(x) AND specialize_treat(x) AND treat_mind_health_disorder(x) AND provide_support(x) AND provide_guide(x) IMPLIES psychologist(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((physical_therapist(x) OR occupation_therapist(x) OR speak_therapist(x) OR psychologist(x)) IMPLIES therapist(x)))

nl: Airports provide essential transportation services. They connect cities via both domestic and international flights. Multiple airlines , including domestic , international , and low - cost carriers , operate within airports. These airlines are supported by ground crew members. Furthermore , airports offer amenities to travelers. These amenities include shopping , dining , and lounges. Thus , airports are complex hubs that facilitate travel and provide services
fol: (FORALL x (airport(x) IMPLIES essential_transport(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (airport(x) AND city(y) IMPLIES connect(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (domestic_fly(x) OR international_fly(x) IMPLIES connect(airport, x))) AND ( FORALL x (domestic_airline(x) OR international_airline(x) OR low_cost_carrier(x) IMPLIES operate_within(x, airport))) AND ( FORALL x (airline(x) IMPLIES support_by(x, ground_crew_member))) AND ( FORALL x (airport(x) IMPLIES offer_amenity(x))) AND ( FORALL x (shop(x) OR dine(x) OR lounge(x) IMPLIES amenity(x))) AND ( FORALL x (airport(x) IMPLIES complex_hub(x) AND facilitate_travel(x) AND provide_serve(x)))

nl: A concert venue hosts live music events. To enter , a person needs a valid ticket. This ticket grants entry to the venue and the enjoyment of the live performance. Concerts at the venue feature live music from performers. If a band is booked and the venue has equipment , the band performs. A concert that features both local and international artists promotes cultural exchange. If the venue has adequate facilities and safety compliance , it can host a music festival
fol: (FORALL x (concert_venue(x) IMPLIES host_live_music(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (person(x) AND have_valid_ticket(x, y) IMPLIES can_enter(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (concert_ticket(x) AND have(y, x) IMPLIES (venue_enter(y) AND enjoy_live_music(y)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (concert_venue(x) AND concert(y) AND feature_live_music(y) IMPLIES host(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (band(x) AND venue(y) AND book(x, y) AND have_equipment(y) IMPLIES perform(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (concert(x) AND feature_local_and_international_artist(x) IMPLIES promote_culture_exchange(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (concert_venue(x) AND music_festival(y) AND have_adequate_facility(x) AND comply_safe(x) IMPLIES host_festival(x, y)))

nl: An object ∃in one of three states : solid , liquid , or gas. No object can simultaneously exist in more than one state. Therefore , a substance can be either solid or liquid , but not both at once. If a substance is not a solid , then it is either a liquid or a gas. However , not all materials are solely solid or liquid. This implies the existence of gaseous materials
fol: (FORALL x (object(x) IMPLIES (solid(x) OR liquid(x) OR gas(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((solid(x) AND liquid(x)) IMPLIES NOT object(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((solid(x) AND gas(x)) IMPLIES NOT object(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((liquid(x) AND gas(x)) IMPLIES NOT object(x))) AND ( FORALL x (substance(x) IMPLIES ((solid(x) OR liquid(x)) AND NOT (solid(x) AND liquid(x))))) AND ( FORALL x ((substance(x) AND NOT solid(x)) IMPLIES (liquid(x) OR gas(x)))) AND ( EXISTS x (material(x) AND NOT (solid(x) OR liquid(x)))) AND ( EXISTS x (material(x) AND gas(x)))

nl: A substance is defined as a liquid if it possesses a fixed volume but no fixed shape. Consequently , any liquid will always take the exact shape of its container. A container is identified as a bottle if it is constructed from glass or plastic , featuring a narrow neck and a wide base. Such containers are typically used for storing liquids. Because a container meeting these criteria is a bottle , it will inherently possess a cap. Moreover , it is designed specifically to hold liquid. However , not every container capable of holding liquid is necessarily a bottle. If a container holds liquid but is not a bottle , it could be a cup. Alternatively , it might be a pitcher. Furthermore , such a container could also be a jug
fol: (FORALL x (substance(x) IMPLIES (liquid(x) IFF (fix_volume(x) AND no_fix_shape(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (liquid(x) IMPLIES take_shape_of_container(x))) AND ( FORALL x (container(x) IMPLIES (bottle(x) IFF (made_of_glass_or_plastic(x) AND narrow_neck(x) AND wide_base(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (bottle(x) IMPLIES (has_cap(x) AND can_hold_liquid(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((container(x) AND hold_liquid(x) AND NOT bottle(x)) IMPLIES (cup(x) OR pitcher(x) OR jug(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((container(x) AND hold_liquid(x) AND NOT bottle(x)) IMPLIES (cup(x) OR pitcher(x) OR jug(x)))) AND ( EXISTS x ((container(x) AND hold_liquid(x) AND NOT bottle(x))))

nl: A photograph captures a moment. If a photograph captures a moment , it can be printed on paper or displayed on a screen. A camera captures images with a specific resolution and sensor type. A camera with a higher resolution captures more detailed images. Therefore , images captured by a camera with a higher resolution are more detailed. A photograph is high quality if it has good resolution and accurate color representation. The images captured by a camera can be in color or black and white. Thus , the final photograph can be both printed or displayed , and can be in high quality
fol: (FORALL x (photograph(x) IMPLIES capture_moment(x))) AND ( FORALL x (photograph(x) AND capture_moment(x) IMPLIES (print_paper(x) OR display_screen(x)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (camera(x) AND resolution(y) AND sensor_type(z) IMPLIES capture_image(x, y, z))) AND ( FORALL x (camera(x) AND high_resolution(x) IMPLIES capture_more_detail(x))) AND ( FORALL x (image(x) AND capture_high_resolution(x) IMPLIES more_detail(x))) AND ( FORALL x (photograph(x) AND good_resolution(x) AND accurate_color_represent(x) IMPLIES high_quality(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (camera(x) AND image(y) AND capture(x, y) IMPLIES (in_color(y) OR in_black_and_white(y)))) AND ( FORALL x (photograph(x) IMPLIES (print_paper(x) OR display_screen(x)) AND high_quality(x)))

nl: A book is engaging if it has a captivating plot , relatable characters , and a well - paced narrative. A book also is not engaging if it has a confusing storyline and lacks character development. Therefore , a book is engaging only if it has a captivating plot , relatable characters , and a well - paced narrative. A novel can be enthralling if it features a captivating plot , well - developed characters , and evocative language. A novel is engaging if it has a compelling plot , well - developed characters , and an immersive setting. If a book is engaging with a compelling narrative and well - developed characters , then it appeals to readers interested in fiction , nonfiction , or memoirs. Consequently , a novel with a compelling plot , well - developed characters , and evocative language can be enthralling
fol: (FORALL x ((book(x) AND captivate_plot(x) AND relate_character(x) AND well_pace(x)) IMPLIES engage(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((book(x) AND confuse_storyline(x) AND lack_character_develop(x)) IMPLIES NOT engage(x))) AND ( FORALL x (engage(x) IMPLIES (captivate_plot(x) AND relate_character(x) AND well_pace(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((novel(x) AND captivate_plot(x) AND well_develop_character(x) AND evoke_language(x)) IMPLIES enthrall(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((novel(x) AND compel_plot(x) AND well_develop_character(x) AND immersive_setting(x)) IMPLIES engage(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y ((book(x) AND engage(x) AND compel_narrative(x) AND well_develop_character(x) AND person(y) AND (interest_fiction(y) OR interest_non_fiction(y) OR interest_memoir(y))) IMPLIES appeal(y, x))) AND ( FORALL x ((novel(x) AND compel_plot(x) AND well_develop_character(x) AND evoke_language(x)) IMPLIES enthrall(x)))

nl: Trains facilitate passenger transport between stations. For a train to depart , its engine must function , the track must be clear , and passengers must be onboard ( Composition ). Train stations serve as designated stops for passengers to board and disembark ( Definition of Train Station ). A train 's journey between stations A and B is possible if a direct route ∃ ( Hypothetical Syllogism ). Moreover , train travel adheres to a fixed schedule. The train also follows a specific route between stations
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (train(x) AND passenger(y) AND station(z) IMPLIES transports_between(x, y, z))) AND ( FORALL x (train(x) AND departs_station(x) IMPLIES (functioning_engine(x) AND clear_track(x) AND passengers_board(x)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL p (train_station(x) AND train(y) AND passenger(p) IMPLIES (stop_to_pick_up(y, x, p) AND stop_to_disembark(y, x, p)))) AND ( EXISTS x FORALL y FORALL z (train(x) AND station(y) AND station(z) AND direct_route(y, z) IMPLIES travel_from_to(x, y, z))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (train(x) AND station(y) AND station(z) IMPLIES fix_schedule(x, y, z))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL r (train(x) AND route(r) AND on_route(x, r) IMPLIES follows_route(x,r)))

nl: An eating utensil is a handheld tool used for preparing , serving , or eating food. Forks , knives , and spoons are typical utensils for eating meals. A fork has prongs and is used for piercing and picking up food. A spoon has a bowl - like shape and is used for scooping , stirring , and consuming liquid - based foods. A knife is sharp and used for cutting. Utensils are made of metal or plastic. A tool is useful if it is either durable or inexpensive , but not if it is both. Forks and spoons are utensils , while a knife is a cutting tool
fol: (FORALL x ((utensil_eat(x) AND handheld_tool(x)) IMPLIES (use_prepare_food(x) OR use_serve_food(x) OR use_eat_food(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((fork(x) OR knife(x) OR spoon(x)) IMPLIES utensil_eat(x))) AND ( FORALL x (fork(x) IMPLIES (have_prong(x) AND use_pierce_pick_food(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (spoon(x) IMPLIES (have_bowl_shape(x) AND use_scoop_stir_consume_liquid_food(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (knife(x) IMPLIES (sharp(x) AND use_cut(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (utensil(x) IMPLIES (metal(x) OR plastic(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((fork(x) OR spoon(x)) IMPLIES utensil(x))) AND ( FORALL x (knife(x) IMPLIES tool_cut(x))) AND ( FORALL x (tool(x) IMPLIES ((durable(x) OR cheap(x)) AND NOT (durable(x) AND cheap(x)))))

nl: A company can be categorized as either a startup or an established business. Startups are newly established and not subsidiaries of larger corporations. A business is deemed successful when it generates profit , provides value to customers , and has a positive impact on society. Profit generation also requires either high revenue and low expenses , or a large market share. Furthermore , maintaining a satisfied customer base and generating profit both contribute to a company 's overall success. A profitable and growing company is distinct from one that is struggling and downsizing ; they are mutually exclusive. Ultimately , a company succeeds if it combines innovative products and an efficient supply chain , or employs effective marketing strategies. These factors contribute to both profitability and overall business success
fol: (FORALL x (company(x) IMPLIES (startup(x) OR establish(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (startup(x) IMPLIES (new_establish(x) AND NOT subsidiary_of_corporation(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (business(x) IMPLIES (generate_profit(x) AND provide_value_customer(x) AND positive_impact_society(x) IMPLIES succeed(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (generate_profit(x) IMPLIES (high_revenue(x) AND low_expend(x) OR large_market_share(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (company(x) IMPLIES (satisfy_customer(x) AND generate_profit(x) IMPLIES succeed(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((profitable(x) AND grow(x)) IMPLIES NOT (struggle(x) AND downsize(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((struggle(x) AND downsize(x)) IMPLIES NOT (profitable(x) AND grow(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (company(x) IMPLIES ((innovate_product(x) AND efficient_supply_chain(x)) OR effective_market_strategy(x) IMPLIES succeed(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (succeed(x) IMPLIES (profitable(x) AND succeed(x))))

nl: If there are technical issues , poor weather , or crew unavailability , then a flight is delayed. A flight is also delayed if it departs later than its scheduled time. Because a flight is delayed if it departs later than its scheduled time , and technical issues , poor weather , or crew unavailability can cause the flight to depart later than scheduled time , the flight is delayed due to these factors. If a flight is delayed , it is either because of bad weather or technical issues , but not both. A train is delayed when there are technical issues or severe weather conditions. An airplane with a delayed departure and no available alternative flights causes inconvenience for passengers. A flight is on time if it departs no later than a certain amount of time after the scheduled departure time and arrives no later than a specific time after the scheduled arrival time. A flight is delayed due to inclement weather , technical issues , or air traffic congestion. This is similar to the factors that cause a delayed flight
fol: (FORALL x (fly(x) AND (technical_issue(x) OR poor_weather(x) OR crew_unavailable(x)) IMPLIES delay(x))) AND ( FORALL x (fly(x) AND depart_late_schedule(x) IMPLIES delay(x))) AND ( FORALL x (train(x) AND (technical_issue(x) OR severe_weather(x)) IMPLIES delay(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (airplane(x) AND delay_depart(x) AND no_alternative_available(x) AND passenger(y) IMPLIES cause_inconvenience(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (fly(x) AND (depart_no_late_schedule_time(x) AND arrive_no_late_plan_arrive(x)) IMPLIES on_time(x))) AND ( FORALL x (fly(x) AND delay(x) IMPLIES ((bad_weather(x) OR technical_issue(x)) AND NOT (bad_weather(x) AND technical_issue(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (fly(x) AND delay(x) IFF (inclement_weather(x) OR technical_issue(x) OR air_traffic_congest(x))))

nl: Mountains are formed by tectonic plate movement , volcanic activity , or erosion. These elevated landforms create rugged terrain. Mountains support unique ecosystems. These ecosystems provide habitats for diverse flora and fauna. Mountains , deserts , and forests exemplify Earth 's diverse landscapes. If a mountain is covered in snow and ice , then it is not a volcano ( Modus Tollens implication ). A landscape featuring hills , valleys , and a river is picturesque. Furthermore , a landscape is stunning if it contains mountains , a river , or both a desert and a forest
fol: (FORALL x (mountain(x) IMPLIES (form_by_tectonic_plate(x) OR form_by_volcano(x) OR erode(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (mountain(x) IMPLIES rugged_terrain(x))) AND ( FORALL x (mountain(x) IMPLIES support(x, unique_ecosystem))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((unique_ecosystem(x) AND flora(y, x) AND fauna(z, x)) IMPLIES habitat(x, y, z))) AND ( FORALL x (landscape(x) IMPLIES (mountain(x) OR desert(x) OR forest(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((mountain(x) AND cover_snow(x) AND cover_ice(x)) IMPLIES NOT volcano(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((landscape(x) AND hill(x) AND valley(x) AND river(x)) IMPLIES picturesque(x))) AND ( FORALL x (landscape(x) IMPLIES ((mountain(x) OR river(x) OR (desert(x) AND forest(x))) IMPLIES stun(x))))

nl: Traffic congestion in a city occurs when the volume of vehicles exceeds road capacity , resulting in delays. Cities with high population density often experience traffic congestion due to insufficient infrastructure. A clean city is characterized by regularly swept streets and proper garbage disposal. Furthermore , low air pollution is essential for a clean city. A clean environment requires both good air quality and efficient waste management. Cities with large populations tend to have both heavy traffic and extensive public transportation. For a city to be considered truly clean , it must have efficient waste management , clean air , and well - maintained public spaces , free from excessive litter and pollution
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (city(x) AND timeperiod(y) AND vehicle_volume_exceed_capacity(x, y) IMPLIES traffic_congest(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) AND high_population_density(x) AND insufficient_infrastructure(x) IMPLIES traffic_congest(x))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) AND regular_sweep_street(x) AND proper_garbage_dispose(x) IMPLIES clean(x))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) AND low_air_pollution(x) IMPLIES clean(x))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) AND clean_environment(x) IMPLIES (good_air_quality(x) AND efficient_waste_manage(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) AND high_population_density(x) IMPLIES (well_developed_public_transport(x) AND traffic_congest(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) AND large_population(x) IMPLIES (heavy_traffic(x) AND extensive_public_transport(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) AND clean(x) IMPLIES (efficient_waste_manage(x) AND clean_air(x) AND well_maintain_public_space(x) AND NOT overwhelmedbylitter(x) AND NOT pollution(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (city(x) AND good_air_quality(x) AND efficient_waste_manage(x) IMPLIES clean_environment(x)))

nl: A planet orbits a star. The star can be part of a galaxy. The Moon orbits Earth. Earth orbits the Sun. The Sun is the center of the solar system. A celestial object is a satellite if it revolves around another object due to gravitational forces. Since the Moon orbits Earth , and this is due to gravitational forces , the Moon is a satellite of Earth. Earth orbits the Sun due to gravitational forces ; thus , Earth is a satellite of the Sun. Because a star is part of a galaxy , and planets orbit stars , planets can be considered within the context of galaxies. The solar system is centered on the Sun
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (planet(x) AND star(y) IMPLIES orbit(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (star(x) AND galaxy(y) IMPLIES galaxy_part(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (moon(x) AND planet(y) IMPLIES orbit(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (planet(x) AND sun(y) IMPLIES orbit(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (sun(x) IMPLIES system_center(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (object(x) AND object(y) AND rotate_around(x, y) AND gravity_force(x, y) IMPLIES satellite(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (moon(x) IMPLIES object(x))) AND ( FORALL x (planet(x) IMPLIES object(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (moon(x) AND planet(y) AND orbit(x, y) AND gravity_force(x, y) IMPLIES satellite(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (planet(x) AND sun(y) AND orbit(x, y) AND gravity_force(x, y) IMPLIES satellite(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (star(x) AND galaxy(y) AND planet(z) AND orbit(z, x) IMPLIES galaxy_context(z, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (system(x) AND sun(y) IMPLIES center_on(x, y)))

nl: A bird can migrate long distances if it has strong wings , high stamina , and a good sense of direction. A bird can also migrate long distances if it has strong wings , efficient metabolism , and good navigation skills. If a bird is not injured , the weather is good , or it is migrating , then it flies. However , a bird cannot migrate if it is injured. Birds that can fly long distances and have strong navigation skills are more likely to migrate. Many birds , such as sparrows and pigeons , are found in urban environments. Therefore , these birds have adapted to their environment. A bird species that adapts to various habitats , has a varied diet , and is resistant to diseases is more likely to have a large population
fol: (FORALL x (bird(x) IMPLIES (can_migrate_long_distance(x) IMPLIES ((strong_wing(x) AND high_stamina(x) AND good_sense_direction(x)) OR (strong_wing(x) AND efficient_metabolism(x) AND good_navigate_skill(x)))))) AND ( FORALL x (bird(x) IMPLIES (NOT injure(x) IMPLIES fly(x)))) AND ( FORALL x ((good_weather(x) OR migrate(x)) IMPLIES fly(x))) AND ( FORALL x (bird(x) IMPLIES (injure(x) IMPLIES NOT can_migrate(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (bird(x) AND can_fly_long_distance(x) AND strong_navigate_skill(x) IMPLIES more_likely_to_migrate(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((sparrow(x) OR pigeon(x)) IMPLIES (bird(x) AND find_in_urban_environment(x) AND adapt(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (bird_species(x) AND adapt_to_wide_range_habitat(x) AND vary_diet(x) AND resist_common_disease(x) IMPLIES more_likely_have_large_population(x)))

nl: Adventurous travelers frequently explore new cultures. This exploration often involves trips organized by travel agencies. These agencies offer diverse destinations , like tropical islands or historical cities. A trip is considered adventurous if it includes activities such as hiking or exploring new destinations. Furthermore , a trip is memorable if it includes adventure and cultural experiences. Traveling expands our understanding of different cultures and perspectives. A vacation spot is relaxing if it has beautiful scenery , quiet surroundings , and comfortable accommodations. A trip is memorable if it includes either an exciting adventure or a cultural experience and does n't result in unfortunate events. Therefore , a well - planned trip can be both adventurous , culturally enriching , and memorable
fol: (FORALL x (travel(x) AND frequent(x, explore_new_culture) IMPLIES travel_adventure(x))) AND ( FORALL x (explore(x) IMPLIES organize_by(x, travel_agency))) AND ( FORALL x (travel_agency(x) IMPLIES offer(x, tropic_island) OR offer(x, history_city))) AND ( FORALL x (trip(x) AND include(x, hike) OR include(x, explore_new_destination) IMPLIES adventure(x))) AND ( FORALL x (trip(x) AND include(x, adventure) AND include(x, culture_experience) IMPLIES memorable(x))) AND ( FORALL x (travel(x) IMPLIES expand(x, understand_differ_culture_perspective))) AND ( FORALL x (vacation_spot(x) AND have(x, beautiful_scenery) AND have(x, quiet_surround) AND have(x, comfortable_accommodation) IMPLIES relax(x))) AND ( FORALL x (trip(x) AND (include(x, excite_adventure) OR include(x, culture_experience)) AND NOT result(x, unfortunate_event) IMPLIES memorable(x))) AND ( FORALL x (trip(x) AND well_plan(x) IMPLIES adventure(x) AND culture_enrich(x) AND memorable(x)))

nl: Medicines , in general , can alleviate pain. However , a medication is only truly effective if it targets the correct ailment. Furthermore , effective medications have minimal side effects and are taken as prescribed. Medicines targeting specific pathogens are even more effective when combined with a healthy lifestyle. Moreover , some medications alleviate pain , reduce fever , or both. For example , medications that reduce inflammation can alleviate pain for arthritis patients. Finally , a medicine is considered effective against a disease if it has been clinically tested , has a high success rate , and causes minimal side effects , and is administered correctly to a patient who is n't resistant
fol: (FORALL x (medicine(x) IMPLIES alleviate_pain(x))) AND ( FORALL x (medication(x) IMPLIES (target_correct_ailment(x) IMPLIES effective(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (medication(x) IMPLIES (effective(x) IMPLIES (minimal_side_effect(x) AND take_prescribe(x))))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (medicine(x) AND target_specific_pathogen(x) AND healthy_lifestyle(y) IMPLIES more_effective(x))) AND ( EXISTS x (medication(x) AND (alleviate_pain(x) OR reduce_fever(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (medication(x) AND reduce_inflame(x) IMPLIES alleviate_pain_arthritis(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (medicine(x) AND clinical_test(x) AND high_succeed_rate(x) AND minimal_side_effect(x) AND patient(y) AND disease(z) AND administer(x, y) AND NOT resist(y, x) IMPLIES effective_against(x, z)))

nl: Trains are a mode of transportation that runs on tracks. Specifically , trains travel on railway tracks and rails. The engine powers the train , enabling it to carry passengers. Some trains operate on high - speed rail networks. These trains are often popular for long - distance travel due to their comfortable seating. In contrast , buses travel on roads , and boats travel on water. Cars , similarly to buses , use roads , while boats navigate water

nl: A tree grows strong and healthy if it receives sufficient sunlight , has access to water , and is rooted in nutrient - rich soil. A tree needs sunlight , water , and nutrients to grow. A healthy tree requires both nutrients and sunlight. A tree grows when it receives sunlight and water. Trees thriving depend on fertile soil , adequate sunlight , and sufficient rainfall. Adequate water and sunlight result in a strong and healthy tree. Sunlight and access to nutrients also support a tree 's growth. A tree will grow in an area with fertile soil , adequate sunlight , and sufficient water. A tree grows taller in nutrient - rich soil , with access to sunlight , and adequate water. Finally , a tree grows tall and strong when it receives sunlight , absorbs nutrients from the soil , and receives sufficient water
fol: (FORALL x (tree(x) AND suffice_sunlight(x) AND access_water(x) AND nutrient_rich_soil(x) IMPLIES strong_healthy(x))) AND ( FORALL x (tree(x) IMPLIES require_sunlight(x) AND require_water(x) AND require_nutrient(x))) AND ( FORALL x (tree(x) AND need_nutrient(x) AND need_sunlight(x) IMPLIES healthy(x))) AND ( FORALL x (tree(x) AND receive_sunlight(x) AND receive_water(x) IMPLIES grow(x))) AND ( FORALL x (tree(x) AND fertile_soil(x) AND adequate_sunlight(x) AND suffice_rain(x) IMPLIES thrive(x))) AND ( FORALL x (tree(x) AND receive_adequate_water(x) AND receive_sunlight(x) IMPLIES strong_healthy(x))) AND ( FORALL x (tree(x) AND get_sunlight(x) AND access_nutrient(x) IMPLIES grow(x))) AND ( FORALL x (tree(x) AND fertile_soil(x) AND adequate_sunlight(x) AND adequate_water(x) IMPLIES grow(x))) AND ( FORALL x (tree(x) AND nutrient_rich_soil(x) AND access_sunlight(x) AND adequate_water(x) IMPLIES grow_tall(x))) AND ( FORALL x (tree(x) AND receive_sunlight(x) AND absorb_nutrient_soil(x) AND receive_suffice_water(x) IMPLIES grow_tall_strong(x)))

nl: A machine is considered a robot if it senses its environment , processes information , and acts autonomously or semiautonomously. Robots can be autonomous , semiautonomous , or remotely controlled , performing tasks with varying human intervention. Successful task completion requires correct instructions , the necessary tools , and a controlled environment. Domestic robots , for example , might clean or cook , but not simultaneously , and cannot perform surgery. Finally , a robot performs a task within a specific environment under program control , provided the robot 's design , the environment 's resources , and the program 's instructions align
fol: (FORALL x ((machine(x) AND sense_environment(x) AND process_information(x) AND (perform_action_autonomous(x) OR perform_action_semiautonomous(x))) IMPLIES robot(x))) AND ( FORALL x (robot(x) IMPLIES (autonomous(x) OR semiautonomous(x) OR remote_control(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (robot(x) IMPLIES perform_task_human_intervene(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (((robot(x) AND instruction(y) AND tool(z) AND correct(x, y) AND need(x, z) AND control_environment(z)) IMPLIES complete_task(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (robot(x) AND domestic(x) IMPLIES ((perform_clean(x) OR perform_cook(x)) AND NOT perform_surgery(x)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z ((robot(x) AND task(y) AND environment(z) AND design_to_perform(x, y) AND need(z) AND control(x, z)) IMPLIES perform_task(x, y, z)))

nl: A student attends school and learns math , science , or literature. Excelling in both math and science indicates a strong STEM aptitude. Students who perform well in math and science are often considered academically gifted in STEM fields. Therefore , such students are strong candidates for STEM scholarships. If a student studies math and speaks English and plays soccer , it is a different case than if a student studies history , speaks French and plays tennis. Furthermore , high performance in math , science , and language arts points to broad academic abilities. A math teacher does not teach literature or biology. Conversely , a literature teacher does not instruct in biology
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y (student(x) AND school(y) IMPLIES (attend(x, y) AND (learn_math(x) OR learn_science(x) OR learn_literature(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (student(x) AND exceed_in_math(x) AND exceed_in_science(x) IMPLIES aptitude(x))) AND ( FORALL x (student(x) AND perform_well_in_math(x) AND perform_well_in_science(x) IMPLIES gift(x))) AND ( FORALL x (student(x) AND perform_well_in_math(x) AND perform_well_in_science(x) IMPLIES strong_candidate_for_scholarship(x))) AND ( FORALL x ((student(x) AND study_math(x) AND speak_english(x) AND play_soccer(x)) IMPLIES NOT (study_history(x) AND speak_french(x) AND play_tennis(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (student(x) AND perform_well_in_math(x) AND perform_well_in_science(x) AND perform_well_in_language(x) IMPLIES broad(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (math_teacher(x) AND teach(x, y) IMPLIES (NOT teach_literature(x) AND NOT teach_biology(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (literature_teacher(x) IMPLIES NOT teach_biology(x)))

nl: A personal finance app is a software application for mobile devices. It assists users in managing finances by tracking expenses and creating budgets. Another type of app is a smartphone app designed to help users track fitness goals. These apps also often monitor diet and connect users with supportive communities. Not all apps are for productivity. Some apps provide entertainment or social connection. A useful smartphone app helps users accomplish tasks. A good app is also easy to use and has a clean interface. Programmers develop smartphone apps that can belong to categories like productivity or gaming. Users then download these apps
fol: (FORALL x (software_application(x) AND mobile_device(x) AND assist_users(x) AND track_expend(x) IMPLIES personal_finance_app(x))) AND ( FORALL x (smartphone_app(x) AND track_fitness_goal(x) AND monitor_diet(x) AND connect_person(x) IMPLIES help_users(x))) AND ( EXISTS x (app(x) AND NOT productive_app(x))) AND ( EXISTS x (app(x) AND (entertain_app(x) OR connect_person(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (smartphone_app(x) AND help_task(x) AND easy_use(x) AND clean_interface(x) IMPLIES useful(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (smartphone_app(x) AND program(y) IMPLIES develop_by(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL z (smartphone_app(x) AND (productive(z) OR game(z)) IMPLIES belong_category(x, z))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL w (smartphone_app(x) AND user(w) IMPLIES download_by(x, w)))

nl: A bridge 's primary function is to connect two landmasses. This connection facilitates passage across a body of water or a valley. To achieve this , a bridge must span the gap between the landmasses. Furthermore , bridges typically provide a transportation route , such as a road , for vehicles. Therefore , a structure that spans a body of water , connects two landmasses , and supports vehicle traffic is definitively a bridge. Thus , by definition , a bridge serves as a crucial link between separated land areas
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (bridge(x) AND landmass(y) AND landmass(z) AND (EXISTS w (body_of_water(w) OR valley(w)) AND pass(x, w)) IMPLIES connect(x, y, z))) AND ( FORALL x (bridge(x) IMPLIES (connect_landmass(x) AND pass(x)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z FORALL w (bridge(x) AND landmass(y) AND landmass(z) AND body_of_water(w) IMPLIES connect_across_water(x, y, z, w))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (bridge(x) AND landmass(y) AND landmass(z) AND span(x, y, z) IMPLIES connect(x, y, z))) AND ( FORALL x (structure(x) AND (EXISTS y (span(x, y) AND body_of_water(y))) AND connect_landmass(x) AND support_vehicle_traffic(x) IMPLIES bridge(x))) AND ( FORALL x (bridge(x) IMPLIES span_body_of_water(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (bridge(x) AND vehicle(y) IMPLIES provide_transport_route(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (connect_landmass(x) IMPLIES crucial_link(x))) AND ( FORALL x (bridge(x) IMPLIES connect_land(x)))

nl: Musical instruments are categorized by their method of sound production. String instruments , for example , are those characterized by their use of strings. A guitar , having six strings , is a clear example of a string instrument. Similarly , a violin is a string instrument , specifically identified by its four strings. A musical instrument with strings , if not a guitar , is therefore classified as either a violin or a cello. In an orchestra , string instruments are typically played using a bow. In contrast , percussion instruments produce sound when struck or shaken. Another distinct category comprises brass instruments. These generate sound through the vibration of the player 's lips and are played by pressing valves or slides. Such instruments include the trumpet or the trombone. Furthermore , a flute , being a woodwind instrument , notably has no strings
fol: (FORALL x (music_instrument(x) IMPLIES (string_instrument(x) OR percussion_instrument(x) OR brass_instrument(x) OR woodwind_instrument(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (string_instrument(x) IMPLIES use_string(x))) AND ( guitar(g) AND string_instrument(g) AND have_string(g)) AND ( violin(v) AND string_instrument(v) AND have_string(v)) AND ( FORALL x (music_instrument(x) AND use_string(x) AND NOT guitar(x) IMPLIES (violin(x) OR cello(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (string_instrument(x) AND orchestra(x) IMPLIES play_bow(x))) AND ( FORALL x (percussion_instrument(x) IMPLIES (strike(x) OR shake(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (brass_instrument(x) IMPLIES (produce_sound_lip_vibration(x) AND (play_press_valve(x) OR play_press_slide(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (trumpet(x) IMPLIES brass_instrument(x))) AND ( FORALL x (trombone(x) IMPLIES brass_instrument(x))) AND ( flute(f) AND woodwind_instrument(f) AND NOT use_string(f))

nl: A museum displays artwork if the artist is famous and the artwork is undamaged. A painting is displayed in a museum if it 's part of the museum 's collection. Museums display paintings , sculptures , and photographs. This collection attracts art enthusiasts worldwide. Art exhibits often feature paintings , sculptures , and photographs , but they do n't exclusively focus on a single artist or time period
fol: (FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (museum(x) AND artwork(y) AND artist(z) AND artist_art_have(y, z) AND artist_fame(z) AND NOT damage_art(y) IMPLIES display_art(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (paint(x) AND museum(y) AND art_collection(x, y) IMPLIES display_in(y, x))) AND ( FORALL x (art_exhibit(x) IMPLIES (feature_paint(x) AND feature_sculpture(x) AND feature_photograph(x) AND NOT focus_single_artist_time(x)))) AND ( EXISTS x (museum(x) AND display_paint(x) AND display_sculpture(x) AND display_photograph(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (museum(x) AND art_enthusiastic(y) AND attract(x, y) IMPLIES worldwide(y))) AND ( FORALL x (paint(x) IMPLIES artwork(x))) AND ( FORALL x (sculpture(x) IMPLIES artwork(x))) AND ( FORALL x (photograph(x) IMPLIES artwork(x)))

nl: Herbivores eat plants. This provides them with sustenance. Carnivores , on the other hand , eat other animals. Meat is their primary source of nutrients. In forest ecosystems , herbivores consume plants. Carnivores prey on these herbivores , or even other carnivores , for nourishment. Thus , dietary roles within an ecosystem are clearly defined. A carnivorous plant consumes insects. Similarly , a herbivorous animal consumes plants. An omnivorous animal consumes both , illustrating a broader dietary range. Predatory fish hunt smaller fish. Herbivorous fish consume aquatic plants. Therefore , the pattern of carnivores eating animals and herbivores eating plants holds true across different species
fol: (FORALL x (herbivore(x) IMPLIES eat_plant(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (herbivore(x) AND plant(y) IMPLIES consume(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (carnivore(x) IMPLIES eat_other_animal(x))) AND ( FORALL x (carnivore(x) IMPLIES primary_consume_meat(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (forest_ecosystem(x) AND herbivore(y) IMPLIES consume_plant(y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (forest_ecosystem(x) AND carnivore(y) AND herbivore(x) IMPLIES prey(y, x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (forest_ecosystem(x) AND carnivore(y) AND carnivore(x) IMPLIES prey(y, x))) AND ( FORALL x (carnivorous_plant(x) IMPLIES consume_insect(x))) AND ( FORALL x (herbivorous_animal(x) IMPLIES consume_plant(x))) AND ( FORALL x (omnivorous_animal(x) IMPLIES (consume_plant(x) AND consume_animal(x)))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (predator_fish(x) AND small_fish(y) IMPLIES hunt(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (herbivorous_fish(x) AND plant(y) IMPLIES consume(x, y))) AND ( FORALL x (carnivore(x) IMPLIES eat_animal(x))) AND ( FORALL x (herbivore(x) IMPLIES eat_plant(x))) AND ( FORALL x (animal(x) IMPLIES (herbivore(x) OR carnivore(x) OR omnivorous(x))))

nl: Seasons include spring , summer , fall , and winter. Each season is characterized by unique weather conditions and varying temperatures. Winter is typically cold. It often brings snow. During this season , days are shorter , and nights are longer. In contrast , summer is hot. It generally lacks snow. Summer also features longer days , and its nights are shorter. The timing of winter varies by hemisphere. Northern Hemisphere countries experience winter during December , January , and February. Conversely , Southern Hemisphere countries have their winter in June , July , and August. An equinox marks a time when day and night are of equal length
fol: (FORALL x (season(x) IMPLIES (spring(x) OR summer(x) OR fall(x) OR winter(x)))) AND ( season(spring) OR season(summer) OR season(fall) OR season(winter)) AND ( FORALL x (season(x) IMPLIES (characterize_weather(x) AND characterize_temperature(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (winter(x) IMPLIES cold(x))) AND ( FORALL x (winter(x) IMPLIES snow(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (winter(x) IMPLIES (short_day(y) AND long_night(y)))) AND ( FORALL x (summer(x) IMPLIES hot(x))) AND ( FORALL x (summer(x) IMPLIES NOT snow(x))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y (summer(x) IMPLIES (long_day(y) AND short_night(y)))) AND ( FORALL x (country(x) AND northern_hemisphere(x) IMPLIES winter(x))) AND ( FORALL y (country(y) AND southern_hemisphere(y) IMPLIES winter(y))) AND ( FORALL x FORALL y FORALL z (equinox(x) AND day(y) AND night(z) IMPLIES equal(y, z, x)))

nl: A rigorous scientific study follows strict protocols and produces reproducible results. It also contributes to the existing body of knowledge and undergoes peer review. Similarly , a study is robust if it has a large sample size , controls for confounding variables , and employs a rigorous methodology. These foundational elements , including robust methodology , accurate data analysis , and a large sample size , enable a study to produce reliable results. Adhering to the scientific method is also crucial for a study to be deemed reliable. Conversely , a study is not deemed reliable if researchers have conflicts of interest or if data is cherry - picked. For a study to be considered valid , it must be peer - reviewed and have a large sample size. However , the presence of a high risk of bias will invalidate its conclusions. Credibility is established when a study uses a large sample size and rigorous methodology. This credibility is further strengthened by either peer review or support from independent research. Ultimately , scientific studies that are rigorous , robust , and reliable significantly contribute to the advancement of knowledge. This includes the important work of a scientist who successfully disproves an existing theory
fol: (FORALL x (science_study(x) IMPLIES (follow_strict_protocol(x) AND produce_reproduce_result(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (science_study(x) IMPLIES (contribute_knowledge(x) AND undergo_peer_review(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (study(x) IMPLIES (large_sample_size(x) IMPLIES (control_variable(x) AND employ_rigorous_methodology(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (study(x) IMPLIES ((robust_methodology(x) AND accurate_data_analyze(x) AND large_sample_size(x)) IMPLIES produce_reliable_result(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (study(x) IMPLIES (follow_science_method(x) IMPLIES deem_reliable(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (study(x) IMPLIES ((conflict_interest(x) OR cherry_pick_data(x)) IMPLIES NOT deem_reliable(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (study(x) IMPLIES ((peer_review(x) AND large_sample_size(x)) IMPLIES valid(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (study(x) IMPLIES (high_risk_bias(x) IMPLIES NOT validate_conclude(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (study(x) IMPLIES ((large_sample_size(x) AND rigorous_methodology(x)) IMPLIES credibility(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (credibility(x) IMPLIES (peer_review(x) OR support_independent_research(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (science_study(x) IMPLIES ((rigorous(x) AND robust(x) AND reliable(x)) IMPLIES contribute_advance_knowledge(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (scientist(x) IMPLIES (disprove_theory(x) IMPLIES contribute_advance_knowledge(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (study(x) IMPLIES (large_sample_size(x) AND control_variable(x) AND rigorous_methodology(x) IMPLIES robust(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (science_study(x) IMPLIES (follow_strict_protocol(x) AND undergo_peer_review(x) IMPLIES rigorous(x))))

nl: A park is a public outdoor area used for recreation. Well - designed parks often include green spaces , walking paths , and playgrounds. They provide areas for leisure , recreation , and socialization. A park is suitable for picnics if it has tables , green spaces , and cleanliness. Alternatively , a park is suitable if it has grassy areas and available benches or picnic tables. Parks can be closed for maintenance , or they may be open with restricted areas. While parks allow picnics or barbecues , they prohibit them during fire bans or in areas designated only for sports. A park is child - friendly when it has playground equipment , designated safety zones , and accessible restroom facilities. Finally , a park is popular for picnics if it provides clean areas , available seating , and plenty of shade
fol: (FORALL x (park(x) IFF (public_outdoor_area(x) AND use_recreation(x)))) AND ( FORALL p (well_design(p) AND park(p) IMPLIES (green_space(p) AND walk_path(p) AND playground(p)))) AND ( FORALL x (park(x) IMPLIES ((green_space(x) OR playground(x)) AND (leisure_use(x) OR recreation_use(x) OR socialize_use(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (park(x) AND ((grass_area(x) AND available_bench(x)) OR picnic_table(x)) IMPLIES suitable_picnic(x))) AND ( FORALL x (park(x) AND (table(x) AND green_space(x) AND clean(x)) IMPLIES suitable_picnic(x))) AND ( FORALL x (park(x) IMPLIES (close_maintain(x) OR (open(x) AND restrict_area(x))))) AND ( FORALL x (park(x) AND NOT (fire_ban(x) OR sport_only(x)) IMPLIES (allow_picnic(x) OR allow_barbecue(x)))) AND ( FORALL x (park(x) AND playground_equipment(x) AND safe_zone(x) AND access_restroom(x) IMPLIES child_friendly(x))) AND ( FORALL x (park(x) AND clean_area(x) AND available_seat(x) AND plenty_shade(x) IMPLIES popular_picnic(x)))
